blocks:
Ayah

ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَجَعَلَ ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتِ وَٱلنُّورَ ۖ ثُمَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِرَبِّهِمْ يَعْدِلُونَ (۱)
All praise belongs to Allah who created the heavens and the earth and made the darkness and the light. Yet the faithless equate [others] with their Lord. (1)

Key Points

Throughout the entire Quran, the word nūr (light) is singular, while the word ẓulumāt (darknesses) is plural. Fundamentally, the Truth is one, but the paths of falsehood are many. Indeed, light is a sign of unity, and darknesses are a sign of dispersion. Ya‘dilūn (یعدلون), derived from ‘adl (عدل), means equal or counterpart.

The first Ayah of this Surah refers to the "creation of the cosmic order," the second Ayah refers to the "creation of man," and the third Ayah refers to the supervision over "human deeds and behavior."

According to the statement of Hadrat Ali عليه السلام: This Ayah is a response to three groups of deviants:

A: The "Materialists" (Māddiyyūn) who deny Divine creation and the origination of existence. «خلق السماوات ...»

B: The "Dualists" who believe in two origins for light and darkness. «جعل الظّلمات والنّور» (230)

C: The "Polytheists" (Mushrikīn) who assign partners and likenesses to Allah. «ثمّ الّذین كفروا بربّهم یعدلون» (231)

Imam Musa ibn Ja‘far علیهما السلام said regarding «...بربّهم یعدلون»: It means that the disbelievers consider darkness and light, and injustice and justice, to be equal. (232)

230) The current Avesta considers Ahriman the god of darkness and Ahura Mazda the god of light, positing a contradiction between the two. Tafsir Al-Kashif.

231) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

232) Tafsir Ayyashi; Bihar, Vol. 9, p. 355.

Messages

1- Allah has brought things into existence from non-existence, and He also places new qualities and innovations within existing beings. (Initial creation: khalaqa (خَلَق); subsequent creation/making: ja‘ala (جَعَل))

2- Assigning partners to Allah is a form of denying Him and disbelief in Him. «الّذین كفروا بربّهم یعدلون»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-An‘ām (The Cattle)

This Surah has one hundred and sixty-five Ayahs, and all of its verses were revealed at once in Mecca with special solemnity. Gabriel descended with this Surah upon the Prophet of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, escorted by seventy thousand angels. The main message of the Ayahs of this Surah is the struggle against polytheism (shirk) and the invitation to Monotheism (Tawḥīd). Since the polytheists of the Arabian Peninsula, based on their beliefs, considered some cattle lawful (ḥalāl) and others unlawful (ḥarām), the Quran, in the context of combating such superstitions and false beliefs, states rulings regarding cattle starting from Ayah 136 of this Surah. For this reason, the Surah is named "Al-An‘ām" (The Cattle). Narrations have been reported concerning the virtue of this Surah, as well as the fulfillment of needs through its recitation. For instance, it is narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام that whoever performs four units of prayer (rak‘āt) (with two salutations), and then recites this Surah followed by a specific supplication (du‘ā), his needs will be fulfilled. (Tafsir Atyab al-Bayān.) The word Qul (Say) has not appeared in any other Surah as frequently as it has in this one. The repetition of this address to the Prophet 44 times is perhaps because this Surah discusses the false beliefs, deviations, and undue expectations of the polytheists, necessitating decisiveness in the matter. This point indicates that the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is commanded to convey the text of the revelation completely and without omission.

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكُم مِّن طِينٍ ثُمَّ قَضَىٰٓ أَجَلًا ۖ وَأَجَلٌ مُّسَمًّى عِندَهُۥ ۖ ثُمَّ أَنتُمْ تَمْتَرُونَ (۲)
It is He who created you from clay, then ordained the term [of your life]—the specified term is with Him—and yet you are in doubt. (2)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, cosmic issues and the creation of the heavens and the earth were discussed; here, the creation of man and his limited life in this world are mentioned. In the Quran, ajal musamma (a fixed term) is mentioned 21 times. Allah has set two types of timeframes for human life: one is definite (fixed), where even if all precautions are taken, the lifespan (like the oil in a lamp) will end. The other is indefinite (non-fixed), which relates to our own actions, like a lamp that has oil but is exposed to a storm. In narrations, actions such as maintaining kinship ties (silat al-rahm), charity (sadaqah), Zakat, and supplication (du'a) are considered causes of a longer life, while actions such as severing kinship ties and oppression are considered causes of a shorter life. [233] Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding "أجلاً و أجلٌ" (a term and a term): They are two terms: one is definite and certain (mahtum), and the other is conditional (mawquf), meaning conditional and suspended. [234] According to Ibn Abbas, Allah has set two terms for man: one from birth until death, and the other from death until the Day of Resurrection. Man, through his actions, sometimes reduces one and adds to the other. Thus, the end of no one's term is changeable. «ومایُعمَّر مِن مُعَمَّرٍ ولایَنقَصُ مِن عُمُرِه الاّ فى كتاب» [235]

233) مستدرك الوسائل، ج 15، ص 249. 234) كافى، ج 1، ص 147. 235) فاطر، 11.

Messages

  1. The duration of life and its end are not in our hands. «هو الذى.... قضى اجلاً»
  2. Only Allah is aware of the ajal musamma (the definite term). «أجل مسمّى عنده»
  3. Although the creation and the end of man's affair are all from Allah and by His hand, why then should we doubt the Origin and the Return? «ثم انتم تمترون»

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱللَّهُ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَفِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ يَعْلَمُ سِرَّكُمْ وَجَهْرَكُمْ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا تَكْسِبُونَ (۳)
He is Allah in the heavens and on the earth: He knows your secret and your overt [matters], and He knows what you earn. (3)

Key Points

In response to the deviant belief in polytheism (the god of rain, the god of war, the god of peace, the god of earth, etc.), this Ayah states: The God of everything and everywhere is one. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: He encompasses all creation in terms of knowledge, power, dominion, and sovereignty. [236]

236) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Tawhid al-Saduq, p. 133.

Messages

1- The domain of Allah's sovereignty is the entire existence. «فى السموات و فى الارض» 2- Because Allah is the Sovereign, He is aware. «هو اللَّه فى السموات و فى الارض یعلم...» 3- Man's manifest and hidden deeds are the same to Allah. [237] «یعلم سرّكم و جهركم» 4- Belief in Allah's comprehensive knowledge is both a motivation for good deeds and a deterrent from bad deeds. «یعلم سرّكم و جهركم» 5- Allah is aware of the future consequences of man's actions. «و یعلم ما تكسبون»

237) If Allah's knowledge regarding man's manifest and hidden deeds differed, the phrase «یعلم» would have been repeated, and He would have said: «یعلم سرّكم و یعلم جهركم»

Ayahs

وَمَا تَأْتِيهِم مِّنْ ءَايَةٍ مِّنْ ءَايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِمْ إِلَّا كَانُوا۟ عَنْهَا مُعْرِضِينَ (٤)
There did not come to them any sign from among the signs of their Lord, but that they used to disregard it. (4)
فَقَدْ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِٱلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَآءَهُمْ ۖ فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِيهِمْ أَنۢبَـٰٓؤُا۟ مَا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (٥)
They have certainly denied the truth when it came to them, but soon there will come to them the news of what they have been deriding. (5)

Key Points

| Perhaps the intent of the "great news" (خبرهاى بزرگ) in the Ayah is the news of the conquest of Mecca (فتح مكّه) or the defeat of the polytheists in the Battle of Badr, and similar events. [238]

238) Tafsir Maraghi.

Messages

| 1- For the obstinate, the type of evidence or Ayah makes no difference; they reject them all. (آیة من آیات ربّهم) 2- The obstinate and the disbelievers are not willing to listen, reflect, or investigate; as soon as the truth arrives, they deny it. (فقد كَذّبوا بالحقّ لمّا جاءهم) 3- Believers must be reassured that their path is the truth (بالحق), and the disbelievers must be warned that bitter news will come to them. (فسوف یأتیهم) 4- Mockery is the constant method of the disbelievers. (كانوا به یستهزءون) 5- The downfall of man has three stages: turning away (اعراض), denial (تكذیب), and mockery (استهزا). All three stages are referenced in these two Ayahs. (معرضین - كذّبوا یستهزءون)

Ayah

أَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ كَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّن قَرْنٍ مَّكَّنَّـٰهُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مَا لَمْ نُمَكِّن لَّكُمْ وَأَرْسَلْنَا ٱلسَّمَآءَ عَلَيْهِم مِّدْرَارًا وَجَعَلْنَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرَ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهِمْ فَأَهْلَكْنَـٰهُم بِذُنُوبِهِمْ وَأَنشَأْنَا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ قَرْنًا ءَاخَرِينَ (٦)
Have they not regarded how many a generation We have destroyed before them whom We had granted power in the land in respects that We did not grant you, and We sent abundant rains for them from the sky and made streams run for them? Then We destroyed them for their sins, and brought forth another generation after them. (6)

Key Points

| «قَرْن» (Qarn) refers to a nation that was destroyed entirely [239]. It is also used for people who live at the same time (a generation). Since a generation usually lasts from 60 to 100 years, 60, 80, or 100 years is therefore called a Qarn (a century/generation) [240]. The word «مدرار» (Midrār) means the abundant and continuous pouring down of rain, in the necessary amount [241].

239) Aqrab al-Mawārid. 240) Tafāsīr al-Mīzān wa Kabīr Fakhr Rāzī. 241) Tafsīr Majma‘ al-Bayān under Ayah 52 of Sūrat Hūd.

Messages

| 1- Individuals who do not take heed from history are rebuked. «ألم یروا» 2- The disbelievers of early Islam were aware of the history of previous nations and could have learned lessons. «ألم یروا» 3- Attention to the disappearance of blessings and the downfall of sinners is a factor in removing heedlessness. «ألم یروا... فاهلكناهم بذنوبهم» 4- Civilizations existed before Islam and were subsequently extinguished. «اهلكنا من قبلهم» 5- It is one of the educational methods of the Quran to narrate real and instructive stories for people to take warning. «اهلكنا من قبلهم» 6- The punishment for those who misuse God-given resources is destruction. «كم أهلكنا» 7- Instead of saying «أرسلنا من السماء» (We sent down from the sky), He said: «أرسلنا السماء» (We sent the sky for you), to express the ultimate extent of Divine favor. 8- The actions of the people are the cause of historical transformations and events. «فاهلكناهم بذنوبهم» 9- The destruction of people due to their sins is one of the Divine traditions (Sunan). «فاهلكناهم بذنوبهم» 10- Allah punishes not only in the Hereafter but also in this world. «فأهلكناهم» 11- If resources are in the hands of the righteous, they establish prayer. «ان مكّناهم فى الارض اقاموا الصلاة» [242] But if they are in the hands of the unworthy and faithless, they commit corruption and sin. «مكّناهم... فاهلكناهم بذنوبهم» 12- Material resources do not prevent Divine wrath and punishment. «مكّناهم... فاهلكناهم» contrary to what the disbelievers imagined: «نحن اكثر أموالاً وأولاداً ومانحن بمعذّبین» [243] 13- Material resources are not the key to success. «مكّناهم... فاهلكناهم» as we read elsewhere: «انّ الانسان لیطغى أن رآه استغنى» [244] 14- We have two types of death: natural, which occurs upon the arrival of the appointed term (ajal), and unnatural, which occurs due to Divine punishment and unexpected events. «بذنوبهم» 15- Powerful sinners should not think that the world will always be to their liking; Allah replaces them with others. «أهلكناهم ... و أنشأنا من بعدهم قرناً آخرین»

242) Hajj, 41. 243) Saba’, 35. 244) Alaq, 6 to 7.

Ayah

وَلَوْ نَزَّلْنَا عَلَيْكَ كِتَـٰبًا فِى قِرْطَاسٍ فَلَمَسُوهُ بِأَيْدِيهِمْ لَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ (۷)
Had We sent down to you a Book on paper so they could touch it with their [own] hands, [still] the faithless would have said, ‘This is nothing but plain magic.’ (7)

Key Points

A group of polytheists (mushrikūn) used to say: We will only believe if You send down to us a writing on paper, accompanied by an angel. But they were lying and were seeking excuses. Qirṭās (قِرطاس) is anything upon which one writes, whether paper, wood, skin, or stone, but today it is commonly referred to as paper.

Messages

1- When obstinacy is involved, no proof is effective; they even deny tangible realities. «فلمسوه بایدیهم... ان هذا الاّ سحر مبین»

2- The accusation of magic (sihr) was one of the most common accusations the polytheists leveled against the Prophet. «ان هذا الاّ سحر مبین»

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ لَوْلَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَيْهِ مَلَكٌ ۖ وَلَوْ أَنزَلْنَا مَلَكًا لَّقُضِىَ ٱلْأَمْرُ ثُمَّ لَا يُنظَرُونَ (۸)
They say, ‘Why has not an angel been sent down to him?’ Were We to send down an angel, the matter would surely be decided, and then they would not be granted any respite. (8)

Key Points

The descent of the angel requested by the disbelievers—if it were in human form, it would be just like the Prophet Himself; and if it were to manifest in its true form, they would not have the strength to see it and would perish upon witnessing it. [245] The satanic spirit of arrogance does not allow a person to follow a human being like himself. Sometimes he says: Why do the prophets eat food like us, walk in the markets, and wear clothes like us? «و قالوا ما لهذا الرّسول یأكل الطعام و یمشى فى الاسواق» [246] Sometimes they would say to one another: If you obey a human being like yourselves, you will surely be losers. «ولئن أطعتم بشراً مثلكم انّكم اذاً لخاسرون» [247]

245) Tafasir Kabir Fakh al-Razi and Nur al-Thaqalayn. 246) Al-Furqan, 7. 247) Al-Mu'minun, 34.

Messages

1- The fault-finding disbelievers did not consider a human being worthy of the station of prophethood and requested to see an angel. «لولا انزل علیه ملك»

2- The Divine tradition is such that if a miracle is performed at the request of the people and they deny it, certain destruction will befall them. [248] «لقضى الامر»

(The method of Divine invitations is based on freedom, contemplation, choice, and having respite. Requesting another way, such as the descent of an angel or heavenly food, removes the opportunity and respite. In that case, the only path is acceptance of the invitation, otherwise, it is destruction.)

248) Tafsir al-Maraghi.

Ayah

وَلَوْ جَعَلْنَـٰهُ مَلَكًا لَّجَعَلْنَـٰهُ رَجُلًا وَلَلَبَسْنَا عَلَيْهِم مَّا يَلْبِسُونَ (۹)
Had We made him an angel, We would have surely made him a man, and We would have surely confounded them just as they confound [the truth now]. (9)

Key Points

| The word labs (لَبس) (pronounced like dars) means concealment and causing confusion, but the word lubs (لُبس) (pronounced like qufl) means wearing clothes. (249) If the Prophet, who is the role model for humanity, were an angel, how could He be the leader of humans who are afflicted by the storms of instincts, and possess stomachs and desires? The meaning of the Ayah might be this: If the Prophet were made an angel, He would have to appear in the form of a man so that people could see Him, and this would cause confusion among the people as to whether He was a human or an angel. «للبسنا علیهم»

249) Tafsir Namūneh.

Messages

| 1- Divine traditions (Sunan) are based on wisdom and do not change according to the desires of this person or that. «لو جعلناه ملكا» (If We had made him an angel). (The particle law (لَو) indicates that We carry out Our own plan and pay no heed to baseless demands.) 2- For education and propagation (Da'wah), human role models must be presented, who are pioneers in both invitation and action. «لجعلناه رجلاً» (We would certainly have made him a man). 3- Prophets are chosen from among the people. «لجعلناه رجلاً» (We would certainly have made him a man).

Ayah

وَلَقَدِ ٱسْتُهْزِئَ بِرُسُلٍ مِّن قَبْلِكَ فَحَاقَ بِٱلَّذِينَ سَخِرُوا۟ مِنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (۱٠)
Apostles were certainly derided before you. Then those who ridiculed them were besieged by what they used to deride. (10)

Key Points

This Ayah is a consolation for the Prophet of Islam, stating that firstly: previous prophets were also subjected to ridicule. Secondly: not only the punishment of the Hereafter, but also worldly wrath will afflict those who mock.

Messages

1- Remembering the difficulties of others increases one's patience, and the preacher of religion should not be distressed by the mockery of opponents. «لقد استهزى ء برُسلٍ من قبلك»

2- Mockery (Estehza) is one of the enemy's psychological wars intended to weaken the morale of leaders, and it must be resisted. «استهزى ء برسل من قبلك»

3- Those who mock will ultimately be disgraced, and the ridicule will afflict them themselves. «فحاق بالّذین سخروا»

4- Mocking the religion is one of the major sins for which the promise of punishment has been given. «فحاق بالّذین سخروا...»

5- Allah is the Protector of the Prophets and destroys those who mock. «فحاق بالّذین سخروا...»

6- Mockery is the constant method of the disbelievers (Kuffār). «كانوا به یستهزءون»

Ayah

قُلْ سِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ثُمَّ ٱنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (۱۱)
Say, ‘Travel over the land, and then observe how was the fate of the deniers.’ (11)

Key Points

| The command "سیروا فى الارض" (Travel through the earth) has appeared six times in the Quran. Unfortunately, the disbelievers acted upon this instruction more than we did, and they explored every inch of the Islamic countries, becoming aware of the resources, reserves, strengths and weaknesses, cultural artifacts, manuscripts, and arts of the Muslims, and they plundered them, while the Muslims remained in the sleep of negligence.

Messages

| 1- Educational, instructive, and cautionary journeys are praiseworthy and good. «سیروا... ثمّ انظروا» | 2- The defeat and downfall of the opponents of truth is inevitable. If you doubt this, read their history, see their ruins through travel, and take a lesson. «سیروا... ثم انظروا» | 3- The factors leading to the glory or downfall of societies are governed by law (are systematic). If factors such as denying and rejecting the truth caused destruction at one time, they will cause it at another time as well. «سیروا... انظروا» | 4- One of the factors in the collapse of civilizations is the rejection of the prophets. «عاقبة المكذّبین» | 5- Transient appearances are not important; the end result is important. «عاقبة المكذّبین»

Ayah

قُل لِّمَن مَّا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ قُل لِّلَّهِ ۚ كَتَبَ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِهِ ٱلرَّحْمَةَ ۚ لَيَجْمَعَنَّكُمْ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ ۚ ٱلَّذِينَ خَسِرُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ فَهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۱۲)
Say, ‘To whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth?’ Say, ‘To Allah. He has made mercy incumbent upon Himself. He will surely gather you on the Day of Resurrection, in which there is no doubt. Those who have ruined their souls will not have faith.’ (12)

Key Points

The topic of the obligation of mercy upon Allah is mentioned twice in the Qur'an, both times in this Surah (Ayahs 12 and 54). The phrase «لاریب فیه» (there is no doubt in it) is mentioned both concerning the Qur'an and concerning the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah).

Just as Allah has made duties obligatory upon us, He has also decreed duties upon Himself, including: guiding: «اِنّ علینا للهدى» (250), providing sustenance: «على اللّه رزقها» (251), showing kindness (mercy): «كتب على نفسه الرّحمة» (252), and the victory and dominance of Allah's religion: «كتب اللّه لاغلبنّ انا و رسلى» (253).

However, the condition for benefiting from Divine Mercy is showing mercy to His servants. As stated in the Hadith: «مَن لایَرْحَم لا یُرحَم» (Whoever does not show mercy will not be shown mercy) (254).

Salman narrated from the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله that he said: Allah's Mercy has one hundred degrees. One degree of it has become the source of all Divine favors in this world. On the Day of Resurrection, Allah will deal with people using all one hundred degrees of His Mercy. (255)

The Qur'an considers Divine Mercy to encompass all things: «رَحمَتى وَسعت كلّ شى ء» (My Mercy encompasses all things) (256). This Mercy has numerous manifestations, including:

Rain: «ینزّل الغیث من بعد ما قنطوا وینشر رحمته» (257) Wind: «یُرسل الرّیاح بُشرىً بین یدى رحمته» (258) Night and Day: «و مِن رَحمته جَعَل لكم اللیل و النّهار» (259) The Prophet: «و ما أرسلناك الاّ رحمة للعالمین» (260) The Qur'an: «هذا بصائر للنّاس و هُدىً و رحمة» (261) The Torah: «كتاب موسى اماماً و رحمة» (262) Deliverance/Salvation: «فأنجیناه و الّذین معه برحمة منّا» (263) Affection for one's spouse: «و جعل بینكم مودّة و رحمة» (264) Plants and fruits: «فانظُر الى آثار رحمة اللّه كیف یُحیى الارض بعد موتها» (265) Acceptance of repentance: «لاتَقنطوا من رحمة اللّه» (266)

250) Layl, 12. 251) Hud, 6. 252) An‘am, 12. 253) Mujadilah, 31. 254) Kanz al-‘Ummal, Vol. 3, p. 162 and Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Qur’an. 255) Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Qur’an. 256) A‘raf, 156. 257) Shura, 28. 258) A‘raf, 57. 259) Qasas, 73. 260) Anbiya, 107. 261) Jathiyah, 20. 262) Hud, 17. 263) A‘raf, 72. 264) Rum, 21. 265) Rum, 50. 266) Zumar, 53.

Messages

1- One of the methods of propagation that the Prophet is commanded to use is posing questions and providing answers. «قل لمن... قل للّه» (Say: To whom... Say: To Allah). 2- Existence is founded upon mercy, and Divine Mercy is widespread over everything and everywhere. «كتب على نفسه الرّحمة» (He has prescribed mercy upon Himself). 3- Just as Allah's creations in this world (such as wind, rain, day and night, plants...) are mercy, the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is also mercy. «لیجمعنّكم» (He will surely gather you). 4- The reason for the establishment of the Day of Resurrection is the people's benefit from mercy. «كتب على نفسه الرّحمة لیجمعنّكم» (He has prescribed mercy upon Himself, that He will surely gather you). 5- Although Divine Mercy is vast and necessary, some groups deprive themselves of it. «خسروا انفسهم» (They have lost their souls). 6- Pursuing whims and desires instead of seeking evidence, turning to the Taghut (tyrants/false deities) instead of the friends of Allah, committing disbelief instead of having faith and belief in the Hereafter, and submitting to the Fire (Nar) instead of submitting to the Light (Nur)—this is the immense loss of the disbelievers. (The entire Ayah)

Ayah

۞ وَلَهُۥ مَا سَكَنَ فِى ٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (۱۳)
To Him belongs whatever abides in the night and the day, and He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing. (13)

Key Points

Night and day, like a cradle, provide tranquility to humans and creatures in their lap. Some creatures rest at night, and others rest during the day.

Messages

1- Both the entire system originates from Allah; «وله ما سكن», and supervision and control belong to Him. «هو السمیع العلیم».

Ayah

قُلْ أَغَيْرَ ٱللَّهِ أَتَّخِذُ وَلِيًّا فَاطِرِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَهُوَ يُطْعِمُ وَلَا يُطْعَمُ ۗ قُلْ إِنِّىٓ أُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ أَوَّلَ مَنْ أَسْلَمَ ۖ وَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (۱٤)
Say, ‘Shall I take for guardian [anyone] other than Allah, the originator of the heavens and the earth, who feeds and is not fed?’ Say, ‘I have been commanded to be the first of those who submit [to Allah], and [told,] “Never be one of the polytheists.” ’ (14)

Key Points

| It is mentioned in the context of the revelation (Shān Nuzūl) of this Ayah that a group of the people of Mecca told the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: "You have abandoned the idols because of poverty. We are ready to make you rich so that you do not oppose us." This Ayah was then revealed, providing a response to them.

Messages

| 1- One of the methods of propagation and guidance that the Prophet uses to awaken people's intellect is stating the argument in the form of a question. «قل أغیر اللَّه» | 2- Due to his needs and limitations, man instinctively seeks a guardian and a refuge, but the main discussion revolves around whom we should seek. «قل أغیر اللَّه اتّخذ...» | 3- Allah created the heavens and the earth without a prior model. «فاطر السموات» Fāṭir means the innovative Creator. | 4- Providing needs is among the functions of Divine guardianship (Wilāyah). «ولیّاً... یطعم» | 5- Except for Allah, all deities and creatures are needy. «لایطعم» | 6- Since Allah is both the Creator of mankind and His Sustainer, and He fulfills His needs, man must therefore show devotion and acceptance of His guardianship (Wilāyah). «فاطر... و هو یطعم... انّى اُمرتُ أن اكون اوّل مَن اسلم» | 7- The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is commanded to declare his ideological stance. «قل انّى اُمرت أن...» | 8- The leader must be a pioneer in the religion and possess the highest degree of sincerity and submission. «اوّل مَن أسلم» | 9- Accepting the guardianship (Wilāyah) of anyone other than Allah is polytheism (Shirk). «أغیراللَّه اتّخذ ولیّاً... لاتكوننّ من المشركین»

Ayah

قُلْ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ إِنْ عَصَيْتُ رَبِّى عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ (۱٥)
Say, ‘Indeed, should I disobey my Lord, I fear the punishment of a tremendous day.’ (15)

Key Points

Fear is of two types: A: Undesirable, such as fear of Jihad. B: Desirable, such as fear of the Divine punishment.

Messages

1- The Divine law is uniform for everyone; even the Prophet of God, if He disobeys, must fear its consequence. «أخاف اِن عَصیتُ ربّى»

2- The fear of Allah's saints is of Divine wrath, not of tyrants or people. «أخاف... ربّى»

3- Attention to the Lordship of Allah necessitates abstaining from disobeying Him. «ان عصیت ربّى»

4- Fear of punishment is among the factors that prevent deviation and error. «أخاف ان عصیت ربّى عذاب یوم عظیم»

5- The Messenger of God's expression of fear of the Day of Judgment has a constructive role for others. «قل انّى أخاف... عذاب یوم عظیم»

6- In the face of temptation and promises from others, use the leverage of the reckoning of the Day of Judgment. (Considering the occasion of revelation of the previous Ayah, where they suggested, "We will make you rich, just stop preaching," the Prophet says: "I fear the Day of Judgment.") «انّى اخاف... عذاب یوم عظیم»

Ayah

مَّن يُصْرَفْ عَنْهُ يَوْمَئِذٍ فَقَدْ رَحِمَهُۥ ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْمُبِينُ (۱٦)
Whoever is spared of it on that day, He has certainly been merciful to him, and that is manifest success. (16)

Key Points

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: "I swear by the God in Whose Hand is my soul, no one will enter Paradise on the Day of Resurrection merely by his deeds." They asked: "Not even you, O Messenger of Allah?" He replied: "Not even me, unless the favor and mercy of Allah encompass me." Then He placed His blessed hands on His head and recited this Ayah. [267] Of course, it is obvious that Divine Mercy is granted only to righteous deeds and virtuous individuals.

267) Tafasir Majma' al-Bayan and Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Danger threatens everyone, and salvation from Divine punishment requires special grace. «من یُصرف... فقد رَحِمَه» 2- Submission to Allah is the prerequisite for receiving Divine Mercy. «انّى اُمرت ان اكون اوّل مَن اسلم - من یُصرف عنه... فقد رَحِمَه» 3- Only Divine Mercy averts His wrath; the acceptance of our deeds and the intercession of Allah's saints are also a reflection of His Mercy. «مَن یُصرف... فقد رَحِمَه»

Ayah

وَإِن يَمْسَسْكَ ٱللَّهُ بِضُرٍّ فَلَا كَاشِفَ لَهُۥٓ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ وَإِن يَمْسَسْكَ بِخَيْرٍ فَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۱۷)
Should Allah visit you with some distress there is no one to remove it except Him; and should He bring you some good, then He has power over all things. (17)

Messages

1- The source of all affairs is one, not that good things originate from one source and evils from another. «و اِن یَمْسَسْك اللّه بضرّ... و اِن یَمْسَسْك بخیر»

2- All hopes must be directed towards Allah and all fears must be from Him. «فلا كاشف له الاّ هو»

3- Divine laws are not subject to exception; the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله must also be mindful of Allah when facing bitter and sweet events. «و اِن یَمْسَسْك اللّه بضرّ... واِن یَمْسَسْك بخیر فهو على كلّ شى ء قدیر»

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلْقَاهِرُ فَوْقَ عِبَادِهِۦ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْحَكِيمُ ٱلْخَبِيرُ (۱۸)
And He is the All-dominant over His servants, and He is the All-wise, the All-aware. (18)

Key Points

The word "Qahr" (قهر) refers to a type of dominance where the subjugated (maqhūr) is humiliated before the dominator (qāhir). In Ayah 14, the guardianship (wilāyah) and sustenance (rizāqiyyah) of Allah were discussed: «ولیّاً...و هو یطعم». In Ayah 15, the domination (qahr) and the Divine Resurrection: «انّى اخاف...عذاب». In Ayah 16, the salvation and mercy of Allah: «...فقد رَحِمَه». In Ayah 17, the resolution of problems and the attainment of good things: «فلا كاشف له الاّ هو». And in this Ayah, the absolute power of Allah is discussed: «و هو القاهر فوق عباده».

Messages

  1. We should not fear others, for the power of Allah is above all powers. «و هو القاهر فوق عباده»

  2. The power and dominance (Qahāriyyah) of Allah are accompanied by His wisdom and knowledge. «الحكیم الخبیر»

  3. Allah's granting of benefit or harm is based on His wisdom and awareness. «یَمْسسك اللَّه بضرّ... یَمْسسك بخیر... و هو الحكیم الخبیر»

Ayah

قُلْ أَىُّ شَىْءٍ أَكْبَرُ شَهَـٰدَةً ۖ قُلِ ٱللَّهُ ۖ شَهِيدٌۢ بَيْنِى وَبَيْنَكُمْ ۚ وَأُوحِىَ إِلَىَّ هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانُ لِأُنذِرَكُم بِهِۦ وَمَنۢ بَلَغَ ۚ أَئِنَّكُمْ لَتَشْهَدُونَ أَنَّ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ ءَالِهَةً أُخْرَىٰ ۚ قُل لَّآ أَشْهَدُ ۚ قُلْ إِنَّمَا هُوَ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ وَإِنَّنِى بَرِىٓءٌ مِّمَّا تُشْرِكُونَ (۱۹)
Say, ‘What thing is greatest as witness?’ Say, ‘Allah! [He is] witness between me and you, and this Quran has been revealed to me that I may warn thereby you and whomever it may reach.’ ‘Do you indeed bear witness that there are other gods besides Allah?’ Say, ‘I do not bear witness [to any such thing].’ Say, ‘Indeed He is the One God, and I indeed disown what you associate [with Him].’ (19)

Key Points

The polytheists of Mecca demanded a witness from the Prophet of Islam for his prophethood and refused to accept his mission, saying: "Even the Jews and Christians do not consider you a prophet." This Ayah, revealed during the period when Islam was marginalized (in its early stages), gives news of a bright future and the universal mission of Islam. Regarding the interpretation of the phrase «انّما هو اله واحد» (He is only one God), Imam Ali عليه السلام said: "If there were other gods, they too would have sent prophets." [268] A group of people was brought to the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله as captives. The Prophet asked them: "Have you ever been invited to Islam?" They replied: "No." The Prophet ordered that they all be taken to a safe place and freed, saying that one who has not yet heard the call of Islam should not be taken captive. He then recited this Ayah: «لانذركم به و من بلغ» [269] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: "Perpetual warning (Inthar) must be accompanied by a perpetual warner (Munthir). Therefore, divine Imamate and leadership must also accompany the Quran forever." [270] Usually, the motivation to avert harm is stronger in humans than the motivation to gain benefit. Therefore, the Quran has emphasized the issue of warning (Inthar). «لانذركم» (that I may warn you with it). In other Ayahs, we also read: «ان أنت الاّ نذیر» [271] (You are only a warner), and «انّما أنا نذیر مبین» [272] (I am only a clear warner). In numerous narrations, «مَن بَلَغ» (whomever it reaches) has been interpreted as referring to the Infallible Imams عليهم السلام. [273] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام also said regarding «مَن بَلَغ»: "The intended meaning is the one upon whom the duty of Imamate has fallen, and he warns the people with the Quran, just like the Prophet." [274]

268) Nahj al-Balaghah, Letter 31. 269) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur. 270) Tafsir Safi. 271) Fatir, 23. 272) Ankabut, 50. 273) Tafsir Ayyashi. 274) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 416.

Messages

  1. For the truthfulness of the Prophet of Islam, the witnessing of Allah is sufficient. «قل اللَّه شهید»
  2. Presenting and stating truths in the form of question and answer is one of the Quran's methods of propagation. «قل اىّ شى ء...قل اللَّه»
  3. Negligence, error, forgetfulness, and limitation reduce both man's ability to gather information and his ability to bear witness. Since God is far removed from these defects, Allah is the greatest of witnesses. «أكبر شهادة قل اللَّه»
  4. The Quran is the greatest witness to the Prophet's mission. «اللَّه شهید بینى و... هذا القرآن»
  5. For heedless people, speaking from the perspective of warning (Inthar) is more effective. «لانذركم»
  6. The mission of Prophet Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله is universal and eternal, intended for all people across all ages and generations. «لانذركم به و مَن بلغ»
  7. Until the law is conveyed to the people, there is no reproach or responsibility involved. «و من بلغ» (and whomever it reaches). In the terminology of the science of Usul (principles of jurisprudence), punishment without prior declaration is abhorrent (Qabih).
  8. Among the necessary conditions for a divine leader are: faith in the doctrine: «اوحى الى هذا القرآن» (this Quran has been revealed to me); hope for the future: «و مَن بَلَغ» (and whomever it reaches); firmness: «قل لا أشهد» (Say: I do not bear witness); and dissociation from polytheism: «انّنى برى ء ممّا تشركون» (Indeed, I am innocent of what you associate with Allah).

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ يَعْرِفُونَهُۥ كَمَا يَعْرِفُونَ أَبْنَآءَهُمُ ۘ ٱلَّذِينَ خَسِرُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ فَهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۲٠)
Those whom We have given the Book recognize him just as they recognize their sons. Those who have ruined their souls will not have faith. (20)

Key Points

This verse is similar to verse 146 of Surah Al-Baqarah. Both the name and characteristics of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله were present in the Torah and the Gospel, and the scholars of the People of the Book used to give glad tidings to the people about him as the promised Prophet. Furthermore, the morals of His Holiness and his companions were recorded in their books: «محمّد رسول اللّه والّذین معه أشدّاء علَى الكفّار رحماء بینهم... ذلك مثلهم فى التوراة و مثلهم فى الانجیل كزرع...» (275) The recognition of a child is the most fundamental and oldest form of recognition. This is because they are recognized from the moment of birth, whereas the recognition of a brother, father, or mother occurs months after birth, just as the recognition of a spouse is after marriage. Therefore, the verse says: they recognize the Prophet like they recognize their own children.

275) Al-Fath, 29.

Messages

  1. Allah has completed the proof against the People of the Book, and they knew the Prophet of Islam well. «الّذین آتیناهم الكتاب یعرفونه»
  2. The Prophet must be recognized in such a way that there is no doubt about him. «یعرفونه كما یعرفون أبناءهم»
  3. Knowledge and recognition alone are not the source of human salvation. How many knowers of God, knowers of the Prophet, and knowers of religion are among the losers due to obstinacy and stubbornness. «یعرفونه كما یعرفون ابناءهم الّذین خسروا أنفسهم»
  4. Concealing the truth leads to a bad end and loss. «خسروا أنفسهم فهم لایؤمنون»

Ayah

وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ ٱفْتَرَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا أَوْ كَذَّبَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِهِۦٓ ۗ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُفْلِحُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ (۲۱)
Who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah, or denies His signs? Indeed the wrongdoers will not be felicitous. (21)

Key Points

The phrase "مَن أظلَم" (who is more unjust) appears fifteen times in the Quran, referring to fabricating lies against Allah, preventing people from the mosque, and concealing testimony and the truth. This indicates that cultural injustice and preventing people from growth and understanding is the worst injustice against society.

Messages

1- The more precious and sacred something is, the greater the danger of injustice concerning it. Therefore, injustice against Allah and fabricating lies against His sacred essence is the worst of injustices. "ومن أظلم"

2- Injustice against human thought and culture is the worst of oppressions. Polytheism (shirk), fabricating lies against Allah, false claims of prophethood, innovation (bid'ah), and interpretation based on personal opinion (tafsir bi al-ra'y) are all examples of this type of injustice. "و مَن اظلم ممّن افترى على اللَّه كذبا او كذّب بآیاته"

Ayah

وَيَوْمَ نَحْشُرُهُمْ جَمِيعًا ثُمَّ نَقُولُ لِلَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوٓا۟ أَيْنَ شُرَكَآؤُكُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ كُنتُمْ تَزْعُمُونَ (۲۲)
On the day when We gather them all together, We shall say to those who ascribed partners [to Allah] ‘Where are your partners that you used to claim?’ (22)

Key Points

The humiliating interrogation of the polytheists (mushrikīn) on the Day of Judgment is a sign of their deprivation from salvation, the same deprivation that was mentioned in the previous Ayah. Although the Ayah focuses on polytheism (shirk), those who accept the leadership of others besides the Friends of Allah (Awliyā’ Allāh) and rise up in opposition and war against the infallible Divine Friends (Awliyā’ Allāh) are polytheists in a sense. In Ziyarat Jami'ah Kabirah, we read: “و مَن حاربكم مشرك” (And whoever fights you is a polytheist) [276] And in a Hadith, it is stated: “الرّاد علینا كالرّاد على اللّه و هو على حدّ الشرك باللّه” (The one who rejects us is like the one who rejects Allah, and this is on the verge of associating partners with Allah.) [277] The one who rejects our word and our path is like the one who does not accept the word of God, and such a person is considered a polytheist.

276) Mafatih al-Jinan. 277) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 67.

Messages

1- The court of the Day of Judgment is public. “نحشرهم جمیعاً ثم نقول”

2- Before adopting any belief, love, or worship, we must be ready to answer on the Day of Judgment. “و یوم نحشرهم... أین شركاؤكم”

3- The Day of Judgment is the day when the truth of Monotheism (Tawhid) becomes manifest, even for the polytheists. “أین شركاؤكم”

4- Polytheism (Shirk) is nothing more than an illusion. “تزعمون”

Ayahs

ثُمَّ لَمْ تَكُن فِتْنَتُهُمْ إِلَّآ أَن قَالُوا۟ وَٱللَّهِ رَبِّنَا مَا كُنَّا مُشْرِكِينَ (۲۳)
Then their only excuse will be to say, ‘By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists.’ (23)
ٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَذَبُوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ ۚ وَضَلَّ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَفْتَرُونَ (۲٤)
Look, how they forswear themselves, and what they used to fabricate has forsaken them! (24)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of fitnah (trial/excuse) in "لم تكن فتنتهم" (their excuse will not be) is the apology of the polytheists. [278] "ضلّ الشى ء" (something went astray) means the wasting, destruction, and annihilation of something. [279] Liars, according to the characteristic they acquired in this world, will also lie on the Day of Judgment. (They will say,) "واللّه ما كنّا مشركین" (By Allah, we were not polytheists), just as we read elsewhere: "یوم یبعثهم اللَّه جمیعاً فیَحلفون له كما یَحلفون لكم و یحسبون انّهم على شى ء الا انّهم هم الكاذبون" [280] Regarding this Ayah, Hadrat Ali عليه السلام states in a tradition: After this lie, their lips will be sealed, and their other limbs will speak the truth. [281] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: On the Day of Judgment, Allah will grant such pardon and forgiveness that it will not even cross anyone's mind, to the extent that even the polytheists will say: We were not polytheists (so that they may be included in the Divine pardon). [282]

278) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 279) Lisan al-Arab. 280) Al-Mujadilah, 18. 281) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa'iq. 282) Tafsir Ayyashi.

Messages

1- The Day of Judgment is so certain that it is as if it is present right now. (The command is) "اُنظر" (Look), and not "ستنظر" (You will look). (The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله can witness the scenes of the Day of Judgment even in this world through his celestial eye.)

2- Non-Divine supports will be annihilated. "ضلّ عنهم"

3- In the Divine court, neither man's lie nor his oath will be effective. "ضلّ عنهم ما كانوا یفترون"

4- All that insistence on non-Divine supports is nothing more than an illusion. "و ضلّ عنهم ما كانوا یفترون"

Ayah

وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَسْتَمِعُ إِلَيْكَ ۖ وَجَعَلْنَا عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ أَكِنَّةً أَن يَفْقَهُوهُ وَفِىٓ ءَاذَانِهِمْ وَقْرًا ۚ وَإِن يَرَوْا۟ كُلَّ ءَايَةٍ لَّا يُؤْمِنُوا۟ بِهَا ۚ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءُوكَ يُجَـٰدِلُونَكَ يَقُولُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّآ أَسَـٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۲٥)
There are some of them who prick up their ears at you, but We have cast veils on their hearts lest they should understand it, and a deafness into their ears; and though they should see every sign, they will not believe in it. When they come to you, to dispute with you, the faithless say, ‘These are nothing but myths of the ancients.’ (25)

Key Points

It is mentioned in the context of the revelation of this Ayah that Abu Sufyan, Walid ibn Mughirah, Utbah, Shaybah, and Nadr ibn Harith were listening to the Prophet's recitation of the Quran near the Ka'bah. They asked Nadr: What is He reciting? He said: By the God of the Ka'bah, I do not understand what He is reciting, but it is nothing but myths and stories of the ancients; I have told you similar stories. Then this Ayah was revealed. 'Akinna (أكنّة) is the plural of kinn (كنّ) or kinān (كنان), meaning a curtain or veil. Waqr (وقر) means heaviness (or deafness). And asāṭīr (أساطير) is the plural of usṭūrah (اسطوره), meaning successive and imaginary narratives that are attractive to hear. The veiling of the heart and the heaviness of the ear are due to the spirit of obstinacy of the polytheists. The Quran states: «فلما زاغوا أزاغ اللّه قلوبهم» (283) Since the previous Ayah spoke about the polytheists (mushrikīn), but this Ayah refers to the same group using the term al-ladhīna kafarū (الّذین كفروا - those who disbelieved), it becomes clear that the polytheists are the same as the disbelievers (kuffār).

283) Saff, 5.

Messages

1 - Prophets and Divine leaders must be aware of the attitudes of the disbelievers and opponents and their tactics. «و منهم مَن یستمع»

2 - Do not despair of all disbelievers; some of them are obstinate. «و منهم»

3 - Hearing the sound of the Quran is valuable only when it affects the heart. «أكنة ان یفقهوه»

4 - The disbelievers insist on their denial, and Allah's veiling of the disbelievers' hearts is due to their own obstinacy and insistence. «ان یَروا كلّ آیة لایؤمنوا»

5 - Obstinacy is an incurable disease, and like a wavy mirror, it makes even the best images appear ugly. «ان یروا كل آیة لایؤمنوا»

6 - If someone meets even the Prophet with the intention of dispute, suspicion, a negative attitude, and a preconceived position, he will gain no benefit. «جاءك یجادلونك...»

7 - The disbelievers had no correct or logical way to confront the Prophet of Islam. (Resorting to dispute and slander is a sign of their inability to use logic.) «یجادلونك...»

8 - The Quran is attractive even to the disbelievers and polytheists. «یقول الذین كفروا ان هذا الاّ اساطیر الاوّلین» (Asāṭīr (اساطیر) means beautiful speech. (284))

284) Lisān al-Arab.

Ayah

وَهُمْ يَنْهَوْنَ عَنْهُ وَيَنْـَٔوْنَ عَنْهُ ۖ وَإِن يُهْلِكُونَ إِلَّآ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَمَا يَشْعُرُونَ (۲٦)
They dissuade [others] from [following] him, and [themselves] avoid him; yet they destroy no one except themselves, but they are not aware. (26)

Key Points

The word «یَنئَون» is from the root "na'ā," meaning to distance or keep away. Some of the Ahl al-Sunnah, in their exegesis, consider this Ayah to be about Abu Talib, saying that he forbade people from harming the Prophet but did not believe himself and kept away from becoming Muslim. They consider some other verses to be about this as well (such as Tawbah 113, Qasas 56). However, in the view of the followers of the School of Ahl al-Bayt, Abu Talib is among the best Muslims, and the proofs of the faith of Abu Talib, the dear father of Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, are numerous, some of which I will mention:

1- The statements made by the Prophet Muḥammad صلى الله عليه وآله himself and the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام regarding his faith are the best proof of his faith. 2- His unwavering support for the Prophet during extremely difficult circumstances. 3- Abu Talib's acknowledgments in his poetry regarding the Prophet and his relationship with God. 4- His recommendations to his wife, son, and brother Hamzah to support Islam, prayer, and the Prophet. 5- The great sorrow and grief that afflicted the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله due to the death of Abu Talib. 6- Abu Bakr and Abbas testified that Abu Talib, at the time of his death, uttered the words «لااله الاّاللّه و محمّد رسول اللّه» (There is no god but Allah, and Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah). 7- The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله prayed for Abu Talib atop the pulpit, attended his funeral procession, and instructed Hadrat Ali عليه السلام to shroud him. The funeral prayer (Salat al-Mayyit) was not performed over him only because the funeral prayer had not yet been made obligatory until that day, just as it was not performed over Khadijah. 8- Hadrat Ali عليه السلام wrote in a letter to Mu'awiyah that there is a great difference between my father, Abu Talib, and your father, Abu Sufyan. 9- The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: On the Day of Resurrection, I will be the intercessor for my father, my mother, and my uncle Abu Talib. 10- We read in a Ḥadīth that Allah revealed to His Messenger that the Fire is forbidden upon your father, your mother, and your guardian (kafil). 11- There is no doubt about the Muslim faith of Fatimah bint Asad علیها السلام, who was a woman of excellent standing and an Emigrant (Muhajirah). If Abu Talib had not been a Muslim, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله would never have allowed the husband of this woman to be a faithless man. [285]

285) Al-Ghadir, Vol. 7 & 8; Al-Sahih min al-Sirah, Vol. 1, pp. 134-142.

Messages

1- The disbelievers and polytheists were always striving to obstruct and prevent people from listening to the Quran. «و هم ینهون عنه...» 2- Keeping away from accepting the truth leads to the destruction of the person himself. «و ما یهلكون الاّ انفسهم» 3- True consciousness is finding the path of truth; losing the path of truth and the true leader, regardless of who does it, is a lack of consciousness. «و ما یشعرون»

Ayah

وَلَوْ تَرَىٰٓ إِذْ وُقِفُوا۟ عَلَى ٱلنَّارِ فَقَالُوا۟ يَـٰلَيْتَنَا نُرَدُّ وَلَا نُكَذِّبَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ رَبِّنَا وَنَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۲۷)
Were you to see when they are brought to a halt by the Fire, whereupon they will say, ‘If only we were sent back [into the world]! Then we will not deny the signs of our Lord, and we will be among the faithful!’ (27)

Key Points

| According to the verses of the Quran, the desire to return to the world (Dunya) exists at the moment of death, in the grave, and on the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah): «ربّ ارجعون لعلّى اعمل صالحاً» [286], «ربّنا اخرجنا منها فان عُدنا فانّا ظالمون» [287]. In Ayah 23, the polytheists denied their polytheism, but in this Ayah, they confess and request return and rectification.

286) Mu’minun, 100. 287) Mu’minun, 107.

Messages

| 1- When the disbelievers (kuffar) are presented to the Fire, they cry out in terror and humiliation. «اذ وقفوا على النّار فقالوا» The stages and stations (mawaqif) of the Day of Judgment are numerous; in some stages, the sinners wail, and in other stations, the seal of silence is placed upon their mouths. 2- Denying the Divine verses leads to regret in the Hereafter. «اذ وقفوا... فقالوا یا لیتنا» 3- Let us believe while the opportunity in this world remains, because there is no chance of return in the Hereafter. «یا لیتنا نردّ» Among the wishes and regrets of the disbelievers on the Day of Judgment is returning to the world, believing, and obeying. «و لا نكذّب... و نكون من المؤمنین»

Ayah

بَلْ بَدَا لَهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يُخْفُونَ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَلَوْ رُدُّوا۟ لَعَادُوا۟ لِمَا نُهُوا۟ عَنْهُ وَإِنَّهُمْ لَكَـٰذِبُونَ (۲۸)
Indeed, what they used to hide before has now become evident to them. But were they to be sent back they would revert to what they were forbidden, and they are indeed liars. (28)

Key Points

The Day of Resurrection is the day when people's hidden secrets are revealed, and the Quran has referred to this truth many times. Including: «وبدالهم سیّئات ما عملوا» [288] , «و بدالهم سیّئات ما كسبوا» [289]

288) Jathiyah, 33. 289) Zumar, 48.

Messages

1- On the Day of Resurrection, all secrets and mysteries will be uncovered, and the hidden inner self of humans will be revealed. «بل بدالهم ما كانوا یخفون» 2- Returning from the Hereafter to the world is impossible. «و لو ردّوا» 3- For some, there is never any hope for their betterment or reform; even if they are given a chance (which they themselves request), they will return to being exactly as they were. «لو ردّوا لعادوا» (Yes, man often makes resolutions in this world when facing hardships and bitterness, but once he achieves comfort, he forgets everything.) 4- When lying becomes a characteristic of man, he will lie even on the Day of Resurrection. «و انّهم لكاذبون» Just as we read in Ayah 23 that the polytheists will swear falsely on the Day of Resurrection, saying: «واللَّه ربّنا ما كنّا مشركین»

Ayahs

وَقَالُوٓا۟ إِنْ هِىَ إِلَّا حَيَاتُنَا ٱلدُّنْيَا وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمَبْعُوثِينَ (۲۹)
They say, ‘There is nothing but our life of this world, and we shall not be resurrected.’ (29)
وَلَوْ تَرَىٰٓ إِذْ وُقِفُوا۟ عَلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ ۚ قَالَ أَلَيْسَ هَـٰذَا بِٱلْحَقِّ ۚ قَالُوا۟ بَلَىٰ وَرَبِّنَا ۚ قَالَ فَذُوقُوا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ (۳٠)
Were you to see when they are brought to a halt before their Lord. He will say, ‘Is this not a fact?’ They will say, ‘Yes, by our Lord!’ He will say, ‘So taste the punishment because of what you used to deny.’ (30)

Key Points

In Ayahs 27 and 30, the phrase "و لو ترى" (and if you could see) is repeated twice to depict the harsh scenes of the Day of Judgment and serve as a step toward guiding people. According to Ayah 30, Allah speaks to the criminal. However, some Ayahs negate God's speaking to them: "لایكلّمهم اللَّه" (Allah will not speak to them). This matter either refers to the different stations and scenes on the Day of Judgment, or it means that Allah will not speak to them with pleasant and comforting words. In Ayah 22, Allah held the disbelievers accountable for denying Monotheism: "أین شركاؤكم" (Where are your partners?). In Ayah 27, He held them accountable for denying Prophethood: "لانكذّب بایات ربّنا" (We would not deny the signs of our Lord). And in Ayah 30, He holds them accountable for denying the Day of Judgment: "ألیس هذا بالحقّ" (Is this not the truth?). Islam outlines several types of life for human beings: 1- Worldly life. «زهرة الحیاة الدّنیا» (the splendor of the life of this world) [290] 2- Barzakh (intermediate) life. «من ورائهم برزخ الى یوم یبعثون» (behind them is a barrier until the Day they are resurrected) [291] 3- Spiritual life and guidance. «دعاكم لما یحییكم» (He called you to that which gives you life) [292] 4- Social life. «ولكم فى القصاص حیاة» (And there is for you in legal retribution [saving of] life) [293] 5 - Pure life (Hayat Tayyibah) (in the shade of a tranquil heart and contentment). «فلنحیّینه حیاة طیّبة» (We will surely cause him to live a good life) [294]

290) Taha, 131. 291) Al-Mu'minun, 100. 292) Al-Anfal, 24. 293) Al-Baqarah, 179. 294) An-Nahl, 97.

Messages

1- The polytheists are superficial and materialistic individuals who see the scope of life only in this world and deny the Resurrection. «اِن هى الاّ حیاتنا الدّنیا» (It is nothing but our worldly life). (If, elsewhere in the Quran, the polytheists consider idols their intercessors: «هؤلاء شفعاؤنا» [These are our intercessors] [295], their intention is intercession in this world [296].) 2- Allah consoles and comforts His Prophet that all obstinacies will not go unanswered. «و لوترى» (And if you could see). 3- The criminals will be detained and held, like humiliated captives. «اذ وقفوا» (when they are made to stand). 4- On the Day of Judgment, the Judge and Interrogator is primarily Allah Himself. «قال ألیس هذا بالحقّ» (He will say, 'Is this not the truth?'). 5- The confessions of the disbelievers and polytheists on the Day of Judgment will be of no benefit. «قالوا بلى ... فذوقوا» (They will say, 'Yes'... So taste). 6- The disbelievers will swear oaths repeatedly on the Day of Judgment. «واللّه ربّنا ما كنّا مشركین - قالوا بلى و ربّنا» (By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists) - (They will say, 'Yes, by our Lord'). 7- The punishments of the Day of Judgment are due to persistence in disbelief and the denial of the Resurrection in the world. «و مانحن بمبعوثین... فذوقوا العذاب بما كنتم تكفرون» (and we will not be resurrected... So taste the punishment because you used to disbelieve).

295) Yunus, 18. 296) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Ayah

قَدْ خَسِرَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِلِقَآءِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءَتْهُمُ ٱلسَّاعَةُ بَغْتَةً قَالُوا۟ يَـٰحَسْرَتَنَا عَلَىٰ مَا فَرَّطْنَا فِيهَا وَهُمْ يَحْمِلُونَ أَوْزَارَهُمْ عَلَىٰ ظُهُورِهِمْ ۚ أَلَا سَآءَ مَا يَزِرُونَ (۳۱)
They are certainly losers who deny the encounter with Allah. When the Hour overtakes them suddenly, they will say, ‘Alas for us, for what we neglected in it!’ And they will bear their burdens on their backs. Look! Evil is what they bear! (31)

Key Points

The meaning of “Liqā’ Allāh” (meeting with Allah) is a spiritual encounter and inner witnessing (shahūd) on the Day of Judgment, because on that Day, all human attachments to wealth, status, and relatives are severed, and by facing the divine reward and punishment, man realizes the absolute sovereignty of Allah. Ḥasrah (regret/sorrow) is for the loss of benefits, while Nadāmah (remorse) is for the occurrence of harms. [297] The Day of Judgment is called “al-Sā‘ah” (the Hour) because it will be established in a moment, like a flash of lightning. (In Ayah 77 of Sūrat An-Naḥl, it states: «وما أمر الساعة الاّ كلَمح البصر أو هو اقرب», the establishment of the Hour is like the twinkling of an eye, or even nearer.) In a Ḥadīth from the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, it is stated: The people of the Fire will see their place (the place that Allah had designated for every person in Paradise) in Paradise, and realizing they have suffered loss, they will say: «یاحسرتنا». [298]

297) Tafsīr Aṭyab al-Bayān. 298) Tafsīr Nūr al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Whoever does not accept the Day of Judgment has traded his existence for the transient world, and this is the greatest loss. «قد خسر» 2- The Day of Judgment arrives suddenly, and no one is aware of its time, so one must always be prepared. «جاءتهم الساعة بغتة» 3- The Day of Judgment is the Day of regret (ḥasrah). «یا حسرتنا» 4- Confession and regret are signs of man's freedom in action, because he could have acted well but did not. «یا حسرتنا» 5- The Day of Judgment is the day of regret and sorrow, but regrets on that Day are fruitless. «قالوا یا حسرتنا... و هم یحملون...» 6- Sins will be embodied on the Day of Judgment and will be loaded upon man. «یحملون أوزارهم» 7- Denying or forgetting the Day of Judgment is the cause of committing sins which will become a burden (wizr) upon man on the Day of Judgment. «كذّبوا أوزارهم»

Ayah

وَمَا ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَآ إِلَّا لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ ۖ وَلَلدَّارُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ لِّلَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ ۗ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ (۳۲)
The life of the world is nothing but play and diversion, and the abode of the Hereafter is surely better for those who are Godwary. Do you not exercise your reason? (32)

Key Points

| If this world is not established as the farm (or field) for the Hereafter, it becomes a mere plaything, and people become like children engrossed in toys such as wealth, status, and the like. It is like a stage play where one person wears the clothes of a king, another plays the role of a servant, and a third becomes a minister, but an hour later, when all the costumes and roles are set aside, they realize that all the titles were nothing more than imaginary. The goodness of the Hereafter is due to the fact that its pleasures are neither mixed with suffering nor are they fleeting, illusory, or imaginary. Considering other verses of the Quran, this verse should not be interpreted as an invitation to monasticism (Rahbaniyyah) and the abandonment of the world. The word la'b (لعب) refers to an action that lacks a proper intention, and the word lahw (لهو) refers to an action that distracts a person from important and essential tasks. [299] The resemblance of the world to lahw (distraction) and la'b (play) is due to several aspects: A: The world, like a game, is short in duration. B: The world, like a game, involves both enjoyment and fatigue, and is a mixture of bitterness and sweetness. C: Heedless people without purpose make the game their occupation. D: Worldliness, like a game, distracts a person from important goals.

299) Mufradat al-Raghib.

Messages

| 1- World-centeredness is a childish and futile thought. (Lahw and la'b are usually the actions of children, and preoccupation with the world distracts a person from the Hereafter.) «لهو ولعب» 2- The path to salvation from the regret of the Hereafter is reflection and intellect. «یا حسرتنا... أفلا تعقلون» 3- The wise person is not deceived by the world. «و للدّار الاخرة خیر... أفلا تعقلون» 4- Failure to employ intellect is the basis for focusing on the world and neglecting the Hereafter. «أفلاتعقلون» 5- Neglect of the Hereafter leads to reproach. «أفلا تعقلون» 6- Intellect (ta'aqqul) and piety (taqwa) are inseparable companions. «یتّقون أفلاتعقلون»

Ayah

قَدْ نَعْلَمُ إِنَّهُۥ لَيَحْزُنُكَ ٱلَّذِى يَقُولُونَ ۖ فَإِنَّهُمْ لَا يُكَذِّبُونَكَ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ يَجْحَدُونَ (۳۳)
We certainly know that what they say grieves you. Yet it is not you that they deny, but it is Allah’s signs that the wrongdoers impugn. (33)

Key Points

| Regarding the occasion of revelation (Shān Nuzūl) of the Ayah, it is narrated that: The enemies of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله knew him to be trustworthy (Amīn) and truthful (Sādiq), but they would say: If we confirm him, our tribe and position will be humiliated. Sometimes they would also say: He is truthful, but he imagines that revelation has come to him. Therefore, they would deny these divine verses. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said concerning "لا یكذّبونك" (Lā yukadhdhibūnaka): It means that they cannot invalidate your speech and claim. [300]

300) Tafsir Ayyashi.

Messages

| 1- The leader should not be saddened by the denial of opponents. For this reason, Allah consoles and supports His Prophet. «قد نعلم انّه لیحزنك» 2- Allah is aware of the inner states of man. «قد نعلم انّه لیحزنك» 3- Denying the Prophet is denying Allah, just as pledging allegiance to the Prophet is pledging allegiance to Allah. «لایكذّبونك ولكن الظالمین بایات اللَّه یجحدون» 4- Considering the greater difficulties, denials, and insults faced by others, we should regard our own problems as easy. «لایكذّبونك... بآیات اللَّه یجحدون» 5- The opponents of the Messenger of Allah are against the ideology (Maktab), not the person. «لایكذّبونك... بآیات اللّه یجحدون» 6- Denying the divine verses is oppression against oneself, oppression against the Messenger of Allah, oppression against the ideology, and oppression against future generations. «ولكنّ الظالمین بآیات اللّه یجحدون»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ كُذِّبَتْ رُسُلٌ مِّن قَبْلِكَ فَصَبَرُوا۟ عَلَىٰ مَا كُذِّبُوا۟ وَأُوذُوا۟ حَتَّىٰٓ أَتَىٰهُمْ نَصْرُنَا ۚ وَلَا مُبَدِّلَ لِكَلِمَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَلَقَدْ جَآءَكَ مِن نَّبَإِى۟ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۳٤)
Apostles were certainly denied before you, yet they patiently bore being denied and tormented until Our help came to them. Nothing can change the words of Allah, and there have certainly come to you some of the accounts of the apostles. (34)

Key Points

| «كلمات» (Words) means the Divine traditions (Sunan): «ولقد سبقت كلمتنا لعبادنا المرسلین انّهم لهم المنصورون وانّ جُندنا لهم الغالبون» [301] History is an accepted source and resource for understanding events. One must learn lessons from the patience of previous prophets and the destruction of nations like the people of Hud, Salih, and Lot, and others... who denied [the truth]. The Divine tradition and plan is the sending of Messengers and the freedom of people to choose. He punishes the disbelievers and aids the Messengers in the path of invitation. In several verses of the Qur'an, Allah has made the aid and victory of patient leaders and believers incumbent upon Himself, including: * «كتب اللّه لاغلبنّ أنا و رسلى» [302] * «وكان حقّاً علینا نصر المؤمنین» [303] * «انّا لننصر رسلنا» [304] * «ولینصرنّ اللَّه من ینصره» [305]

301) Saffat, 171 to 173. 302) Mujadilah, 21. 303) Rum, 47. 304) Ghafir, 51. 305) Hajj, 40.

Messages

| 1- The path of truth has never been smooth, and the realization of the prophets' ideals has been accompanied by enduring difficulties. «فصبروا على ما كذّبوا» 2- One of the best levers for resistance against hardships is the mention of Divine aid. «فصبروا... حتّى اتاهم نصرنا» 3- Leaders should not expect obedience from everyone. «كُذّبوا» 4- The main condition for victory is patience. «فصبروا... حتّى اتاهم نصرنا» (Allah gives the patient the glad tidings of victory.) 5- Enemies spare no effort against the truth, whether it be denial or persecution. «كذّبوا، اوذوا» 6- Continuous patience paves the way for the descent of Divine help and mercy. «فصبروا... حتّى أتاهم نصرنا» 7- The truth is victorious. «اتاهم نصرنا» 8- The Divine traditions are unchangeable, and Allah does not break His promise. «لامبدّل لكلمات اللّه» 9- Everyone must recognize their historical counterparts. Allah addresses the Prophet, saying: «و لقد جاءك من نبأ المرسلین» 10- The efforts and sufferings of those who came before must be remembered and appreciated. «و لقد جاءك من نبأ المرسلین»

Ayah

وَإِن كَانَ كَبُرَ عَلَيْكَ إِعْرَاضُهُمْ فَإِنِ ٱسْتَطَعْتَ أَن تَبْتَغِىَ نَفَقًا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ أَوْ سُلَّمًا فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ فَتَأْتِيَهُم بِـَٔايَةٍ ۚ وَلَوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ لَجَمَعَهُمْ عَلَى ٱلْهُدَىٰ ۚ فَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْجَـٰهِلِينَ (۳٥)
And should their aversion be hard on you, find, if you can, a tunnel into the ground, or a ladder into sky, that you may bring them a sign. Had Allah wished, He would have brought them together on guidance. So do not be one of the ignorant. (35)

Key Points

It is mentioned in the occasion of revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of the Ayah: The disbelievers used to tell the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله: We will never believe in you unless you split the earth and cause a spring to gush forth for us: «لن نؤمن لك حتّى تَفجر لنا مِن الارض یَنبوعاً» [306], or you ascend to the sky: «أو ترقى فى السماء» [307]. Perhaps the Ayah refers to the nature of their undue demands, meaning that if you dig into the earth or place a ladder to the sky and put yourself through great hardship, it will be of no benefit. There is no flaw in your invitation; these people are obstinate, so do not show so much concern for their guidance.

306) Isra, 90. 307) Isra, 93.

Messages

1- The Prophet was compassionate regarding the guidance of the people and saddened by their turning away. «و ان كان كبر علیك اعراضهم» 2- The Prophet must recognize the dispositions of his audience and know that a group will turn away from any miracle brought to them. «اعراضهم» 3- The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله desired that people be guided by any means, but the problem lies in the obstinate disposition of the disbelievers, about which Allah warns. «تبتغى نفقاً فى الارض او سلّماً فى السماء» 4- Allah is able to guide everyone, but His wisdom necessitates that man be free. «و لو شاء اللَّه لجمعهم على الهدى» 5- Satisfying the demands of fault-finders and the impatience of preachers is ignorance. «فلا تكونن من الجاهلین» (The root of many undue expectations is ignorance.)

Ayah

۞ إِنَّمَا يَسْتَجِيبُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَسْمَعُونَ ۘ وَٱلْمَوْتَىٰ يَبْعَثُهُمُ ٱللَّهُ ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ يُرْجَعُونَ (۳٦)
Only those who listen will respond [to you]. As for the dead, Allah will resurrect them, then they will be brought back to Him. (36)

Key Points

| The Quran has repeatedly referred to the disbelievers using the expression of being dead and deaf. In Ayah 80 of Surah An-Naml and Ayah 52 of Surah Ar-Rum, we read: «فانّك لاتسمع الموتى و لا تسمع الصمّ الدعاء اذا وَلّوا مُدبرین»

Messages

| 1- Man is free in choosing his path. «انّما یستجیب...» | 2- Hearing and accepting the truth is a sign of spiritual life and vitality of the heart. (Indeed, whoever lacks spiritual life and does not accept the truth is dead, because animals also possess life in the sense of eating and sleeping.) «والموتى...» | 3- The truth-seeking hearts are entrusted to you (O Prophet), and the disbelievers are entrusted to Me, so that We may settle their account after the Resurrection. «والموتى یبعثهم اللَّه»

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ لَوْلَا نُزِّلَ عَلَيْهِ ءَايَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ ۚ قُلْ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ قَادِرٌ عَلَىٰٓ أَن يُنَزِّلَ ءَايَةً وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۳۷)
They say, ‘Why has not a sign been sent down to him from his Lord?’ Say, ‘Allah is indeed able to send down a sign,’ but most of them do not know. (37)

Key Points

| Regarding the occasion of revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of this Ayah, it is said that some leaders of Quraysh engaged in malicious propaganda and creating a hostile atmosphere, and out of mere pretext, they told the Prophet of Islam that the Qur'an alone was not sufficient as a miracle, but that he should bring miracles like those of Jesus, Moses, Salih, and others. [308] Of course, a Prophet who reminds people of the miracles of previous Prophets must certainly be able to bring similar ones; otherwise, He would not remind people of those miracles only to have them request similar ones. Furthermore, according to both Shi'a and Sunni narrations, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله had other miracles besides the Qur'an. The purpose of bringing a miracle is to declare the special relationship between God and the Messenger, and to show the infinite Divine power, not to fulfill the endless desires of stubborn people. Of course, sometimes the requested miracle was brought forth to complete the argument (Itmam al-Hujjah). History testifies that successive miracles did not lead the stubborn to guidance, but rather resulted in Divine wrath and punishment. The Qur'an states: If We were to send down the angels to them, or if the dead were to speak to them, still a group of the stubborn would not believe. [309]

308) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan. 309) Al-An‘am, 111.

Messages

| 1- Respond to the pretexts of the opponents. «قالوا... قل» 2- Allah is Capable (Qadir), but He is also Wise (Hakim), and His power manifests when the action and the request are wise. «انّ اللَّه قادر... اكثرهم لا یعلمون»

Ayah

وَمَا مِن دَآبَّةٍ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا طَـٰٓئِرٍ يَطِيرُ بِجَنَاحَيْهِ إِلَّآ أُمَمٌ أَمْثَالُكُم ۚ مَّا فَرَّطْنَا فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مِن شَىْءٍ ۚ ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ يُحْشَرُونَ (۳۸)
There is no animal on land, nor a bird that flies with its wings, but they are communities like yourselves. We have not omitted anything from the Book. Then they will be mustered toward their Lord. (38)

Key Points

The Quran pays special attention to the lives of animals and uses the remembrance of their creation, consciousness, and characteristics for the guidance of people. «و فى خلقكم و ما یبثّ من دابّة آیات لقوم یوقنون» (310) In narrations and human experiences, many examples regarding the consciousness and understanding of animals are observed, and one must reflect and pay attention to find similarities and become familiar with the lives of animals. (311) «امم أمثالكم»

The meaning of "the Book" (كتاب) in the Ayah is either the Quran or the "Preserved Tablet" (لوح محفوظ), which is essentially the Book of Creation. In other verses, expressions such as: «تبیاناً لكلّ شى ء» (312), and «كلٌّ فى كتاب مبین» (313) have appeared, which are signs of the completeness of the Quran.

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The sign of the Quran's perfection is that it is the root and source of all the sayings of the Messenger of Allah and the Imams عليهم السلام. Therefore, whenever we narrate a Hadith, ask us for its Quranic reference.

In a Hadith, the Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله prohibits gossip (gossip and hearsay), wasting wealth, and incessant questioning. When they asked Imam Baqir عليه السلام for its Quranic reference, He said: The reference for the prohibition of gossip is the Ayah: «لا خيرَ فى كَثير مِن نَجواهم الاّ مَن أمَرَ بِصدقةٍ أو مَعروفٍ أو اصلاح بَینَ النّاس» (314). The reference for wasting wealth is the Ayah: «و لاتؤتوا السفهاء أموالكم الّتى جعل اللَّه لكم قیاماً» (315). And the reference for improper questioning is this Ayah: «لاتسألوا عن أشياء اِن تبد لكم تسؤكم» (316).

Consciousness of Animals

It is evident from the verses, narrations, and experiences that consciousness is not exclusive to humans. Pay attention to the following examples:

1- When Prophet Sulayman (Solomon) was passing through an area with his armies, an ant told the other ants: Go quickly into your homes, lest the army of Sulayman crush you underfoot. (317) Recognizing the enemy is part of the ant's instinct, but knowing that his name is Sulayman and that his companions are his army is beyond instinct. 2- The hoopoe, while in the sky, became aware of the polytheism (shirk) of the people on the ground and reported to Sulayman that the people of the Saba region were not monotheists. It then received a special mission. Recognizing Monotheism (Tawhid) and polytheism, the ugliness of polytheism, the necessity of reporting to Prophet Sulayman, and the special mission of delivering a message—this is an issue beyond instinct. (318) 3- The fact that the hoopoe offered a justifiable excuse and an acceptable reason when Prophet Sulayman questioned him about his absence is a sign of consciousness higher than instinct. (319) 4- The fact that the Quran says all creatures glorify Allah (perform tasbih), but you do not understand it (320), means this is not the inherent, existential glorification (tasbih takwini), because we do understand that. Therefore, the Quran is speaking of another kind of glorification. 5- In the verses of the Quran, prostration (sujud) to Allah is attributed to all creatures. «وللَّه یسجد ما فى السموات و ما فى الارض...» (321) 6- Birds participated in Prophet Sulayman's assembly. «وحشر لسلیمان جنوده من الجن و الانس والطیر» (322) 7- Birds speaking with one another, and Sulayman taking pride that Allah taught him the language of the birds. «علّمنا منطق الطیر» (323) 8- The Ayah «واذا الوُحوشُ حُشِرَت» (324) discusses the gathering (resurrection) of wild beasts on the Day of Judgment. 9- The Ayah «والطّیر صافّات كلٌّ قد عَلِم صلاته و تسبیحه» (325) is a sign of the consciousness and aware worship (ibadah) of animals. 10- The existence of loyalty in some animals, including the dog toward its owner and children. 11- Training hunting dogs and police dogs to detect smuggling or purchase goods is a sign of that animal's specific awareness. 12- Islam has prohibited the slaughter of an animal in front of another animal, which is a sign of the animal's consciousness regarding slaughter and killing.

(310) Al-Jathiyah, 4. (311) Tafsir Maraghi. (312) An-Nahl, 89. (313) Hud, 6. (314) An-Nisa, 114. (315) An-Nisa, 5. (316) Al-Ma'idah, 101; Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. (317) An-Naml, 18. (318) An-Naml, 22. (319) An-Naml, 22 to 26. (320) Al-Isra, 44. (321) An-Nahl, 49. (322) An-Naml, 17. (323) An-Naml, 16. (324) At-Takwir, 5. (325) An-Nur, 41.

Messages

1- Divine power in creation and the purposeful nature of creation are the same for all beings. «من دابّة... ولا طائر ...» 2- Order and social life are not exclusive to humans; they are also observed in the lives of animals. «اُمم أمثالكم» 3- Humans and animals all need Divine planning. Allah grants them consciousness according to wisdom, and all possess order and a system. «اُمم أمثالكم» 4- The Holy Quran is the most comprehensive and complete Divine Book. «ما فرّطنا فى الكتاب من شى ء» 5- Whatever leads to the guidance, nurturing, and growth of humans is contained in the Quran. (The Quran lacks nothing in proving the truthfulness of the Prophet of Islam and guiding people.) «ما فرّطنا فى الكتاب» 6- We should not commit injustice or negligence toward animals; they also have the right to life, just like us. «اُمم (326) أمثالكم» 7- Resurrection (Ma'ad) and gathering (Hashr) are not exclusive to humans. «و ما من دابّة... ثمّ الى ربّهم یحشرون» 8- The movement of all creatures toward Allah is an evolutionary movement and a manifestation of Divine Lordship (Rububiyyah). «الى ربّهم یحشرون»

(326) Tafsir Qurtubi.

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا صُمٌّ وَبُكْمٌ فِى ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتِ ۗ مَن يَشَإِ ٱللَّهُ يُضْلِلْهُ وَمَن يَشَأْ يَجْعَلْهُ عَلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (۳۹)
Those who deny Our signs are deaf and dumb, in a manifold darkness. Allah leads astray whomever He wishes, and whomever He wishes He puts him on a straight path. (39)

Key Points

| Although guidance and misguidance are in the hand of Allah, Allah has also granted man will and choice, and the work of Allah is based on wisdom. For example, man's striving (Jihad) in the way of Allah is the basis for divine guidance towards him: «و الّذین جاهدوا فینا لنهدینّهم سُبلنا» [327] Just as oppression against people causes misguidance: «یضلّ اللَّه الظالمین» [328] Denying the divine verses causes Allah's will to misguide man. «كذّبوا... یضلله»

327) Ankabut, 69. 328) Ibrahim, 27.

Messages

| 1- Disbelief (Kufr) and obstinacy are darkness and gloom that cause man to be deprived of utilizing the tools of knowledge. «كذّبوا... صمّ و بكمٌ فى الظلمات» 2- Denying and concealing the truth is "muteness," and not hearing the truth is "deafness." «و الّذین كذّبوا...صمٌ و بكمٌ» 3- The result of denying the divine verses is misguidance and divine wrath. «كذّبوا... یضلله» 4- Moving on the straight path requires a listening ear, a truth-speaking tongue, and inner light. «یجعله على صراط مستقیم»

Ayah

قُلْ أَرَءَيْتَكُمْ إِنْ أَتَىٰكُمْ عَذَابُ ٱللَّهِ أَوْ أَتَتْكُمُ ٱلسَّاعَةُ أَغَيْرَ ٱللَّهِ تَدْعُونَ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (٤٠)
Say, ‘Tell me, should Allah’s punishment overtake you, or should the Hour overtake you, will you supplicate anyone other than Allah, should you be truthful? (40)

Key Points

| Man in a state of comfort and ordinary life is usually heedless, but when encountering hardships, the veil of heedlessness is lifted, and the human nature (fitrah) of seeking God and monotheism appears.

Messages

| 1- One of the methods of propagation and education is questioning the audience. «قل أرایتكم...» | 2- Experience shows that in all human beings (even if they outwardly commit disbelief), there is the nature (fitrah) of seeking God, and it manifests in man during calamities. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is commanded to draw people's attention to this dormant nature. «قل أرایتكم...» | 3- During incidents and hardships, the veils are lifted, and man pays attention only to Allah. The lack of attention to other deities is a sign of their futility. «أغیر اللَّه تدعون»

Ayah

بَلْ إِيَّاهُ تَدْعُونَ فَيَكْشِفُ مَا تَدْعُونَ إِلَيْهِ إِن شَآءَ وَتَنسَوْنَ مَا تُشْرِكُونَ (٤۱)
No, Him you will supplicate, and He will remove that for which you supplicated Him, if He wishes, and you will forget what you ascribe [to Him] as [His] partners.’ (41)

Key Points

Removing the punishment is either for completing the proof (Hujjah) or due to a change in the condition of the people. As He says in Ayah 98 of Surah Yunus: «لمّا آمنوا كشفنا عنهم عذاب الخزى» (When they believed, We removed from them the punishment of disgrace and humiliation.)

Messages

1- All imaginary deities are forgotten when man encounters calamities. «بل ایّاه تدعون... و تنسون ما تشركون» They are not only forgotten but also denied. As we read in another Ayah: «واللَّه ربّنا ما كنّا مشركین» [329] The polytheists deny their polytheism on the Day of Resurrection by swearing an oath. 2- Sincere supplication is accompanied by acceptance (response). «بل ایّاه تدعون فیكشف» 3- Despair from everything other than Allah causes speed in the acceptance of the supplication. (The letter 'fa' in the word «فیكشف») 4- The acceptance of the supplication is subject to the wise Will and Decree of Allah. «فكیشف....ان شاء»

329) Al-An'am, 23.

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَآ إِلَىٰٓ أُمَمٍ مِّن قَبْلِكَ فَأَخَذْنَـٰهُم بِٱلْبَأْسَآءِ وَٱلضَّرَّآءِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَضَرَّعُونَ (٤۲)
We have certainly sent [apostles] to nations before you, then We seized them with stress and distress so that they might entreat [Us]. (42)

Key Points

Al-Ba'sā' (بأساء) means war, poverty, famine, flood, earthquake, and contagious diseases, while Ad-Darrā' (ضرّاء) means grief, sorrow, loss of reputation, ignorance, and bankruptcy. Imam Ali عليه السلام said: If people sincerely lament to Allah during calamities, they will be covered by His grace. [330] Mawlawi (Rumi), in depicting this reality, says:

A single cry of need before the Truth (Allah) Is better than a lifetime spent in prostration and prayer. Abandon force and embrace lamentation; Mercy comes toward lamentation, O poor one.

330) Tafsir Safi and Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The sending of Prophets among people has been one of the Divine traditions throughout history. «لقد ارسلنا الى اُمَم من قبلك» 2- The history of those who passed is a lesson for those who follow. «قبلك» 3- In education and guidance, pressure and strictness are sometimes necessary. «اخذناهم بالبأساء و الضرّاء» 4- Difficulties are a path to awakening the innate nature (Fitrah) and turning attention toward Allah. «یتضرّعون» 5- Not every comfort is a grace, and not every hardship is wrath. «لعلّهم یتضرّعون» 6- Not all stubborn individuals become submissive even when placed under pressure, constraint, and hardship. «لعلّهم یتضرّعون»

Ayah

فَلَوْلَآ إِذْ جَآءَهُم بَأْسُنَا تَضَرَّعُوا۟ وَلَـٰكِن قَسَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَزَيَّنَ لَهُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (٤۳)
Why did they not entreat when Our punishment overtook them! But their hearts had hardened, and Satan had made what they had been doing seem decorous to them. (43)

Messages

| 1- Humble entreaty (supplication) to Allah leads to growth and proximity to Him, while abandoning it is a sign of hardness of heart and infatuation. «فلولا... تضرّعوا» 2- Man innately loves beauty, to the extent that Satan uses this very instinct to deceive him. «زیّن لهم الشیطان» 3- For the obstinate, neither preaching nor chastisement is effective. «قست قلوبهم» 4- The root of arrogance and the abandonment of supplication is being captivated by Satanic embellishments. «فلولا... تضرّعوا... زیّن لهم الشیطان»

Ayah

فَلَمَّا نَسُوا۟ مَا ذُكِّرُوا۟ بِهِۦ فَتَحْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ أَبْوَٰبَ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا فَرِحُوا۟ بِمَآ أُوتُوٓا۟ أَخَذْنَـٰهُم بَغْتَةً فَإِذَا هُم مُّبْلِسُونَ (٤٤)
So when they forgot what they had been admonished of, We opened for them the gates of all [good] things. When they became proud of what they were given, We seized them suddenly, whereat, behold, they were despondent. (44)

Key Points

«Mablisūn» (مبلسون) derives from «Iblās» (ابلاس), meaning sorrow and grief accompanied by despair. This is the state criminals experience when they find no answer in the court [of judgment]. [331] In the two preceding verses, He stated: We afflict people so that they may supplicate. In this verse, He states: Some people forget Allah even in moments of affliction. «Nasū mā dhukkirū...» (They forgot what they had been reminded of...) We read in the Quran: «Wa ‘asā an tuḥibbū shay’an wa huwa sharrun lakum» [332], perhaps you love a thing while it is evil for you.
