And Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «Idhā ra’ayta Subḥānahu yutābi‘u ‘alayka ni‘amahu wa anta ta‘ṣīhi faḥdharhu» [333] (If you see the Glorified One continuously bestowing His favors upon you while you disobey Him, then beware of Him). If you see that Allah continuously grants His blessings upon you, yet you commit sin, then be vigilant, for perhaps these favors do not lead to a happy end. The world and its benefits can be both a blessing (ni‘mah) and a retribution (niqmah) for man. In the verse under discussion, the world is considered a retribution. However, faith and piety bring the blessings of the heavens and the earth for their adherents, as stated in Ayah 96 of Surah Al-A'raf: «Wa law anna ahla al-qurā āmanū wa-ittaqaw lafataḥnā ‘alayhim barakātin mina as-samā’i wa-al-arḍ» (And if the people of the towns had believed and feared Allah, We would have opened upon them blessings from the heaven and the earth). Therefore, heedlessness closes the doors of goodness upon man. The Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: »Idhā ra’ayta Allāha yu‘ṭī ‘alā al-ma‘āṣī fa-inna dhālika istidrāj minhu« (If you see Allah granting [favors] despite sins, then that is Istidrāj (gradual enticement) from Him). If you observe that the world is favorable to sinners, do not rejoice, because this is Divine Istidrāj and His gradual destruction of them. [334] And then the Prophet recited this verse. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said in the interpretation (ta’wīl) of the verse: When the people abandoned the Wilāyah (guardianship) of Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام, though they had been commanded to adhere to it, «Falammā nasū...» (When they forgot...), We expanded their dominion in the world, «Fataḥnā ‘alayhim...» (We opened upon them...), until Al-Qā’im عليه السلام rises, «Ḥattā... akhadhnāhum baghtatan» (Until... We seized them suddenly). [335]

331) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 332) Al-Baqarah, 216. 333) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 25. 334) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān. 335) Tafsir Ayyashi.

Messages

1- Prosperity in life is not always a sign of mercy; sometimes it is a prelude to punishment. «Nasū» (They forgot) - «Fataḥnā» (We opened). 2- Granting respite to criminals and allowing transgressors to become engrossed is one of the Divine traditions. «Falammā nasū... fataḥnā» (So when they forgot... We opened). 3- Allah’s wrath follows the completion of the proof (iṭmām al-ḥujjah). «Nasū mā dhukkirū... akhadhnāhum» (They forgot what they had been reminded of... We seized them). 4- Hardships and expansions are in the hand of Allah. «Akhadhnā» (We seized) - «Fataḥnā» (We opened). 5- Death and Divine wrath come suddenly, so one must always be prepared. «Baghtatan» (Suddenly). 6- The joy of the hedonists will suddenly turn into a cry of despair. «Fariḥū» (They rejoiced) - «Mablisūn» (In despair).

Ayah

فَقُطِعَ دَابِرُ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ۚ وَٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (٤٥)
Thus the wrongdoing lot were rooted out, and all praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds. (45)

Key Points

| Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Whoever wishes that the oppressor remains, in reality, wishes that Allah be disobeyed and transgressed against. Indeed, Allah has praised Himself for destroying the oppressors. «فقطع... و الحمدللّه» [336]

336) Ma'ani al-Akhbar, p. 252.

Messages

| 1- Oppression is not lasting. «قطع» 2- Oppression affects even the lineage (of the oppressors). «دابر» 3- The extinction and destruction of the oppressors is certain. (Oppression uproots civilizations.) «فقطع دابر» Just as He says elsewhere: «فهل ترى لهم من باقیة» [337] 4- Ignoring the warnings of the prophets is oppression. «نسوا ما ذكروا... الّذین ظلموا» 5- Sometimes abundant prosperity is the ground for oppression. «فتحنا علیهم ابواب كلّ شى ء... الّذین ظلموا» 6- Throughout history, there have been nations of whom no trace remains. «فقطع دابر» 7- Destroying the oppressor is a commendable act. «والحمدللَّه» 8- When oppressors are destroyed, Allah must be thanked. «فقطع ... والحمدللَّه» 9- The destruction of the oppressors is a factor in the education (and nurturing) of others. «ربّ العالمین»

337) Al-Haqqah, 8.

Ayah

قُلْ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِنْ أَخَذَ ٱللَّهُ سَمْعَكُمْ وَأَبْصَـٰرَكُمْ وَخَتَمَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِكُم مَّنْ إِلَـٰهٌ غَيْرُ ٱللَّهِ يَأْتِيكُم بِهِ ۗ ٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ نُصَرِّفُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ ثُمَّ هُمْ يَصْدِفُونَ (٤٦)
Say, ‘Tell me, should Allah take away your hearing and your sight and set a seal on your hearts, which god other than Allah can bring it [back] to you?’ Look, how We paraphrase the signs variously; nevertheless they turn away. (46)

Key Points

| From the beginning of the Surah until this point, Allah has invited the opponents to reflect approximately ten times by posing questions. This is because reflection upon the blessings, and imagining and hypothesizing their change and transformation, is the foundation for knowing Allah (God-knowledge). If trees do not turn green, «لو نشاء لجعلناه حطاماً» [338] If waters become bitter and salty, «لو نشاء جعلناه اُجاجاً» [339] If waters sink into the earth, «إن أصبح ماءكم غوراً» [340] If night or day were perpetual, «إن جعل اللَّه علیكم اللیل سرمداً... ان جعل اللَّه علیكم النّهار سرمداً» [341] And if man's eyes, ears, and intellect cease to function, «إن اخذ اللَّه سمعكم و ابصاركم و...» [342] Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: If Allah takes away your guidance, who is there to guide you again? He then recited this Ayah. [343]

338) Al-Waqi'ah, 65. 339) Al-Waqi'ah, 70. 340) Al-Mulk, 30. 341) Al-Qasas, 71 to 72. 342) Al-An'am, 46. 343) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

| 1- One of the Qur'an's methods of propagation and education is appealing to the conscience through questioning. «أرأیتم ان اخذ اللَّه... مَن اله» 2- It is easy for Allah to take back the blessings He has given, so let us be mindful. «أخذ اللَّه سمعكم...» 3- Both creation belongs to Allah, and their moment-by-moment functionality is a blessing from the Lord. «أخذ اللَّه سمعكم» 4- The ear, the eye, and the intellect are tools for human recognition and among the most important Divine blessings. «سمعكم و ابصاركم... قلوبكم» 5- Imaginary deities have neither the power to create nor the power to restore lost blessings. «من اله غیر اللَّه یأتیكم» (Indeed, only He who can both grant a blessing and take it back is worthy of being questioned.) 6- Despite such diversity in argumentation, the polytheists' attention to other than Allah is astonishing. «اُنظر كیف...» 7- For stubborn people, every type of explanation is ineffective. «نُصرّف الآیات ثمّ هم یصدفون»

Ayah

قُلْ أَرَءَيْتَكُمْ إِنْ أَتَىٰكُمْ عَذَابُ ٱللَّهِ بَغْتَةً أَوْ جَهْرَةً هَلْ يُهْلَكُ إِلَّا ٱلْقَوْمُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ (٤۷)
Say, ‘Tell me, should Allah’s punishment overtake you suddenly or visibly, will anyone be destroyed except the wrongdoing lot?’ (47)

Key Points

Perhaps the meaning of Bagh-tah (suddenly) is during sleep at night, and the meaning of Jah-rah (openly) is during work in the day. Like the Ayah: «أتاها أمرنا لیلاً أونهاراً» [344]. Perhaps the meaning of Bagh-tah is punishments without prelude, and the intention of Jah-rah is punishments whose signs become apparent from the very first day, such as the dangerous clouds that descended upon the people of 'Ad. As we read elsewhere: «قالوا هذا عارض ممطرنا بل هو ما استعجلتم به ریح فیها عذاب ألیم» [345]

Question: This Ayah states that only the oppressors (the Zalimin) are destroyed by the Divine punishment, while another Ayah, «و اتّقوا فتنة لا تصیبنّ الّذین ظلموا منكم خاصّة» [346], describes the comprehensiveness of the trial (fitnah) and the flames of punishment affecting everyone, and warns against it. How can the meaning of these two Ayahs be reconciled?

Answer: It is stated in Tafsir Kabir by Fakhr Razi and Majma' al-Bayan that not every trial (fitnah) is destruction (halak). In bitter events, what reaches the oppressors is destruction and wrath, and what reaches the believers is a test and growth.

344) Yunus, 24; Tafsir Ruh al-Ma'ani. 345) Al-Ahqaf, 24; Tafsir Atayib al-Bayan. 346) Al-Anfal, 25.

Messages

1- The Prophet is commanded to question and inquire of them in order to awaken consciences. «قل أرایتكم»

2- Do not be deceived by your rebellion and the Divine respite; perhaps Allah's punishment will arrive suddenly. «بغتة»

3- When Allah's punishment comes, destruction is certain; neither they nor others have the power to defend against it. «عذاب اللَّه - یهلك»

4- Divine punishments are due to the people's oppression and are just. «یهلك... الظالمون»

5- Turning away from the call of the Prophets is oppression. «هم یصدفون - الظالمون»

Ayahs

وَمَا نُرْسِلُ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ إِلَّا مُبَشِّرِينَ وَمُنذِرِينَ ۖ فَمَنْ ءَامَنَ وَأَصْلَحَ فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ (٤۸)
We do not send the apostles except as bearers of good news and warners. As for those who are faithful and righteous, they will have no fear, nor will they grieve. (48)
وَٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا يَمَسُّهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْسُقُونَ (٤۹)
But as for those who deny Our signs, the punishment shall befall them because of the transgressions they used to commit. (49)

Key Points

| Question: Although numerous verses state regarding the friends of Allah (Awliya') that there is no fear upon them, "لا خوف علیهم" (la khawfun 'alayhim), why is fear of God mentioned as one of the characteristics of the friends of Allah, "انّا نخاف من ربّنا..." (Indeed, we fear our Lord...)? Answer: Firstly, sometimes a patient fears surgery and treatment, but the doctor assures him that there is no cause for worry or fear. The absence of cause for worry does not contradict the patient's own anxiety. Secondly: The meaning of "لاخوف علیهم" is that fear does not dominate them. (The particle 'عَلى' [‘alā] signifies superiority and dominance.) It does not mean that they have no fear whatsoever. And "یحزنون" (yahzanūn) means they do not have perpetual sorrow (the imperfect verb form symbolizes continuity), not that they are not worried even for a moment.

Messages

1- Only Allah determines the scope of the Prophets' mission. "و ما نرسل...الاّ مبشّرین..."

2- The responsibility of the Prophets is to give good news and warning, not to compel people. "الاّ مبشرین و منذرین"

3- The general method of the Prophets' work has been consistent. "مبشّرین و منذرین"

4- Guidance and education are based on two pillars: fear and hope, and punishment and reward. "مبشّرین و منذرین"

5- Faith (Iman) and righteous deeds (Amal Salih) are inseparable, and deeds without faith are ineffective. "آمن و اصلح"

6- The actions of the believer must always be directed toward reform and rectification. "آمن و اصلح"

7- Being righteous (Salih) is not enough; being a reformer (Muslih) is necessary. "أصلح"

8- Faith and action insure man against spiritual illnesses such as fear and sorrow. "آمن و اصلح فلاخوف علیهم ولاهم یحزنون" (Mental health is achieved under the shadow of faith and righteous deeds.)

9- The achievement of the prophetic society is a balanced, tranquil, and secure system. "فمن آمن و اصلح فلاخوف..."

10- Man is inherently desirous of tranquility and security. "فلا خوف علیهم و لا هم یحزنون"

11- Persistence in transgression (Fisq) paves the way for divine punishment. "یمسّهم العذاب بما كانوا یفسقون"

Ayah

قُل لَّآ أَقُولُ لَكُمْ عِندِى خَزَآئِنُ ٱللَّهِ وَلَآ أَعْلَمُ ٱلْغَيْبَ وَلَآ أَقُولُ لَكُمْ إِنِّى مَلَكٌ ۖ إِنْ أَتَّبِعُ إِلَّا مَا يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَىَّ ۚ قُلْ هَلْ يَسْتَوِى ٱلْأَعْمَىٰ وَٱلْبَصِيرُ ۚ أَفَلَا تَتَفَكَّرُونَ (٥٠)
Say, ‘I do not say to you that I possess the treasuries of Allah, nor do I know the Unseen, nor do I say to you that I am an angel. I follow only what is revealed to me.’ Say, ‘Are the blind one and the seer equal? So do you not reflect?’ (50)

Key Points

It seems that the disbelieving people imagined that only someone who possessed the Divine treasures could be a prophet, or they expected him to solve all their problems through the path of the knowledge of the unseen (Ghayb). The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is commanded in this Ayah to explicitly stop their undue expectations and false notions, and to state the limits of His duties, saying: My miracles are also within the framework of Divine revelation, not your desires. And know that I will present a miracle wherever I am commanded by revelation, not wherever and whatever you wish. In this Ayah, the Prophet's independent knowledge of the unseen is negated, but sometimes knowledge of the unseen has been granted to the Prophet. Allah has repeatedly stated to the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله regarding the histories of Prophet Yusuf (Joseph), Maryam (Mary), Nuh (Noah), and others: «ذلك من انباءالغیب نوحیه الیك» (That is from the news of the unseen which We reveal to you). These stories are information about the unseen that We give to you. At the end of Surah Al-Jinn, He also stated: Allah possesses the knowledge of the unseen and does not grant it except to His chosen guardians (Awliya'). Thus, the knowledge of the unseen belongs exclusively to Allah, and if He wills, He places a portion of it at the disposal of His guardians. The approach of all the Prophets towards people has been consistent. Prophet Nuh (Noah) also spoke these same words to his people. [347]

347) Hud, 31.

Messages

1- The honesty of the Prophets is such that if they lack a certain power, they announce it to the people. «قل لا أقول»

2- Among the duties of the Prophets is combating superstition and false characterization. «لااقول... لا اعلم»

3- Do not have undue expectations of the Prophets. «لا اقول لكم عندى خزائن اللَّه»

4- The life, goals, and method of work of the leader must be stated clearly and explicitly for the people. «قل لا اقول...»

5- Do not let people imagine you are more than you are. (If personalities negate false titles attributed to themselves, exaggerations (ghuluw) and deviations are prevented). «لا اقول لكم عندى خزائن اللَّه...»

6- The Prophets did not work through money, threats, or temptation, so that others would not gather around them out of fear or greed, imagining that if they were with the Prophet, their problems would be solved through the knowledge of the unseen or the Divine treasury. «قل لا اقول...»

7- For personal life or governing, the Prophet acts through ordinary means like others and does not use the knowledge of the unseen or the Divine treasury for personal benefit, although it is necessary for him to use them to prove his prophethood. «لا اعلم الغیب»

8- Although the Prophet does not possess the Divine treasures and the knowledge of the unseen, he must be followed because "revelation" (wahy) comes to him. «یوحى الىّ»

9- The Prophet's work is based neither on imagination and personal preference, nor on social desires or environmental influence, but solely on following the revelation. «ان اتّبع الاّ مایوحى الى»

10- The conduct and speech of the Prophets are proof (hujjah) for us, because they are based on revelation. «ان اتّبع الا ما یوحى الى»

11- Following the Prophets is insight (basirah), and turning away from them is blindness. «قل هل یستوى الاعمى و البصیر»

12- Correct thinking compels a person to follow the Prophets and discard excuses and expectations. «أفلا تتفكّرون»

Ayah

وَأَنذِرْ بِهِ ٱلَّذِينَ يَخَافُونَ أَن يُحْشَرُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ ۙ لَيْسَ لَهُم مِّن دُونِهِۦ وَلِىٌّ وَلَا شَفِيعٌ لَّعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ (٥۱)
And warn by its means those who fear being mustered toward their Lord, besides whom they shall have neither any guardian nor any intercessor, so that they may be Godwary. (51)

Key Points

In the Quran, Allah has repeatedly told the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله that his warning only takes effect in the hearts of those who fear God, such as: «انّما تنذر من اتّبع الذّكر و خَشِى الرّحمن» [348] and «انّما تنذر الّذین یخشون ربّهم بالغیب» [349].

348) Ya Sin, 11. 349) Fatir, 18.

Messages

1- The readiness of individuals is a condition for the effectiveness of the guidance provided by the prophets. «الّذین یخافون»

2- Belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is the key to piety (Taqwa). «یخافون ان یحشروا ... لعلّهم یتّقون»

Ayah

وَلَا تَطْرُدِ ٱلَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ رَبَّهُم بِٱلْغَدَوٰةِ وَٱلْعَشِىِّ يُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَهُۥ ۖ مَا عَلَيْكَ مِنْ حِسَابِهِم مِّن شَىْءٍ وَمَا مِنْ حِسَابِكَ عَلَيْهِم مِّن شَىْءٍ فَتَطْرُدَهُمْ فَتَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (٥۲)
Do not drive away those who supplicate their Lord morning and evening desiring His face. Neither are you accountable for them in any way, nor are they accountable for you in any way, so that you may drive them away and thus become one of the wrongdoers. (52)

Key Points

Regarding the occasion of the revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of this Ayah, it is narrated that a group of wealthy disbelievers, seeing that the poor, such as Ammar, Bilal, Khabbab, and others, had gathered around the Prophet, suggested that he should expel them so that the wealthy might gather around him. According to Tafsir al-Manar, the second Caliph suggested: "Let us accept the proposal as a test." The above Ayah was then revealed. A similar point is also mentioned in Surah Al-Kahf, Ayah 28. In Tafsir Qurtubi, it is stated: After the revelation of this Ayah, the Prophet of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله would not rise from the gathering of the poor until the poor themselves rose first. The intention behind calling upon Allah morning and evening might be the daily prayers. [350]

350) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Preserving sincere, poor, and striving forces is more important than attracting wealthy disbelievers. «لا تطرد» (Do not drive away)

2- The end does not justify the means. In order to attract the leaders of disbelief, one must not insult the existing Muslims. «لا تطرد»

3- Islam is a school that fights discrimination, racism, privilege-seeking, and extortion. (Considering the occasion of revelation)

4- Most followers of the Prophets have been the barefooted (the poor/dispossessed). (Considering the occasion of revelation)

5- Fault-finders, if they cannot criticize the leader or the school of thought, will criticize the followers and their economic status. (Considering the occasion of revelation)

6- No privilege equals "faith" (Iman). «یریدون وجهه» (They desire His Face/Presence)

7- The criterion is the current state of individuals. If the poor believers had previous faults, their account is with Allah. «ما علیك من حسابهم من شى ء» (You are not accountable for them in any way)

8- Everyone's account is with Allah; even the Noble Messenger is not responsible for the choices and actions of others, «ما علیك من حسابهم من شى ء», and He Himself also has an account (to render). «و ما من حسابك علیهم من شى ء» (And you are not accountable for them in any way)

9- Expelling the sincere barefooted and deprived is oppression (Dhulm). «فتكون من الظالمین» (Lest you become one of the wrongdoers)

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ فَتَنَّا بَعْضَهُم بِبَعْضٍ لِّيَقُولُوٓا۟ أَهَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ مَنَّ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِم مِّنۢ بَيْنِنَآ ۗ أَلَيْسَ ٱللَّهُ بِأَعْلَمَ بِٱلشَّـٰكِرِينَ (٥۳)
Thus do We test them by means of one another so that they should say, ‘Are these the ones whom Allah has favoured from among us?!’ Does not Allah know best the grateful?! (53)

Key Points

The Quran has repeatedly addressed and condemned the high ambitions and excessive expectations of the wealthy. They expected that the revelation (Wahy) and the Quran should be sent down upon them, including [their statements]: «أءُلقى الذّكر علیه من بیننا» [351] Was the Reminder revealed to him from among us?! «لولا نُزّل هذا القرآن على رجل من القریتین عظیم» [352] Why was this Quran not sent down upon a great man from the two towns?! The true believer is grateful for the blessing of faith. A person complained to Imam Kadhim علیه السلام about his poverty. The Imam said: Who, in your opinion, is the wealthiest person? He replied: Harun al-Rashid. He asked: Would you be willing to give up your faith and take his wealth? He said: No. He said: Then you are wealthier, because you possess something that you are unwilling to exchange for his wealth. [353]

351) Qamar, 25. 352) Zukhruf, 31. 353) Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Social differences are sometimes a means of trial, allowing characteristics to blossom and grow. The wealthy are tested by the poor. «فَتنّا بعضهم ببعض»

2- The wealthy belittle the poor and consider themselves valuable. «أهولاء...من بیننا»

3- The faithful poor are the chosen ones of Allah. «مَنَّ اللّه علیهم»

4- The insults of the disbelievers are answered by divine kindness and affection shown to the believers. «أهؤلاء... أعلم بالشاكرین»

5- The divine favor (mann) bestowed upon the poor is the result of their gratitude (shukr). «مَنَّ اللّه... بالشّاكرین»

6- Allah acts according to His wisdom, not according to people's expectations. «ألیس اللّه بأعلم بالشّاكرین»

7- The Prophets are clear examples of the grateful (shakirin). «بالشّاكرین» (The poor who have accepted the guidance of the Prophets are also grateful for the blessing of guidance.) «اعلم بالشّاكرین»

Ayahs

وَإِذَا جَآءَكَ ٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا فَقُلْ سَلَـٰمٌ عَلَيْكُمْ ۖ كَتَبَ رَبُّكُمْ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِهِ ٱلرَّحْمَةَ ۖ أَنَّهُۥ مَنْ عَمِلَ مِنكُمْ سُوٓءًۢا بِجَهَـٰلَةٍ ثُمَّ تَابَ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ وَأَصْلَحَ فَأَنَّهُۥ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (٥٤)
When those who have faith in Our signs come to you, say, ‘Peace to you! Your Lord has made mercy incumbent upon Himself: whoever of you commits an evil [deed] out of ignorance and then repents after that and reforms, then He is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful.’ (54)
وَكَذَٰلِكَ نُفَصِّلُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ وَلِتَسْتَبِينَ سَبِيلُ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ (٥٥)
Thus do We elaborate the signs, so that the way of the guilty may be exposed. (55)

Key Points

Regarding the occasion of revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of the Ayah, a group of sinners came to the Prophet and said: We have committed many transgressions. The Prophet remained silent. This Ayah was then revealed. In this Surah, Allah has stated the phrase: «كتب على نفسه الرحمة» (He has prescribed mercy upon Himself) twice. One is to encourage the believers in this world (this Ayah), and the other is in Ayah 12, which relates to the Resurrection (Ma'ad): «كتب على نفسه الرّحمة لیجمعنّكم» (He has prescribed mercy upon Himself, that He will surely gather you). Ignorance (Jahalah) is sometimes contrasted with knowledge ('Ilm) and sometimes with intellect ('Aql). In Ayah 54, it is contrasted with intellect. This means they were led to sin out of ignorance and lack of wisdom, not because they lacked knowledge [of the prohibition].

Messages

1- The faith of the visitor to the Prophet of Allah is a condition for receiving divine grace. «و اذا جاءك الّذین یؤمنون بآیاتنا فقل...»

2- Visiting the Prophet is a path to receiving Allah's grace. «جاءك... فقل سلام علیكم كتب»

3- The relationship between the leader and the people is based on familiarity and affection. «اذا جاءك... فقل سلام علیكم»

4- The best expression of greeting is «سلام علیكم» (Peace be upon you). «فقل سلام علیكم»

5- Compensate for the humiliation of the believers. (In Ayah 53, the arrogant ones said mockingly: «اَهؤلاء...» but in Ayah 54, Allah compensates for that humiliation and commands His Prophet to greet the believers.) «فقل سلام علیكم»

6- Salam (Peace) is the motto of Islam. The great also greets the small. «فقل سلام علیكم»

7- The educator and preacher must give people dignity through affectionate interaction. «سلام علیكم»

8- If a sin is not committed out of obstinacy or arrogance, it is forgivable. (Ignorance is an acceptable excuse for forgiving and pardoning wrongdoers.) «من عمل منكم سوءً بجهالة»

9- Allah has made mercy obligatory upon Himself, but the condition for receiving it is seeking forgiveness and repentance (Tawbah). «كتب ... ثمّ تاب»

10- Repentance is not just a word; it also requires resolve and reform. «تاب و أصلح»

11- The repentance of a believer is certainly accepted. «عمل منكم» The beginning of repentance is after the sin. «من بعده»

12- Divine pardon is accompanied by mercy. «غفور رحیم» (The sinner should not despair.)

13- Clarifying the characteristics of criminals, the path of wrongdoers, and those who plot, is among the goals of the faith. «لتستبین سبیل المجرمین»

Ayah

قُلْ إِنِّى نُهِيتُ أَنْ أَعْبُدَ ٱلَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ قُل لَّآ أَتَّبِعُ أَهْوَآءَكُمْ ۙ قَدْ ضَلَلْتُ إِذًا وَمَآ أَنَا۠ مِنَ ٱلْمُهْتَدِينَ (٥٦)
Say, ‘I have been forbidden to worship those whom you invoke besides Allah.’ Say, ‘I do not follow your desires, for then I will have gone astray, and I will not be among the [rightly] guided.’ (56)

Messages

1- In response to the inappropriate desires of the polytheists regarding the acceptance of idols and their method, one must give a clear negative answer. «نُهیتُ، لا أتّبع، ضللت» (Yes, polytheism is not permissible in any form.)

2- The Prophet's صلى الله علیه وآله interactions and stances originate from revelation. «قل، قل»

3- Disavowal of polytheism is part of Islam. «نُهیتُ أن أعبد الّذین تدعون من دون اللّه»

4- The root of polytheism is following desires (self-worship). «لا أتّبع أهوائكم»

5- The preacher should not seek to satisfy people's whims. «لا أتّبع أهوائكم»

6- Following desires destroys the potential for guidance within a person. «لاأتّبع اهوائكم... ما أنا من المهتدین»

Ayah

قُلْ إِنِّى عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّى وَكَذَّبْتُم بِهِۦ ۚ مَا عِندِى مَا تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ بِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنِ ٱلْحُكْمُ إِلَّا لِلَّهِ ۖ يَقُصُّ ٱلْحَقَّ ۖ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ ٱلْفَـٰصِلِينَ (٥۷)
Say, ‘Indeed I stand on a manifest proof from my Lord and you have denied it. What you seek to hasten is not up to me. Judgement belongs only to Allah; He expounds the truth and He is the best of judges.’ (57)

Key Points

Bayyinah (بَيِّنَة) derives from the word baynunah (بَيْنُونَة) (separation/distinction), and it refers to a proof that completely and clearly separates truth from falsehood. The proofs and miracles of the Prophets were neither burdensome nor ambiguous; everyone understood them, and if they were not obstinate, they would accept them wholeheartedly. For this reason, the Prophets introduced themselves as possessing Bayyinah. The disbelievers would say: If you are truthful, why does Allah's wrath not descend upon us? (tasta‘jilūna bihi - you seek to hasten it). This is similar to the verse where they said: If this matter is true, O God, rain stones upon us, "فَأَمْطِرْ عَلَيْنَا حِجَارَةً مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ" [354] Seeking to hasten (isti‘jāl) and demanding punishment also occurred among other nations, and they would say to Prophets Hud, Salih, and Nuh: If you are truthful, bring the promised punishment quickly upon us. "فَآتِنَا بِمَا تَعِدُنَا" [355]

354) Al-Anfal, 32. 355) Al-A'raf, 70 and 77; Hud, 32.

Messages

1- The invitation of the Prophets is based on Bayyinah (clear proof), not on imagination or blind imitation. "عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَةٍ"

2- The granting of Bayyinah to the Prophet is a reflection of Allah's Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). "بَيِّنَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّي"

3- Prophets must possess Divine Bayyinah, and not act according to the daily demands of the people. "بَيِّنَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّي" (The Quran is the Bayyinah, the clear proof, and the witness to the truthfulness of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله).

4- The Prophet of Islam has complete mastery over the Quran, which is His Bayyinah. ("عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَةٍ" signifies superiority and mastery over the concepts of the Quran).

5- The disbelievers deny the Prophet's Bayyinah, yet they expect Him to follow their carnal desires. "لَا أَتَّبِعُ أَهْوَاءَكُمْ" - "كَذَّبْتُم بِهِ"

6- Speak clearly with those who make excuses. "مَا عِندِي"

7- The Prophet is merely a messenger accompanied by logic and Bayyinah, and the system of existence is in the hands of Allah. (By demanding the swift descent of Divine wrath from the Prophet, do not seek your own destruction or the annihilation of existence). "مَا تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ بِهِ"

8- The Ayah is both a threat to the disbelievers and a consolation to the Prophet. "وَهُوَ خَيْرُ الْفَاصِلِينَ"

Ayah

قُل لَّوْ أَنَّ عِندِى مَا تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ بِهِۦ لَقُضِىَ ٱلْأَمْرُ بَيْنِى وَبَيْنَكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (٥۸)
Say, ‘If what you seek to hasten were with me, the matter would surely have been decided between you and me, and Allah knows best the wrongdoers.’ (58)

Messages

1- The haste of people does not change the wisdom of Allah. «تستعجلون... واللَّه اعلم»

2- Punishments are in the hand of Allah, but if He were to hasten His punishment at the request of the disbelievers, no one would remain. «لقضى الامر» As stated in Ayah 11 of Surah Yunus: «ولو یُعجّل اللّه للنّاس الشّر استعجالهم بالخیر لقضى الیهم أجلهم»

3- Allah grants respite to the oppressors according to His wisdom and tradition (Sunnah). «قل لو ان عندى ماتستعجلون به لقضى... واللَّه اعلم بالظالمین»

4- The delay in divine wrath and punishment should not cause the disbelievers to imagine that their disbelief has been forgotten. «واللَّه اعلم بالظالمین»

5- Turning away from faith and hastening the punishment is oppression (zulm). «بالظالمین»

Ayah

۞ وَعِندَهُۥ مَفَاتِحُ ٱلْغَيْبِ لَا يَعْلَمُهَآ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا فِى ٱلْبَرِّ وَٱلْبَحْرِ ۚ وَمَا تَسْقُطُ مِن وَرَقَةٍ إِلَّا يَعْلَمُهَا وَلَا حَبَّةٍ فِى ظُلُمَـٰتِ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا رَطْبٍ وَلَا يَابِسٍ إِلَّا فِى كِتَـٰبٍ مُّبِينٍ (٥۹)
With Him are the treasures of the Unseen; no one knows them except Him. He knows whatever there is in land and sea. No leaf falls without His knowing it, nor is there a grain in the darkness of the earth, nor anything fresh or withered but it is in a manifest Book. (59)

Key Points

The word mafātih (مفاتح) is either the plural of miftah (مِفتَح), meaning treasure or treasury, or the plural of miftāḥ (مِفتاح), meaning key. However, the first meaning is more appropriate. [356] The phrase "wet and dry" is a metaphor for all things that stand in opposition to each other, such as death and life, health and sickness, poverty and wealth, good and evil, abstract and material. This concept is also expressed in the verse: «و كلّ شى ء أحصیناه فى امامٍ مُبین» [357]. Perhaps the intent behind the falling of leaves is downward movements, and the intent behind the seeds growing underground is upward movements. [358]

356) Tafsir al-Mizan. 357) Yasin, 12. 358) Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Qur'an.

Messages

1- The reason the hasty punishment requested by the disbelievers does not arrive is Allah's knowledge of the secrets of existence. «ما تستعجلون... و عنده مفاتح الغیب»

2- Divine decrees are based on His awareness of the unseen and the manifest. «اِنِ الحُكم الاّ للّه - و عنده مفاتح الغیب»

3- The scope of the knowledge of the unseen is broader than ordinary sciences. (Because the Qur'an uses an exclusive expression regarding it.) «لا یعلمها الاّ هو»

4- Contrary to those who say that Allah only knows generalities, Allah is knowledgeable of everything and aware of all the details of existence. Therefore, we must be mindful of our actions. «لا تسقط من ورقة...»

5- Apart from Allah, no one possesses knowledge of the unseen independently. «و عنده مفاتح الغیب لایعلمها الاّ هو»

6- There is a center for information in the universe. «فى كتاب مبین»

7- The system of existence has been designed according to a predetermined plan. «كتاب مبین»

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى يَتَوَفَّىٰكُم بِٱلَّيْلِ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا جَرَحْتُم بِٱلنَّهَارِ ثُمَّ يَبْعَثُكُمْ فِيهِ لِيُقْضَىٰٓ أَجَلٌ مُّسَمًّى ۖ ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ ثُمَّ يُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (٦٠)
It is He who takes your souls by night, and He knows what you do by day, then He reanimates you therein so that a specified term may be completed. Then to Him will be your return, whereat He will inform you concerning what you used to do. (60)

Key Points

The word "جَرَحتم" (jarah-tum) is derived from "جارحة" (jārihah), meaning a limb or organ—a limb with which work and earning are done—and subsequently, it has been applied to the work and action itself. Although Allah takes the human soul during sleep, He says: We take you (plural). This indicates that the human soul is the entire reality of the person.

Messages

1- Sleep is a temporary death, and every awakening is a type of resurrection. "يتوفاكم" (He takes you), "يبعثكم" (He raises you).

2- The natural law is that the night is for sleep and the day is for work. "يتوفّاكم بالليل" (He takes you by night), "جرحتم بالنّهار" (you earn by day).

3- Every day of life is a new opportunity and a new resurrection. "يبعثكم" (He raises you).

4- Our life has a specific date, plan, and determined duration. "أجل مسمّى" (an appointed term).

5- We must prepare ourselves for accountability on the Day of Judgment. "إليه مرجعكم" (to Him is your return).

6- Although Allah knows our evil deeds, He still grants us respite and returns the taken soul to the body. "يعلم ما جرحتم بالنّهار ثمّ يبعثكم" (He knows what you earn by day, then He raises you).

7- The Day of Judgment is the day when the reality of deeds will be made clear. "ينبّئكم بما كنتم تعملون" (He will inform you of what you used to do).

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلْقَاهِرُ فَوْقَ عِبَادِهِۦ ۖ وَيُرْسِلُ عَلَيْكُمْ حَفَظَةً حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءَ أَحَدَكُمُ ٱلْمَوْتُ تَوَفَّتْهُ رُسُلُنَا وَهُمْ لَا يُفَرِّطُونَ (٦۱)
He is the All-dominant over His servants, and He sends guards to [protect] you. When death approaches anyone of you, Our messengers take him away and they do not neglect [their duty]. (61)

Key Points

Taking the soul at night and returning it during the day is one of the examples of Allah's dominance over man. The issue of seizing the soul (qabḍ ar-rūḥ) is attributed in the Quran both to Allah, "اللّه یتوفّى الانفس" (Allah takes the souls), and to the Angel of Death (Malak al-Mawt), and to the angels. This difference in expression might be because the angels take the souls of ordinary people, while the Angel of Death, who is a superior angel, takes the souls of prominent individuals, and His Holy Essence takes the souls of the friends of Allah (Awliyā' Allāh). And perhaps it is because the angels first take the souls of people, then hand them over to the Angel of Death, and then the Angel of Death (Azrael) hands the taken souls over to Allah. For this reason, the taking of people's souls is considered the work of the angels, the work of the Angel of Death, and the work of Allah. It is possible that the intent behind the guardian angels (al-malā’ikah al-ḥāfiẓah) are those who watch over and protect man from accidents, "له معقّ [۳۵۹] بات من بین یدیه و من خلفه یحفظونه من امراللَّه" (He has successive [angels] before him and behind him, protecting him by the command of Allah) [360]. And it is possible that the intent behind the guardians (ḥafaẓah) are the angels commissioned to record and register human deeds. "رسلنا لدیهم یكتبون" (Our messengers are with them, recording) [361]. Belief in the angels' supervision of man is a factor for thanking Allah, just as belief in the recording of deeds by the angels is a factor for modesty (ḥayā’) and piety (taqwā).

359) Tafsir al-Mizan. 360) Ra'd, 11. 361) Zukhruf, 80.

Messages

1- Allah possesses complete power and dominance, and if He has left us free, it is due to a respite granted out of His grace. "و هو القاهر..." (And He is the Dominant...)

2- Allah's dominance over His servants is such that no one has the power to resist. "و هو القاهر فوق عباده" (And He is the Dominant above His servants).

3- In the system of creation, various types of supervision exist. "حفظة" (Guardians).

4- The sending of guardian angels is continuous. "یُرسل" (The imperfect verb indicates permanence and continuity).

5- Every group of angels has a specific mission. "حفظة - توفّته" (Guardians - take his soul).

6- Several angels attend to seize the soul of one person. "اذا جاء احدكم الموت توفّته رسلنا" (When death comes to one of you, Our messengers take his soul).

7- The angels do not neglect their mission and are infallible (ma‘ṣūm). "لایفرّطون" (They do not neglect), just as it is stated in another Ayah: "لا یَعصون اللّه ما أمرهم" (They do not disobey Allah in what He commands them) [362].

362) Taḥrīm, 6.

Ayah

ثُمَّ رُدُّوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ مَوْلَىٰهُمُ ٱلْحَقِّ ۚ أَلَا لَهُ ٱلْحُكْمُ وَهُوَ أَسْرَعُ ٱلْحَـٰسِبِينَ (٦۲)
Then they are returned to Allah, their real master. Look! All judgement belongs to Him, and He is the swiftest of reckoners. (62)

Key Points

It is narrated in traditions (Riwāyāt) that Allah performs the reckoning (Hisāb) of His servants in the time it takes to milk a sheep, and in a very short period. [363] Imam Ali عليه السلام was asked: «كیف یُحاسِبُ اللّه الخلقَ على كثرتهم؟ فقال: كما یَرزقُهم على كثرتهم. فقیل: كیف یُحاسِبهم و لایرونه؟ فقال: كما یَرزقهم و لایرونه» [364] How does Allah attend to the reckoning of His numerous creatures? The Imam said: Just as He provides sustenance for them. It was asked: How does He perform the reckoning while they do not see Him? He said: Just as He provides their sustenance, yet they do not see Him. Question: Given all these verses (Āyāt) and traditions (Riwāyāt) regarding Allah's swift reckoning, why is the Day of Judgment long? Answer: The length of the Day of Judgment is a form of punishment, not due to the pressure of work or inability to calculate swiftly. [365] (Mankind, who is Allah's creation, performs the greatest calculations in the world today using computers in less than a minute, for example, examining and calculating warehouses, their goods, and related financial matters.)

363) Tafsīr Majma‘ al-Bayān and Nūr al-Thaqalayn. 364) Nahj al-Balāghah, Saying 300. 365) Tafsīr Atyab al-Bayān.

Messages

1- The return of all is towards Allah, and He is the sole Judge of the Day of Judgment. «ردّوا الى اللَّه... له الحكم»

2- The true Master (Mawlā) is the One in whose hand rests creation, supervision, sleep and wakefulness, death and resurrection, judgment, and reckoning. «الى اللَّه مولاهم الحقّ»

3- All masters (Mawlās) are either false or metaphorical; the true Master is Allah alone. «اللَّه مولاهم الحقّ» And the guardianship (Wilāyah) of the prophets and the saints is a reflection of the Divine Guardianship.

4- The speed of Allah's reckoning is faster than any type or means of calculation. «و هو أسرع الحاسبین»

5- Swiftness in reckoning and attending to affairs is a Divine value and method. «و هو أسرع الحاسبین»

Ayahs

قُلْ مَن يُنَجِّيكُم مِّن ظُلُمَـٰتِ ٱلْبَرِّ وَٱلْبَحْرِ تَدْعُونَهُۥ تَضَرُّعًا وَخُفْيَةً لَّئِنْ أَنجَىٰنَا مِنْ هَـٰذِهِۦ لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلشَّـٰكِرِينَ (٦۳)
Say, ‘Who delivers you from the darkness of land and sea, [when] You invoke Him suppliantly and secretly: ‘‘If He delivers us from this, we will surely be among the grateful’’?’ (63)
قُلِ ٱللَّهُ يُنَجِّيكُم مِّنْهَا وَمِن كُلِّ كَرْبٍ ثُمَّ أَنتُمْ تُشْرِكُونَ (٦٤)
Say, ‘It is Allah who delivers you from them and from every agony, [but] then you ascribe partners [to Him].’ (64)

Key Points

The word taḍarruʿ (تَضرُّع) means open supplication (du'a), and the word khufyah (خُفیة) means hidden supplication. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: The best supplication is the one made in a low voice and hidden (khufyah). He criticized a group who were supplicating loudly and said: Allah is All-Hearing and Near. [366] It is also mentioned in Ayah 12 of Surah Yunus: When a person is in danger, he remembers Allah and calls upon Him, but «فلمّا كشفنا عنه ضرّه مَرّ كأن لم یَدْعُنا الى ضُرٍّ مَسَّه» (But when We removed his distress from him, he passed on as if he had never called upon Us regarding the distress that afflicted him). As soon as his problem is solved, he lowers his head and walks away, as if he had never called upon Us at all. Hardships create four states for man: feeling need, taḍarruʿ (humble supplication), sincerity (ikhlas), and commitment to gratitude. Yes, hardships, difficulties, and the severing of material means cause the spirit of seeking God to flourish, and in problems, man sees the Hand of Allah.

366) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- One of the methods of propagation and preaching is appealing to people's conscience. «مَن یُنَجّیكم»

2- Reminding and drawing man's attention to his sincerity in supplication during hardships is one of the best methods of knowing Allah. «تدعونه تضرّعاً و خفیة»

3- In difficulties and hardships, man abandons disbelief (kufr) and polytheism (shirk) and becomes a monotheist (muwaḥḥid). «تدعونه تضرّعاً و خفیة»

4- For everyone, their own problem is the greatest problem. «أنجانا مِن هذه»

5- Man is unfaithful to his commitments before Allah. «ثمّ أنتم تشركون»

6- The worst type of ingratitude is polytheism (shirk). «لنكوننّ من الشاكرین... ثمّ انتم تشركون»

7- Ease and the feeling of release and self-sufficiency are the grounds for heedlessness toward Allah and associating partners with Him. «قل اللَّه ینجّیكم منها و من كلّ كرب ثمّ انتم تُشركون»

Ayahs

قُلْ هُوَ ٱلْقَادِرُ عَلَىٰٓ أَن يَبْعَثَ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابًا مِّن فَوْقِكُمْ أَوْ مِن تَحْتِ أَرْجُلِكُمْ أَوْ يَلْبِسَكُمْ شِيَعًا وَيُذِيقَ بَعْضَكُم بَأْسَ بَعْضٍ ۗ ٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ نُصَرِّفُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَفْقَهُونَ (٦٥)
Say, ‘He is able to send upon you a punishment from above you or from under your feet, or confound you as [hostile] factions, and make you taste one another’s violence.’ Look, how We paraphrase the signs variously so that they may understand! (65)
وَكَذَّبَ بِهِۦ قَوْمُكَ وَهُوَ ٱلْحَقُّ ۚ قُل لَّسْتُ عَلَيْكُم بِوَكِيلٍ (٦٦)
Your people have denied it, though it is the truth. Say, ‘It is not my business to watch over you.’ (66)

Key Points

Labs (لَبْس) means mixing, and Shiya' (شِیَع) is the plural of Shi'ah (شیعه), meaning group. In the previous Ayah, Allah's saving power was discussed; here, His power of wrath and punishment (Azaab) is mentioned. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The meaning of the heavenly punishment is smoke and a cry (Sayhah), and the meaning of the earthly punishment is sinking into the earth, and the meaning of "یَلبِسَكم شِیَعا" (He covers you with groups) is division in religion, slander, and killing one another. [367] Perhaps the Imam was stating some examples and not all instances, because, for example, the punishment from above includes the oppression of the tyrants (Taghuts) who rule over the people, and the punishment from below also includes the wrath that follows the people's disobedience to the rightful leader who is from the people, among the people, and on their level.

Various expressions in the Quran, addressed to the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, convey the meaning that the Prophet is responsible for conveying Allah's religion, not for compelling people to believe. Including: «ما جعلناك علیهم حفیظا» [368], «لستَ علیهم بمصیطر» [369], «ما أنت علیهم بجبّار» [370], «ما على الرّسول الاّ البَلاغ» [371], «أفانتَ تكره النّاس» [372], «لااكراه فى الدّین» [373], «اِن أنت الاّ نذیر» [374] and «قل لستُ علیكم بوكیل».

367) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn. 368) Al-An'am, 107. 369) Al-Ghashiyah, 22. 370) Qaf, 45. 371) Al-Ma'idah, 99. 372) Yunus, 99. 373) Al-Baqarah, 256. 374) Fatir, 23.

Messages

1- One of the duties of the Prophet is to remind people of Allah's unparalleled power. «قل هو القادر»

2- Polytheism (Shirk) paves the way for receiving various types of punishments. «ثم انتم تشركون قل هو القادر...»

3- Allah is both the Savior and the Punisher. Whoever knowingly turns to other than Allah must await punishment. «قل هوالقادر على أن یبعث ...»

4- Division and fragmentation are among the divine punishments and are mentioned alongside the heavenly and earthly punishments. «یلبسكم شیعاً»

5- Sometimes people are punished and chastised by means of other people and by each other's hands. «یذیق بعضكم بأس بعض»

6- Religious preachers must utilize various methods for guiding people. «اُنظر كیف نصرّف الآیات»

7- If we present a concept in diverse forms, we should hope for greater impact. «نُصرّف الآیات لعلّهم یفقهون»

8- Since your path is the truth (Haqq), do not worry about people's denial. «و هو الحقّ»

9- You are responsible for the duty, but not the guarantor of the result. «لستَ علیكم بوكیل»

Ayah

لِّكُلِّ نَبَإٍ مُّسْتَقَرٌّ ۚ وَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ (٦۷)
For every prophecy there is a [preordained] setting, and soon you will know. (67)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, it was stated: Allah has the power to send down punishment upon you from every direction. This Ayah says: If punishment has not descended upon the stubborn disbelievers, do not rush, because there is an appointed time for the realization of every prophecy.

Messages

1- The realization of all divine prophecies and plans is certain, based on wisdom and a fixed schedule. (No event is without a plan, purpose, or accidental.) «لكلّ نباءٍ مستقرّ»

2- Although you are not compelled to believe, you should think about the consequence and outcome of your own actions. «و سوف تعلمون»

3- Do not judge quickly, and do not assume that the divine granting of respite is a sign of Allah's neglect of you. «سوف تعلمون»

Ayah

وَإِذَا رَأَيْتَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَخُوضُونَ فِىٓ ءَايَـٰتِنَا فَأَعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ حَتَّىٰ يَخُوضُوا۟ فِى حَدِيثٍ غَيْرِهِۦ ۚ وَإِمَّا يُنسِيَنَّكَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ فَلَا تَقْعُدْ بَعْدَ ٱلذِّكْرَىٰ مَعَ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (٦۸)
When you see those who gossip impiously about Our signs, avoid them until they engage in some other discourse; but if Satan makes you forget, then, after remembering, do not sit with the wrongdoing lot. (68)

Key Points

Khawḍ (خوض) means delving into and discussing with the intention of mockery and contempt, not for understanding and grasping the truth. [375] Ba‘da al-Dhikrā (بعد الذّكرى) may mean reminding others, or it may mean the person him/herself becoming mindful.

Question: The Divine Prophets are infallible (ma‘ṣūm), so how does the āyah speak of Satan's influence on the Prophet? (Yunsiyannaka al-Shayṭān - ینسینّك الشیطان)

Answer: Satan's effect in causing forgetfulness is a hypothetical matter, similar to the āyah: «لئن اَشركتَ لیحبطنّ عملك» [376] or the āyah: «ولو تقوّل علینا بعض الاَقاویل» [377]. That is, [it means] if you commit shirk (polytheism), or if you falsely attribute something to Us, not that such a thing has actually occurred.

And it is possible that the primary intent of the āyah is the followers of the Prophet, not the Prophet himself, just as there is a proverb in Persian: "We speak to the door so that the wall may hear."

A similar point is mentioned in Sūrah al-Nisā’, āyah 140: «اذا سمعتم آیات اللَّه یكفر بها و یستهزأ بها فلا تقعدوا معهم حتّى یخوضوا فى حدیث غیره...» (When you hear the signs of Allah being denied and mocked, do not sit with them until they engage in a different discourse...). If you hear that they are committing disbelief in the Divine signs or mocking them, do not sit with them until they change the subject.

In numerous narrations from the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and the infallible Imams علیهم السلام, sitting with people of sin, or participating in a gathering where sin is committed and one lacks the power to prevent it, has been forbidden, even if those people are one's relatives.

Imam Ali علیه السلام advised his son: Allah has made it obligatory upon the ear not to be pleased with hearing sin and backbiting (ghībah). [378]

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, citing this āyah, said: Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day does not sit in a gathering where an Imam is cursed or a Muslim is criticized. [379]

Imam Ali علیه السلام said: «مجالسة الاشرار تورث سوء الظنّ بالاخیار» (Sitting with the wicked inherits ill-will towards the righteous). Sitting with the wicked causes suspicion towards the good. [380]

375) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān. 376) Al-Zumar, 65. 377) Al-Ḥāqqah, 44. 378) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn. 379) Wasā’il, Vol. 16, p. 266. 380) Wasā’il, Vol. 16, p. 365.

Messages

1- Show your religious zeal and commitment towards the sacred things to the enemies. «اذا رأیت الّذین یخوضون فى آیاتنا فأعرض عنهم»

2- Turning away from evils and passive resistance against evildoers is one of the methods of forbidding the evil (nahy ‘an al-munkar). «فأعرض عنهم»

3- Paying attention and listening to false speech (and watching or reading misleading books and programs) is reprehensible. [381] «یخوضون فى آیاتنا فأعرض عنهم» Of course, there is no prohibition in cases where it is done by experts for the purpose of awareness, refutation, and response.

4- Instead of being absorbed into an undesirable society, group, or gathering, change its situation. «حتّى یخوضوا فى حدیث غیره»

5- Verbal declaration of disassociation (barā’ah) and condemnation is not sufficient; one must take action. «فاعرض... فلاتقعد»

6- Association with the oppressors is improper, so we must avoid it. «فلا تقعد»

7- The condition for religious obligation (taklīf) is attention and awareness. «اِمّا ینسینّك الشّیطان» (Forgetfulness is an accepted excuse.)

8- Speaking unjustly about the verses of the Quran (through mockery, interpretation based on personal opinion (tafsīr bi al-ra’y), innovation (bid‘ah), and distortion) is oppression. «الظالمین»

381) Perhaps this āyah can be cited as evidence for the prohibition of misleading books and programs.

Ayah

وَمَا عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ مِنْ حِسَابِهِم مِّن شَىْءٍ وَلَـٰكِن ذِكْرَىٰ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ (٦۹)
Those who are Godwary are in no way accountable for them, but this is merely for admonition’s sake, so that they may beware. (69)

Key Points

When the verse prohibiting association with mockers and frivolous talkers was revealed, some people said: "Then we should not go to the Masjid al-Haram (Sacred Mosque) or perform Tawaf (circumambulation) either, because the scope of their mockery extends even there." This verse was revealed [to clarify] that the account of the pious Muslims who give admonition to the extent of their ability is separate; they should not abandon the Masjid al-Haram. [382] Participating in gatherings of sinners with the intention of forbidding the wrong (Nahy 'an al-Munkar) and guidance is permissible, but only for those who are pious and immune to influence. Otherwise, many go to save the drowning person and end up drowning themselves. A servant went to fetch barley water The barley water came, and took the servant away.

382) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Observing the priority of the more important over the important (Ahamm wa Muhimm) in matters is among the rational and Islamic principles. Temporarily listening to frivolous talk with the intention of responding or saving the misguided is permissible. «و ما على الّذین یتّقون من حسابهم من شى ء»

2- Piety (Taqwa) is a means of protection and insurance for man against sin. (Like fire-resistant clothing for firefighters.) «و ما على الّذین یتّقون»

3- We must avoid associating with frivolous talkers and not listen to their words, but if something unavoidably reaches our ears, there is no objection. «ما على الّذین یتّقون... من شى ء»

4- In addition to our own piety, we must also think about making others pious. «و لكن ذكرى لعلّهم یتّقون»

Ayah

وَذَرِ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ دِينَهُمْ لَعِبًا وَلَهْوًا وَغَرَّتْهُمُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۚ وَذَكِّرْ بِهِۦٓ أَن تُبْسَلَ نَفْسٌۢ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ لَيْسَ لَهَا مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَلِىٌّ وَلَا شَفِيعٌ وَإِن تَعْدِلْ كُلَّ عَدْلٍ لَّا يُؤْخَذْ مِنْهَآ ۗ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُبْسِلُوا۟ بِمَا كَسَبُوا۟ ۖ لَهُمْ شَرَابٌ مِّنْ حَمِيمٍ وَعَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌۢ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْفُرُونَ (۷٠)
Leave alone those who take their religion for play and diversion and whom the life of this world has deceived, and admonish with it, lest any soul should perish because of what it has earned: It shall not have any guardian besides Allah, nor any intercessor; and though it should offer every kind of ransom, it shall not be accepted from it. They are the ones who perish because of what they have earned; they shall have boiling water for drink and a painful punishment because of what they used to defy. (70)

Key Points

The intent behind abandoning the deviants in the phrase «ذر الّذین اتّخذوا دینهم...» is to express aversion and sever ties with them, which sometimes leads to fighting them, and not merely meaning the abandonment of Jihad against them. The word «تُبسَل» means being deprived of goodness and being led to destruction. Taking religion as a plaything manifests itself in different ways at different times: sometimes through expressing superstitious beliefs, sometimes through deeming religious rulings unenforceable, sometimes through justifying sins, and sometimes through innovation (bid'ah), interpretation based on personal opinion (tafsir bi al-ra'y), following ambiguous verses (mutashabihat), and so on.

Messages

1- Religious zeal causes the rejection of irreligious individuals and the boycotting of opponents of the faith in society. «ذر الّذین ...» Just as we read in other verses: «فاعرض عنهم» (383), «ذرهم یأكلوا ویتمتّعوا» (384), and «لا تحزن علیهم» (385).

2- Attachment to and being deceived by the world is the basis for taking religion as a plaything. «الّذین اتّخذوا دینهم لعبا... غرّتهم الحیاة الدّنیا»

3- Admonition and counsel are the means of salvation from divine wrath and punishment. «و ذَكِّر به»

4- In giving admonition and warning, the Quran must be utilized. «و ذَكِّر به»

5- Let us not be deceived by the world, for on the Day of Judgment, nothing besides Allah will come to our aid. «لیس لها من دون اللَّه ولىّ و لا شفیع»

6- The cause of human misery is the person himself and his actions. «بما كسبت... بما كسبوا... عذاب الیم بما كانوا یكفرون»

Ayahs

قُلْ أَنَدْعُوا۟ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا يَنفَعُنَا وَلَا يَضُرُّنَا وَنُرَدُّ عَلَىٰٓ أَعْقَابِنَا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَىٰنَا ٱللَّهُ كَٱلَّذِى ٱسْتَهْوَتْهُ ٱلشَّيَـٰطِينُ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ حَيْرَانَ لَهُۥٓ أَصْحَـٰبٌ يَدْعُونَهُۥٓ إِلَى ٱلْهُدَى ٱئْتِنَا ۗ قُلْ إِنَّ هُدَى ٱللَّهِ هُوَ ٱلْهُدَىٰ ۖ وَأُمِرْنَا لِنُسْلِمَ لِرَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۷۱)
Say, ‘Shall we invoke besides Allah that which can neither benefit us nor harm us, and turn back on our heels after Allah has guided us, like someone seduced by the devils and bewildered on the earth, who has companions that invite him to guidance, [saying,] ‘‘Come to us!’’?’ Say, ‘Indeed it is the guidance of Allah which is [true] guidance. And we have been commanded to submit to the Lord of all the worlds, (71)
وَأَنْ أَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَٱتَّقُوهُ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ (۷۲)
and that ‘‘Maintain the prayer and be wary of Him, and it is He toward whom you will be gathered.’’ ’ (72)

Key Points

In Ayah 72, among all duties, "prayer" (Salat) is mentioned, and among all beliefs, "the Resurrection" (Ma'ad) is mentioned, which indicates their importance. In monotheism (Tawhid), there is peace and focus, but in polytheism (Shirk) and the worship of multiple gods, there is confusion and bewilderment. «ءأرباب متفرّقون خیر أم اللّه الواحد القهّار» [386] Are several diverse lords better, or Allah, the One, the Irresistible? Allah is both One: «قل هو اللّه احد» (Say: He is Allah, [the] One), and He is quickly satisfied «یا سریع الرّضا» (O Quick to be Pleased), but those besides Allah are numerous, and each has various expectations and is not quickly satisfied.

386) Yusuf, 39.

Messages

1- Stimulate consciences through questioning. «قل أندعوا من دون اللَّه»

2- Polytheism is illogical, because the motivation for worship is to gain benefit or repel harm, and idols are incapable of bringing any benefit or inflicting any harm. «لاینفعنا و لایضرّنا»

3- We should utilize people's instinct for seeking benefit in the path of their propagation and education. «لاینفعنا و لایضرّنا»

4- Polytheism is a type of retreat and ideological regression. «نردّ على اعقابنا»

5- Polytheism leads to confusion and bewilderment. «حیران»

6- In the face of deviations, one must take a clear and repeated stance. «قل أندعوا... قل اِنّ هُدَى اللَّه ...»

7- Submission to Allah is the source of our own growth. «لنُسلِمَ لربّ العالمین»

8- Prayer (Salat), accompanied by piety (Taqwa), is effective. «اقیموا الصلوة واتّقوه»

9- Belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is the factor that generates piety in man. «واتّقوه و هو الّذى الیه تحشرون»

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۖ وَيَوْمَ يَقُولُ كُن فَيَكُونُ ۚ قَوْلُهُ ٱلْحَقُّ ۚ وَلَهُ ٱلْمُلْكُ يَوْمَ يُنفَخُ فِى ٱلصُّورِ ۚ عَـٰلِمُ ٱلْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْحَكِيمُ ٱلْخَبِيرُ (۷۳)
It is He who created the heavens and the earth with reason, and the day He says [to something], ‘Be!’ it is. His word is the truth, and to Him belongs all sovereignty on the day when the Trumpet will be blown. Knower of the sensible and the Unseen, He is the All-wise, the All-aware. (73)

Key Points

In the Quran, two types of blowing into the Trumpet (Sūr) are mentioned: one destructive and the other resurrecting people for the Day of Judgment. In this Ayah, one instance is mentioned, but in Ayah 68 of Surah Az-Zumar, the blowing into the Trumpet twice is discussed: «و نُفِخ فى الصّور فَصَعِق مَن فى السموات و مَن فى الارض الاّ مَن شاءاللَّه ثمّ نُفِخ فیه اُخرى فاذا هم قیام ینظرون» If the previous verses spoke of submission to Allah and the establishment of prayer, here the reason for that is mentioned: creation is in His hands, He is Aware and Wise, and He is informed of everything. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding "عالم الغیب و الشهادة" (Knower of the Unseen and the Seen): The Unseen (Ghayb) is that which has not yet come, and the Seen (Shahādah) is that which has been. [387]

387) معانى الاخبار، ص 146.

Messages

  1. Creation is wise and purposeful. «بالحقّ» (by the truth). This point has been referred to in several verses, including: «و ماخلقنا السموات و الارض ومابینهما باطلاً» [388]

  2. There is no obstacle to the Will of Allah. «كن فیكون»

  3. The Unseen and the Seen, the hidden and the manifest, are the same for Allah. «عالم الغیب و الشهادة»

  4. The basis of Divine sovereignty is wisdom and knowledge. «له الملك ... و هو الحكیم الخبیر»

  5. On the Day of Judgment, the manifestation of Divine power will become clear to everyone, because means and instruments will be ineffective there. «له الملك»

388) ص، 27.

Ayah

۞ وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَٰهِيمُ لِأَبِيهِ ءَازَرَ أَتَتَّخِذُ أَصْنَامًا ءَالِهَةً ۖ إِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰكَ وَقَوْمَكَ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ (۷٤)
When Abraham said to Azar, his father, ‘Do you take idols for gods? Indeed I see you and your people in manifest error.’ (74)

Key Points

The word 'ab' (أب) naturally means father, but it is also used to mean maternal grandfather, uncle, and guardian/tutor. Just as the children of Jacob referred to their fathers and their father's uncle, Prophet Ishmael (Isma'il), as 'ab' when they said: «نعبد الهك و اله آبائك ابراهیم و اسماعیل و اسحاق» [389]. And the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله also said: «أنا و علىّ أبَوا هذه الاُمّة» [390], meaning, "Ali and I are the fathers of this Ummah (nation)." Azar was Abraham's uncle, not his father. Abraham's fathers (ancestors) were all monotheists (muwahhid). Furthermore, scholars from the Ahl al-Sunnah (Sunni tradition), such as Tabari, Alusi, and Suyuti, state that Azar was not Abraham's father. Moreover, Abraham prayed for his parents: «ربّنَا اغفِر لى و لوالدىّ...» [391], whereas a Muslim does not have the right to seek forgiveness for a polytheist (mushrik), even if he is a relative. From the totality of this evidence, it appears that 'ab' (أب) in this ayah (verse) does not mean father. Furthermore, the name of Abraham's father عليه السلام is mentioned in history books as "Tarukh," not Azar. [392] Prophet Abraham's seeking forgiveness for his uncle Azar was also before his spirit of disbelief became clear and was due to a promise He had made to him. When he realized that he would not accept the truth, he disassociated himself from him and separated. «فلمّا تبیّن له انّه عدوٌّ للّه تبرّأ منه» [393]

389) Al-Baqarah, 133. 390) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 16, p. 364. 391) Ibrahim, 41. 392) Tafsir al-Mizan. 393) At-Tawbah, 114.

Messages

1- Kinship is not an obstacle to forbidding evil (nahy 'anil-munkar). «اذ قال ابراهیم لابیه...»

2- The criterion for interaction is the truth, not age. «قال ابراهیم لابیه» (Prophet Abraham clearly stated the truth and warned his uncle, who was older than him.)

3- In the method of inviting to the truth, one must start with close relatives. «لابیه» And one must speak about fundamental issues. «أتتّخذ اصناماً آلهة»

4- The history of polytheism (shirk) and idol worship dates back to before the prophethood of Abraham. «أتتّخذ اصناماً»

5- Majority, history, and age do not turn falsehood (batil) into truth, nor are they always valuable. «أراك و قومك فى ضلال»

6- Idol worship is a deviation that sound consciences and intellect criticize. «أتتّخذ أصناماً... ضلال مبین»

7- Although Abraham عليه السلام was forbearing, «اِنّ أبراهیم لاوّاه حلیم» [394], and a prophet must speak gently even to Pharaoh, the tone of Prophet Abraham's confrontation in this ayah was due to his uncle's insistence on disbelief (kufr). «اِنّى اراك و قومك فى ضلال مبین»

394) At-Tawbah, 114.

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ نُرِىٓ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ مَلَكُوتَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَلِيَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُوقِنِينَ (۷٥)
Thus did We show Abraham the dominions of the heavens and the earth, that he might be of those who possess certitude. (75)

Key Points

Regarding Malakūt (the dominion/kingdom), scholars have offered statements, some of which we quote here: The word Malakūt is derived from mulk (sovereignty/possession), and the letters wāw and tā’ have been added to it for emphasis and hyperbole. The Malakūt of the heavens means the absolute and true ownership of Allah over them, and observing the Malakūt means observing things in terms of their relation to Allah. A Malakūtī vision means perceiving Tawhīd (monotheism) in existence and the falsehood of shirk (polytheism). (395)

The realms of existence are divided into four sections: Lāhūt (the realm of Divinity, which none knows save Allah), Jabarūt (the realm of incorporeal beings), Malakūt (the realm of subtle bodies/essences), and Nāsūt (the realm of generation, corruption, change, and transformation). (396)

The realm of Malakūt is the world of secrets, order, wonders, and the unseen world (Ghayb). (397)

The Malakūt of the heavens refers to their marvels. (398)

By seeing the Malakūt of the heavens and the earth, Abraham became more acquainted with the divine tradition (Sunnah), creation, wisdom, and Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). According to Imam Baqir عليه السلام, Allah granted Abraham عليه السلام power and light in his eyes, enabling him to observe the depths of the heavens, the Throne (‘Arsh), and the earth. (399)

Sound argumentation and scholarly engagement with others require heartfelt faith. Prophet Abraham, after achieving certainty, "ليكون من الموقنين" (that he might be among those who are certain), enters into the argumentation that follows in the subsequent verses. (400)

The letter wāw (and) in the phrase "وليکون من الموقنين" signifies that the Malakūtī vision had several goals, one of which was for Prophet Abraham to attain certainty.

395) Tafsir al-Mizan. 396) Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan. 397) Qamus, Mu’jam al-Wasīt. 398) Tafsir Ruh al-Ma’ani. 399) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 400) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Whoever recognizes the truth, invites others to it, and fears nothing, Allah grants him a "Malakūtī vision." (Like Prophet Abraham, according to the preceding verse) "و كذلك نُرِى ابراهیم ملكوت..." Yes, even a young person can attain a Malakūtī vision through spiritual growth, as, according to several interpretations, Abraham was a youth at that time.

2- The Prophets benefit from the special guidance of Allah. "و كذلك نُرِى ابراهیم"

3- Certainty (Yaqīn) is the highest degree of faith. "و لیكون من الموقنین"

4- Attaining certainty requires divine assistance. "نُرِى... الموقنين"

Ayah

فَلَمَّا جَنَّ عَلَيْهِ ٱلَّيْلُ رَءَا كَوْكَبًا ۖ قَالَ هَـٰذَا رَبِّى ۖ فَلَمَّآ أَفَلَ قَالَ لَآ أُحِبُّ ٱلْـَٔافِلِينَ (۷٦)
When night darkened over him, he saw a star and said, ‘This is my Lord!’ But when it set, he said, ‘I do not like those who set.’ (76)

Key Points

During the time of Ibrahim عليه السلام, star worship was common, and people believed that the stars were effective in managing the universe.

Question: Could a great Prophet like Hazrat Ibrahim, even for a single moment, worship a star, the moon, or the sun?

Answer: The debate and dialogue of Hazrat Ibrahim with the polytheists was based on accommodation (mumaashat) and showing flexibility in the discussion, intended to refute the opponent's belief, not because it was his heartfelt belief, as polytheism (shirk) contradicts infallibility ('ismah). The use of the phrase «یا قوم» (O my people) in the two subsequent verses is a sign of accommodation regarding his statement «هذا ربّى» (This is my Lord). For this reason, when he sees the setting of the moon and the sun, he says: I am innocent of your polytheism, and he does not say innocent of my own polytheism (Ayah 78). Furthermore, we read in the previous Ayah that Allah showed Ibrahim the dominion (Malakut) of the heavens and the earth, and he attained certainty (yaqin). The beginning of this Ayah starts with the letter "fa" (ف), meaning that the result of that dominion and certainty was this type of argumentation. An entity that rises and sets is subject to laws, not ruling over them. Ibrahim first temporarily expresses acceptance of it so that he can later refute it through argumentation.

Messages

1- One of the methods of argumentation is to express agreement with a false belief and then refute it. «هذا ربّى»

2- Awakening innate nature (fitrah), activating thoughts, and paying attention to emotions are among the best methods of propagation (da'wah). «لا اُحبّ الافلین»

3- The true Beloved is one who is not restricted by place, time, or temporariness. «لاأحبّ الافلین»

4- The Worshipped (Ma'bud) must be beloved, and worship must be based on love; the spirit of religion is love. «لاأحبّ الافلین»

Ayah

فَلَمَّا رَءَا ٱلْقَمَرَ بَازِغًا قَالَ هَـٰذَا رَبِّى ۖ فَلَمَّآ أَفَلَ قَالَ لَئِن لَّمْ يَهْدِنِى رَبِّى لَأَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلضَّآلِّينَ (۷۷)
Then, when he saw the moon rising, he said, ‘This is my Lord!’ But when it set, he said, ‘Had my Lord not guided me, I would surely have been among the astray lot.’ (77)

Key Points

The word bāzigh (بازغ) is derived from bazgh (بزغ), meaning to cleave or to cause blood to flow. However, here it seems that the rising of the sun and the moon cleaves the curtain of the night's darkness and creates a faint redness around itself. [401] Abraham (عليه السلام) confronted both the star-worshippers and the moon and sun-worshippers. [402] According to many commentators, the dialogue of Prophet Abraham was with the polytheists of the region of Babylon. Whenever a person reaches a dead end in any hypothesis, he must change his path without obstinacy. Contrary to the popular saying that a man is one who stands by his word and insists upon it, this Ayah tells us that a man is one whose word is Haqq (Truth), even if it requires changing his position.

401) Tafsir-e Nemooneh. 402) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

Messages 1, 2, and 3 of the previous Ayah are also relevant here.

4- In criticism, one must utilize the step-by-step method. In the previous Ayah, he said: I do not love the āfilīn (those that set). But here he says: The worship of the moon is deviation and misguidance (ḍalālah).

5- On the path of recognition (of God), one must rely on Divine grace and guidance, and without it, one cannot reach the clear spring of knowledge (ma'ārif). [403] «لئن لم یهدنى ربّى» (If my Lord does not guide me)

6- Within man, there is something lost, which he sometimes seeks in various (and sometimes deviant) manifestations in his search for it. «هذا ربّى» (This is my Lord) appears in two consecutive Ayahs.

7- Even the Prophets are in need of Divine guidance. «لئن لم یهدنى ربّى»

8- Guidance is among the functions of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «یهدنى ربّى»

403) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Ayah

فَلَمَّا رَءَا ٱلشَّمْسَ بَازِغَةً قَالَ هَـٰذَا رَبِّى هَـٰذَآ أَكْبَرُ ۖ فَلَمَّآ أَفَلَتْ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ إِنِّى بَرِىٓءٌ مِّمَّا تُشْرِكُونَ (۷۸)
Then, when he saw the sun rising, he said, ‘This is my Lord! This is bigger!’ But when it set, he said, ‘O my people, indeed I disown what you take as [His] partners.’ (78)

Key Points

In this Ayah too, Ibrahim (Abraham) عليه السلام is in the position of debate and discussion, not in the position of stating his personal belief. As mentioned previously, the phrase "يا قوم" (O my people) and also "ممّا تشركون" (what you associate [with Allah], not what I associate) is evidence that the Prophet himself did not worship the moon, the sun, or the stars. [404]

404) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan.

Messages

Messages 1, 2, and 3 of Ayah 76 are also applicable here.

4- The size or magnitude of objects is not important; since all of them are changing and unstable, they cannot be God. "هذا أكبر" (This is greater).

5- The method of teaching the truth or criticizing falsehood must be step-by-step. First, negating the star and the moon, and finally the sun. "فلمّا رءا الشمس... قال" (So when he saw the sun... he said).

6- Disavowal of polytheism (Shirk) is the "Ibrahimic cry." "اِنّى برى ء ممّا تشركون" (Indeed, I am free from what you associate [with Allah]).

7- The disavowal is from "Shirk" (polytheism), not from "individuals." "ممّا تشركون" (from what you associate), not "منكم" (from you).

8- Seeking disavowal must follow the presentation of proof and evidence. (First, he raised the issue of setting and disappearance, then the disavowal of Shirk). "فلمّا أفلت قال..." (So when it set, he said...).

Ayah

إِنِّى وَجَّهْتُ وَجْهِىَ لِلَّذِى فَطَرَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ حَنِيفًا ۖ وَمَآ أَنَا۠ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (۷۹)
‘Indeed I have turned my face toward Him who originated the heavens and the earth, as a Hanif, and I am not one of the polytheists.’ (79)

Key Points

Passing beyond decline and instability and reaching stability is a manifestation of the celestial vision that Allah granted to Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham). "Hanif" (حَنیف), derived from "hanaf" (حنف), means pure, without deviation, and inclined toward the straight and true path. "Fatr" (فَطر), which means creation, originally carries the concept of splitting or cleaving. Perhaps this refers to modern science which states that the universe was initially a single mass, then it split apart, and the celestial bodies appeared one after another. [405]

405) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- As soon as the path of truth becomes clear to us, we must declare it decisively and not fear loneliness. «اِنّى وَجّهت»

2- Whoever moves beyond the worship of partial, material, limited, and perishable idols reaches a Deity Who is total, spiritual, infinite, and eternal. «للّذى فطر السموات والارض»

3- Do not become immersed in manifestations, for they fade sooner or later; pay attention to Allah, Who is the Creator of manifestations. «فَطَر السموات والارض»

4- Man can reach a point where he shatters the false beliefs of generations and ages. «و ما أنا من المشركین»

5- Pure Monotheism (Tawhid) is accompanied by dissociation from polytheism (Shirk). «و ما أنا من المشركین»

Ayah

وَحَآجَّهُۥ قَوْمُهُۥ ۚ قَالَ أَتُحَـٰٓجُّوٓنِّى فِى ٱللَّهِ وَقَدْ هَدَىٰنِ ۚ وَلَآ أَخَافُ مَا تُشْرِكُونَ بِهِۦٓ إِلَّآ أَن يَشَآءَ رَبِّى شَيْـًٔا ۗ وَسِعَ رَبِّى كُلَّ شَىْءٍ عِلْمًا ۗ أَفَلَا تَتَذَكَّرُونَ (۸٠)
His people argued with him. He said, ‘Do you argue with me concerning Allah, while He has guided me for certain? I do not fear what you ascribe to Him as [His] partners, excepting anything that my Lord may wish. My Lord embraces all things in [His] knowledge. Will you not then take admonition? (80)

Messages

1- The people of falsehood are fanatical. «حاجّه قومه» (Just as a debtor, if left alone, acts like a creditor, the misguided people, instead of being ashamed of their polytheism, stand up and argue.)

2- The fanaticism and insistence of the people of falsehood are astonishing. «أتحاجّونى فى اللَّه»

3- A monotheist, even if alone, is not worried about confronting a crowd. «أتحاجّونى فى اللَّه... و لا اخاف...»

4- Seeking the truth is from man, but guidance is from Allah. «هَدانِ» (Even the Prophets need Divine guidance.)

5- Whoever is covered by Divine guidance has no fear. «هَدانِ ولا أخاف»

6- Not fearing anything other than Allah is a sign of monotheism (Tawhid). «لا أخاف» (The polytheists were threatening Prophet Ibrahim (عليه السلام) with the danger and vengeance of the idols.)

7- Among the roots of polytheism (Shirk) is the assumption of harm and the fear of damage caused by idols and tyrants (taghuts). «لا أخاف ما تشركون» (The imaginary fear of the idols was the cause of their insistence and fanaticism in idol worship.)

8- The effect and role of every phenomenon depend on the will of Allah. «لا أخاف ما تشركون به الاّ ان یشاء ربّى»

9- Lordship (Rububiyyah) is worthy of the One who possesses comprehensive knowledge. «وسع ربّى كلّ شى ء علماً»

10- The truth is an innate (fitrī) and conscientious matter, and remembrance (tadhakkur) is sufficient for its recognition. «أفلا تتذكّرون»

Ayah

وَكَيْفَ أَخَافُ مَآ أَشْرَكْتُمْ وَلَا تَخَافُونَ أَنَّكُمْ أَشْرَكْتُم بِٱللَّهِ مَا لَمْ يُنَزِّلْ بِهِۦ عَلَيْكُمْ سُلْطَـٰنًا ۚ فَأَىُّ ٱلْفَرِيقَيْنِ أَحَقُّ بِٱلْأَمْنِ ۖ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (۸۱)
How could I fear what you ascribe [to Him] as [His] partners, when you do not fear ascribing to Allah partners for which He has not sent down any authority to you? So [tell me,] which of the two sides has a greater right to safety, if you know? (81)

Key Points

The word "سلطان" (Sultān) means proof, evidence, and demonstration. Contrary to those who consider fear the motive for believing in Allah, this Ayah considers fear the motive for polytheism (Shirk).

Messages

1- Do not fear imaginary threats. «كیف أخاف ما أشركتم»

2- Spiritual security is achieved in the light of Monotheism (Tawhid). «كیف أخاف ما أشركتم»

3- Religious beliefs must be based on proof and evidence. «كیف ما أشركتم باللَّه ما لم یُنزّل به علیكم سلطانا»

3- In discussion and debate, one should not provoke people's fanaticism. «فأىّ الفریقین» (Then which of the two parties...) [The speaker] did not say: We are safe and you are in danger.

5- Correct knowledge is the means of finding the way to Allah; if we use it properly, we will reach correct conclusions. «ان كنتم تعلمون»

Ayahs

ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَلَمْ يَلْبِسُوٓا۟ إِيمَـٰنَهُم بِظُلْمٍ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمُ ٱلْأَمْنُ وَهُم مُّهْتَدُونَ (۸۲)
Those who have faith and do not taint their faith with wrongdoing—for such there shall be safety, and they are the [rightly] guided.’ (82)
وَتِلْكَ حُجَّتُنَآ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهَآ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ عَلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦ ۚ نَرْفَعُ دَرَجَـٰتٍ مَّن نَّشَآءُ ۗ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ حَكِيمٌ عَلِيمٌ (۸۳)
This was Our argument that We gave to Abraham against his people. We raise in rank whomever We wish. Indeed your Lord is all-wise, all-knowing. (83)

Key Points

The previous verses were about Tawhid (monotheism) and Shirk (polytheism), and these verses serve as a summary of the preceding verses, just as the Imam (عليه السلام) stated: the meaning of zulm (injustice/wrongdoing) in this verse is Shirk. [406] In some narrations, the meaning of zulm has been taken as doubt and skepticism. [407] Of course, doubt is sometimes involuntary and a prelude to research, but sometimes it takes the form of an excuse or casting suspicion, and this second type is zulm. [408] Imam Baqir (عليه السلام) said: This verse was revealed concerning Hadrat Ali (عليه السلام), who never resorted to Shirk for a moment throughout His life. [409] The word “labas” (لبس) means to cover or conceal. Therefore, since faith (Iman) is innate (fitrī), it is not destructible; rather, it is covered by dust (or impurities).

406) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 407) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Kafi, vol. 2, p. 399. 408) Tafsir Rahnamā. 409) Bihar, vol. 23, p. 367.

Messages

1- The affliction of faith is zulm (injustice/wrongdoing), Shirk, and resorting to non-divine leaders. «آمنوا و لم یلبسوا ایمانهم بظلم»

2- Preserving faith is more important than faith itself. «لم یلبسوا ایمانهم بظلم اولئك لهم الامن»

3- True security and guidance are achieved under the shade of faith and justice. «آمنوا... لهم الأمن و هم مهتدون» (Neither unjust believers are guided, nor are faithless seekers of justice.)

4- As long as faith is not pure, anxiety persists. «و لم یلبسوا...بظلم...لهم الأمن»

5- Knowledge (‘ilm) and wisdom (ḥikmah) are two necessary conditions for planning and management. «اِنّ ربّك حكیم علیم» (Considering that the word Rabb linguistically means manager and educator/nurturer.)

6- A monotheist who stands against societal deviations with proof and evidence possesses ranks (or degrees). «درجات»

7- Divine ranks are bestowed upon individuals wisely. «نرفع درجات ... حكیم»

Ayah

وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُۥٓ إِسْحَـٰقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ ۚ كُلًّا هَدَيْنَا ۚ وَنُوحًا هَدَيْنَا مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِهِۦ دَاوُۥدَ وَسُلَيْمَـٰنَ وَأَيُّوبَ وَيُوسُفَ وَمُوسَىٰ وَهَـٰرُونَ ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۸٤)
And We gave him Isaac and Jacob and guided each of them. And Noah We had guided before, and from his offspring, David and Solomon, Job, Joseph, Moses and Aaron—thus do We reward the virtuous— (84)

Key Points

In this Ayah and the two succeeding Ayahs, the names of 17 Prophets are mentioned in total. This arrangement is neither based on chronological order nor rank. Perhaps their division across these three Ayahs is based on the principle that Ayah 84 mentions Prophets who held governance (rule). Ayah 85 mentions Prophets who were poor and had no worldly possessions. And Ayah 86 mentions Prophets who faced specific hardships. [410]

Regarding the pronoun in "ذرّیته" (his progeny), there are two possibilities: One is that the pronoun refers back to Prophet Abraham (Ibrahim), because the preceding Ayahs concern him, and most of these individuals are from his lineage; the narration (Hadith) also supports this. The other is that it refers back to Noah (Nuh) عليه السلام, because the pronoun is closer to his name, and Prophets such as Lot (Lut) were from his lineage.

410) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- A righteous child is a divine gift and blessing. "وهبنا" (We granted) - "نجزى" (We recompense).

2- Being the descendant of a Prophet is not a source of honor by itself; the progeny of Abraham themselves possessed merits and benefited from divine guidance. "كلاً هدینا" (We guided each one).

3- The tradition (Sunnah) of guidance and prophethood has always been continuous. "ونوحاً هدینا من قبل" (And Noah We guided before).

4- Divine favors are not granted to anyone without reason or calculation. "كذلك نجزى المحسنین" (Thus do We recompense the doers of good).

Ayahs

وَزَكَرِيَّا وَيَحْيَىٰ وَعِيسَىٰ وَإِلْيَاسَ ۖ كُلٌّ مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۸٥)
and Zechariah, John, Jesus and Ilyas—each of them among the righteous— (85)
وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَٱلْيَسَعَ وَيُونُسَ وَلُوطًا ۚ وَكُلًّا فَضَّلْنَا عَلَى ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۸٦)
and Ishmael, Elisha, Jonah and Lot—each We graced over all the nations— (86)

Key Points

|

Ayah

وَمِنْ ءَابَآئِهِمْ وَذُرِّيَّـٰتِهِمْ وَإِخْوَٰنِهِمْ ۖ وَٱجْتَبَيْنَـٰهُمْ وَهَدَيْنَـٰهُمْ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (۸۷)
and from among their fathers, their descendants and brethren—We chose them and guided them to a straight path. (87)

Key Points

Some have assumed that Dhurriyyah (offspring/progeny) refers only to grandsons through the male line, whereas Prophet Jesus (عليه السلام), who had no father and was related to Abraham (عليه السلام) only through his mother, is counted among the Dhurriyyah of Prophet Abraham in this Ayah. «و من ذرّیّته... عیسى» Imam Sadiq and Imam Kadhim (علیهما السلام), based on this very Ayah, considered the Ahl al-Bayt (علیهم السلام), who trace their lineage to the Prophet through his daughter (mother), to be the Dhurriyyah of the Messenger of Allah and the sons (Abna') of the Messenger of Allah. [411] Fakhr al-Razi also accepted this point in his Tafsir.
