In Tafsir al-Manar, a Hadith is also quoted from Sahih al-Bukhari stating that the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله) used the word Dhurriyyah concerning Imam Hassan (عليه السلام).

There are various opinions regarding whether Yasa' (یَسَع) is an independent name, or if it is the present tense form of wasa'a (like Yahya), or if it is the same as Yusha' (یوشع) which entered Arabic from Hebrew with some modifications.

Some have sought to use the word min (مِن) in «مِن آبائِهم» (from their fathers) to suggest that there were also misguided individuals among the fathers of the prophets, but the tone of the verses is about selecting the fathers for prophethood, not about their disbelief or faith. [412]

411) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

412) Tafsir Namuneh.

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ هُدَى ٱللَّهِ يَهْدِى بِهِۦ مَن يَشَآءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِۦ ۚ وَلَوْ أَشْرَكُوا۟ لَحَبِطَ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۸۸)
That is Allah’s guidance: with it, He guides whomever He wishes of His servants. But were they to ascribe any partners [to Allah], what they used to do would not avail them. (88)

Messages

1- True guidance is the guidance of Allah. Other forms of guidance are a mirage. «ذلك هدى اللَّه»

2- Guidance is the work of Allah. Even the prophets do not possess a path to guidance independently. «یهدى به مَن یشاء»

3- Polytheism (Shirk) is the factor that nullifies and invalidates deeds. «لو اشركوا لحبط»

4- There is no discrimination in the Divine tradition (Sunnah). Even if the prophets commit polytheism, they will be punished, because the essence lies with the Truth (Haqq), not with individuals. (Considering that the preceding verses relate to the prophets). «ولو أشركوا»

5- The prophets are infallible (Ma'sum), and the dust of polytheism never settles upon their character. In the verse, the assumption of polytheism is presented as an impossible hypothesis. «لَوْ»

6- In the Divine worldview, the value of deeds lies in their sincerity (Ikhlas). If there is no sincerity, nothing remains. «ولو أشركوا لحبط»

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْحُكْمَ وَٱلنُّبُوَّةَ ۚ فَإِن يَكْفُرْ بِهَا هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ فَقَدْ وَكَّلْنَا بِهَا قَوْمًا لَّيْسُوا۟ بِهَا بِكَـٰفِرِينَ (۸۹)
They are the ones whom We gave the Book, judgement and prophethood. So if these disbelieve in them, We have certainly entrusted them to a people who will never disbelieve in them. (89)

Key Points

The word "Hukm" (حُكم) means both governance and rule, and also judgment and arbitration, and also understanding and intellect. In Mufradat al-Raghib, its original meaning is mentioned as prevention and restraint, and since sound intellect, judgment, and governance prevent error and transgression, the term Hukm is applied to them. It is narrated from commentators in Tafsir al-Manar and Ruh al-Ma'ani that the intent behind (the people who do not disbelieve and who accept and support the truth) are the Iranians. [413] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The companions of the Master of this Affair are protected; if all people abandon Him, Allah will bring His companions. They are the very ones about whom Allah said: «فاِن یكفر بها هؤلاء فقد وكّلنا...» [414]

413) Tafsir Namuneh. 414) Ghaybat Nu'mani, p. 316.

Messages

1- Prophets have the right to govern and judge. «آتیناهم الكتاب و الحكم و النبوّة»

2- Good-doing (benevolence), righteousness, and finding the straight path are among the characteristics of the Prophets. «نجزى المحسنین....كلٌّ من الصالحین....هدیناهم الى صراط مستقیم.....آتیناهم الكتاب»

3- Every school of thought has supporters and opponents, and among them there is both attrition (falling away) and growth. Sometimes a supporter becomes an opponent, or an opponent becomes a supporter. A Muslim becomes an apostate, and a disbeliever becomes a Muslim. «فاِن یكفر بها هؤلاء فقد وكّلنا بها قوماً»

4- With the disbelief of a group, the school of truth does not remain without supporters. When the path is true and Divine, the coming and going of individuals should not affect a person. «فاِن یكفر بها هؤلاء فقد وكّلنا بها قوماً»

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ هَدَى ٱللَّهُ ۖ فَبِهُدَىٰهُمُ ٱقْتَدِهْ ۗ قُل لَّآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ أَجْرًا ۖ إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرَىٰ لِلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۹٠)
They are the ones whom Allah has guided. So follow their guidance. Say, ‘I do not ask you any recompense for it. It is just an admonition for all the nations.’ (90)

Key Points

The letter 'h' (ه) in "iqtadih" (اقتده) is not a pronoun; rather, it is the 'Hā' of silence (Hā' as-sakt) and is used for pausing (waqf).

Messages

1- Following the guidance of the prophets is emulating Divine guidance. "هَدَى اللَّه فَبِهُداهمُ اقتَدِه"

2- The name, memory, and path of Allah's saints (Awliyā') must remain alive, and innovations should not cause the previous values to be forgotten. "فبهداهُمُ اقتَدِه"

3- The worth of a human being lies in his intellectual path and practical conduct. (He said: "فَبِهُداهُمُ اقتده" [follow their guidance], and He did not say: "بهم اقتَدِه" [follow them]).

4- The abrogation (naskh) of past religions does not mean the invalidation of their principles and generalities; the general path of the prophets must continue. "فَبِهُداهمُ اقتَدَه"

5- The most successful human being must benefit from the perfections, good morals, and firm method of those who came before. "فَبِهداهمُ اقتَده"

6- The goals of all the prophets are one. (Because it is impossible to emulate prophets who have contradictory goals). "فبهداهم اقتده"

7- The reason and result of following must be the attainment of guidance. "هَدَى اللَّه فبهداهم اقتده"

8- The preacher (muballigh) should not be inclined towards worldliness (dunyā-seeking) or be accused of it. "قل لاأسئلكم"

9- One of the differences between the prophets and other claimants is that the prophets' goal is not material gain. "لا أسئلكم"

10- The prophets are a means of remembrance (dhikrā) for forgetful and heedless people. "ذِكرى "

11- Islam is a universal religion. "ذكرى للعالمین"

Ayah

وَمَا قَدَرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ حَقَّ قَدْرِهِۦٓ إِذْ قَالُوا۟ مَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ بَشَرٍ مِّن شَىْءٍ ۗ قُلْ مَنْ أَنزَلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ ٱلَّذِى جَآءَ بِهِۦ مُوسَىٰ نُورًا وَهُدًى لِّلنَّاسِ ۖ تَجْعَلُونَهُۥ قَرَاطِيسَ تُبْدُونَهَا وَتُخْفُونَ كَثِيرًا ۖ وَعُلِّمْتُم مَّا لَمْ تَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنتُمْ وَلَآ ءَابَآؤُكُمْ ۖ قُلِ ٱللَّهُ ۖ ثُمَّ ذَرْهُمْ فِى خَوْضِهِمْ يَلْعَبُونَ (۹۱)
They did not regard Allah with the regard due to Him when they said, ‘Allah has not sent down anything to any human.’ Say, ‘Who had sent down the Book that was brought by Moses as a light and guidance for the people, which you make into parchments that you display, while you conceal much of it, and [by means of which] you were taught what you did not know, [neither] you nor your fathers?’ Say, ‘Allah!’ Then leave them to play around in their impious gossip. (91)

Key Points

A group of Jews, despite believing in the descent of revelation upon Prophet Moses, would say out of sheer obstinacy: Allah has not sent down a book upon any prophet. This Ayah states: Your statement contradicts your belief. If Allah does not send revelation upon anyone, then who sent down the Torah, in which you believe?

Messages

1- Sending the prophets and heavenly books is a favor (lutf) from God to His servants. Those who deny them are, in reality, denying Divine favor, mercy, and wisdom. «ما قدروا اللَّه...»

2- Doubts that are raised must be answered. «قالوا... قل»

3- The original Torah was light and guidance. «نوراً و هدى»

4- There were contents in the Torah that the Jewish scholars concealed. «تخفون كثیرا»

5- Religious preachers must explain the teachings and truths of the faith to the people without undue expediency. «ماقدروا اللَّه... تخفون كثیرا»

6- Without revelation, human access to many profound teachings is limited. «عُلِّمتم ما لم تعلموا...»

7- The duty of the prophets is conveyance (of the message), not compulsion. «قل اللّه ثمّ ذرهم»

8- Argumentation should be limited to necessity, and no more. This Ayah, which is itself a form of argumentation, states: Now that they do not accept, leave them alone. «ذرهم»

Ayah

وَهَـٰذَا كِتَـٰبٌ أَنزَلْنَـٰهُ مُبَارَكٌ مُّصَدِّقُ ٱلَّذِى بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ وَلِتُنذِرَ أُمَّ ٱلْقُرَىٰ وَمَنْ حَوْلَهَا ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِۦ ۖ وَهُمْ عَلَىٰ صَلَاتِهِمْ يُحَافِظُونَ (۹۲)
Blessed is this Book, which We have sent down, confirming what was [revealed] before it, so that you may warn the Mother of Cities and those around it. Those who believe in the Hereafter believe in it, and they are watchful of their prayers. (92)

Key Points

In this Ayah, the word latundhir (لتنذر) is accompanied by the waw (the conjunction 'and'), forming wa litundhira (وَ لِتُنذِرَ). Perhaps this indicates that the Quran has other goals besides warning (indhar). Although the Prophets are bearers of good tidings (mubashshirūn) regarding guidance and and salvation, because averting harm takes precedence over achieving benefit, and because the spiritual need of man for warning (indhar) is greater, the word nadhīr (warner) and its derivatives are used more frequently in the Quran than bashīr (bearer of good tidings) and its derivatives.

Messages

1- The Quran encompasses all blessings (barakāt). (Such as: guidance, lesson, healing, growth, honor/might) Mubārak (blessed).

2- The Quran is consistent with other heavenly books and confirms them, which is a sign of the unity of purpose and their Divine origin. Muṣaddiq (confirmer).

3- In propagation (tablīgh), one must start with Umm al-Qurā (the Mother of Cities) and important centers, and then address the surrounding areas. لِتُنذِرَ أُمَّ الْقُرَىٰ وَمَنْ حَوْلَهَا

4- Belief in the Quran and the Day of Judgment go hand in hand. يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِ

5- The clearest manifestation of faith is prayer (ṣalāh). عَلَىٰ صَلَاتِهِمْ يُحَافِظُونَ

6- Faith in the Hereafter is one of the factors for guarding the prayer. يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ... عَلَىٰ صَلَاتِهِمْ يُحَافِظُونَ

Ayah

وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ ٱفْتَرَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا أَوْ قَالَ أُوحِىَ إِلَىَّ وَلَمْ يُوحَ إِلَيْهِ شَىْءٌ وَمَن قَالَ سَأُنزِلُ مِثْلَ مَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ ۗ وَلَوْ تَرَىٰٓ إِذِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ فِى غَمَرَٰتِ ٱلْمَوْتِ وَٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ بَاسِطُوٓا۟ أَيْدِيهِمْ أَخْرِجُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَكُمُ ۖ ٱلْيَوْمَ تُجْزَوْنَ عَذَابَ ٱلْهُونِ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَقُولُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ غَيْرَ ٱلْحَقِّ وَكُنتُمْ عَنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ تَسْتَكْبِرُونَ (۹۳)
Who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah, or says, ‘It has been revealed to me,’ while nothing was revealed to him, and he who says, ‘I will bring the like of what Allah has sent down?’ Were you to see when the wrongdoers are in the throes of death, and the angels extend their hands [saying]: ‘Give up your souls! Today you shall be requited with a humiliating punishment because of what you used to attribute to Allah untruly, and for your being disdainful towards His signs.’ (93)

Key Points

Regarding the occasion of the revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of this Ayah, it is narrated that a person named 'Abd ibn Sa'd was one of the scribes of the revelation (Wahy). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله expelled him due to an act of treachery. He then gathered people and claimed: I, too, can bring forth verses like the Qur'an. Some have considered the occasion of the Ayah's revelation to be the claim of prophethood by "Musaylimah al-Kadhdhab" (the Liar), which occurred toward the end of the Prophet's صلى الله علیه وآله era, and they consider the Ayah to be Medinan, placed here by the Prophet's command. Ghamarāt (غَمَرات), derived from Ghamrah (غمره), means the severities of the moment of death that engulf a person. Some of those who claimed prophethood included: Musaylimah in Yemen, Aswad al-'Ansi in Yemen, and Tulayhah al-Asadi among Banu Asad. [415] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of 'Adhāb al-Hūn (the humiliating punishment) is death while thirsty. [416] Imam Baqir عليه السلام considered those who unjustly claim the Imamate (leadership) to be among the examples covered by this Ayah. [417]

415) Tafsir al-Maraghi. 416) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 417) Tafsir Ayyashi.

Messages

1- Slander against Allah is cultural oppression, and the claim of leadership by the unqualified is the greatest oppression. «و مَن أظلم ممّن افترى... أو قال اوحى الىّ»

2- The enemy of truth either lowers the truth or elevates himself. First, he says: The Qur'an is a myth; if he fails, he says: I, too, can produce interesting words like the Qur'an. «سأنزل مثل ما أنزل اللّه» In another place, we read: «لو نشاء لقلنا مثل هذا» [418]

3- The unqualified and false claimants of religious positions die a terrible death. «ولو ترى اِذِالظّالمون...»

4- The punishments of the Hereafter begin from the moment of death. The disbelievers are told: Give up your souls, die, «أخرجوا أنفسكم», which is accompanied by a form of humiliation.

5- The human soul is abstract and independent of the body. «أخرجوا أنفسكم»

6- The punishment for insulting the revelation (Wahy) and the religion is a humiliating and degrading torment. «تجزون عذاب الهون»

418) Al-Anfal, 31.

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ جِئْتُمُونَا فُرَٰدَىٰ كَمَا خَلَقْنَـٰكُمْ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ وَتَرَكْتُم مَّا خَوَّلْنَـٰكُمْ وَرَآءَ ظُهُورِكُمْ ۖ وَمَا نَرَىٰ مَعَكُمْ شُفَعَآءَكُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ زَعَمْتُمْ أَنَّهُمْ فِيكُمْ شُرَكَـٰٓؤُا۟ ۚ لَقَد تَّقَطَّعَ بَيْنَكُمْ وَضَلَّ عَنكُم مَّا كُنتُمْ تَزْعُمُونَ (۹٤)
‘Certainly you have come to Us alone, just as We created you the first time, and left behind whatever We had bestowed on you. We do not see your intercessors with you—those whom you claimed to be [Our] partners in [deciding] you[r] [fate]. Certainly all links between you have been cut, and what you used to claim has forsaken you!’ (94)

Key Points

This address is made to the polytheists (mushrikūn) at the moment of death or during the Day of Resurrection (Qiyāmah). The word "خَوَّلنا" (khawwalnā) comes from "خَوَل" (khawal), meaning something that requires a guardian or caretaker, and it usually refers to possessions (amwāl). "خوّلناكم" (khawwalnākum) means granting ownership and bestowal.

Messages

1- On the Day of Resurrection, man is alone. "جئتمونا فُرادى"

2- The Resurrection (Ma'ād) is physical. "كما خلقناكم أوّل مرّة"

3- The polytheists relied on four things: tribe and clan, wealth and possessions, leaders and masters, and idols and deities. This verse addresses the futility of all four supports on the Day of Resurrection:

"فُرادى" (alone) – without tribe or clan.

"تَركتُم ما خَوّلناكم" (You have left behind what We bestowed upon you) – without wealth, riches, or possessions.

"ما نَرَى مَعكم شُفعاء" (We do not see with you any intercessors) – without helpers or supporters.

"ضَلّ عنكم" (They have strayed from you) – the vanishing of all imaginary powers.

4- Baseless assumptions are the foundation of many deviations. "شُفعاءكم الّذین زعمتم"

5- On the Day of Resurrection, realities become manifest and mirages vanish. "ضَلّ عنكم ما كنتم تزعمون"

Ayah

۞ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ فَالِقُ ٱلْحَبِّ وَٱلنَّوَىٰ ۖ يُخْرِجُ ٱلْحَىَّ مِنَ ٱلْمَيِّتِ وَمُخْرِجُ ٱلْمَيِّتِ مِنَ ٱلْحَىِّ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۖ فَأَنَّىٰ تُؤْفَكُونَ (۹٥)
Indeed Allah is the splitter of the grain and the pit. He brings forth the living from the dead and He brings forth the dead from the living. That is Allah! Then where do you stray? (95)

Key Points

Fāliq (فالق) is derived from falq (فلق), meaning to split or cleave. Ḥabb (حَبّ) and ḥabbah (حَبّه) refer to edible and nutritional seeds, such as wheat and barley. Nawā (نَوى) means pit or kernel (stone). From lifeless grass, a living animal emerges, and from a living animal, lifeless milk appears. From a lifeless pit, a living tree emerges, and from a grown tree, a lifeless pit (seed) is produced. According to narrations, one of the manifestations of the Ayah is that sometimes believing individuals emerge from faithless people, and sometimes disbelieving children are born from faithful people. [419] «یخرج الحىّ مِنَ المیّت و مخرج المیّت مِنَ الحَىّ»

419) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Al-Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 5.

Messages

1- Planting the seed and the pit is the work of man, but splitting and causing growth is the work of Allah. «اِنّ اللَّه فالق الحَبّ و النوى»

2- Reflection upon natural phenomena is one of the best ways to know Allah. «فالق الحَبّ... مخرج المیّت... ذلكم اللَّه»

3- Man's sustenance and provision come through these very seeds and grains that Allah has caused to grow. So, to whom are you turning away? «فأنّى تؤفكون»

Ayah

فَالِقُ ٱلْإِصْبَاحِ وَجَعَلَ ٱلَّيْلَ سَكَنًا وَٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ حُسْبَانًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ تَقْدِيرُ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْعَلِيمِ (۹٦)
Splitter of the dawn, He has made the night for rest, and the sun and the moon for calculation. That is the ordaining of the All-mighty, the All-knowing. (96)

Key Points

Iṣbāḥ (اِصباح) means both morning and the passing of the night and entering the morning, but here, the intent is the moment the dawn breaks. In the previous Ayah, three signs of Allah's power on Earth were presented; in this Ayah, signs of Divine power in the heavens are mentioned. Night and day are two signs of Divine power that come into existence through the regular rotation of the sun and the moon. Night is for rest, and working, striving, and traveling during the night have been discouraged. [420] (Since night is considered a factor of stillness and tranquility in this Ayah, it is understood that morning is for work and effort.) Imam Riḍā عليه السلام said: Arrange marriage (ceremonies) at night, because both night and the spouse are means of stillness and tranquility for man. [421]

420) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 421) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The creation of night and day requires power and knowledge to accomplish this task with precise planning and measurement. «فالق الاصباح...تقدیر العزیز العلیم»

2- The sun and the moon are means of order, calculation, and planning. [422] «حسباناً»

3- Precise planning and complete execution require knowledge and power. «تقدیر العزیز العلیم»

4- Reflection upon the precise order of the celestial bodies is a path to knowing Allah. «انّى تؤفكون...فالق الاصباح...»

422) Perhaps from the fact that the moon and the sun are counted as means of calculation in this Ayah, one can infer that both the solar year and the lunar year are valid. And perhaps the secret of calculation and precise order is unified, similar to [the grammatical structure] "Zayd is justice" (زيد عدل) which considers Zayd to be entirely just.

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلنُّجُومَ لِتَهْتَدُوا۟ بِهَا فِى ظُلُمَـٰتِ ٱلْبَرِّ وَٱلْبَحْرِ ۗ قَدْ فَصَّلْنَا ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ (۹۷)
It is He who made the stars for you, so that you may be guided by them in the darkness of land and sea. We have certainly elaborated the signs for a people who have knowledge. (97)

Key Points

Astronomy is one of the oldest human sciences, and man has benefited from the stars during land and sea journeys. This tool is reliable, permanent, untouched, natural, universal, and cost-free. Islam pays special attention to the manifestations of nature. The names of some Surahs are derived from natural objects. Islamic worship is also intertwined with natural phenomena, such as: timekeeping, Qibla determination, lunar and solar eclipses (Khusuf and Kusuf) and the prayer of signs (Salat al-Ayat), the beginning of the month, and the crescent moon. These factors led to Muslims becoming familiar with astronomy, the establishment of observatories in Baghdad, Damascus, Cairo, Maragheh, and Andalusia, and the writing of books on this subject by Muslims. Allah has provided guides for sea and land journeys, which rarely occur in human life. Can it be accepted that He would not provide a guide for the constant movement of human beings, preventing them from getting lost on the path of truth? And this indicates the necessity of Prophethood (Risalah) and Imamate for the guidance of humanity. In the narrations (Ahadith), the guiding stars are interpreted as the infallible leaders (Ma'sumeen) and the friends of Allah (Awliya' Allah). (423)

(423) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa'iq.

Messages

1- The stars are creatures of Allah and a means of guidance (not creators and regulators, as some have imagined). «جعل لكم النّجوم لتهتدوا بها»

2- The system of the stars in the sky is so precise that earthly paths can be found using them. «لتهتدوا بها»

3- Knowing the weight, volume, distance, speed of movement, and orbits of the stars—and astronomy and celestial science in general—does not, by itself, lead man to Allah. Man must intend and desire to know Allah through this path. «لقوم یعلمون»

(Indeed, merely looking into a mirror is not enough to fix one's hair and face; the will, decision, and action to make the correction are also necessary. How many mirror sellers constantly look into mirrors but do not even straighten their own collars.)

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنشَأَكُم مِّن نَّفْسٍ وَٰحِدَةٍ فَمُسْتَقَرٌّ وَمُسْتَوْدَعٌ ۗ قَدْ فَصَّلْنَا ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَفْقَهُونَ (۹۸)
It is He who created you from a single soul, then there is the [enduring] abode and the place of temporary lodging. We have certainly elaborated the signs for a people who understand. (98)

Key Points

Two points are hidden in the expression "أنشأ" (Ansha'a): 1. Innovation (or originality) 2. Continuous nurturing. In the creation of man, there is both novelty and innovation, and continuous nurturing. Indeed, creation that is merely imitative, or creation followed by abandonment, holds little value.

Regarding the expression mustaqarr (settled/stable) and mustawda' (deposited/temporary), other meanings have also been mentioned, including: You possess a stable soul and an unstable body; you possess stable or unstable faith; you originated from the woman's nutfah (sperm/drop) which is stable in the womb, and the man's nutfah which is unstable; you are settled on the earth and deposited in the grave until the Day of Resurrection; blessings are sometimes stable and sometimes unstable.

It is narrated in a supplication from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام that after the Eid al-Ghadeer prayer, one should ask Allah to make His blessings continuous for us, because He Himself said: فمستقر و مستودع (Fa-mustaqarrun wa mustawda'un). [424]

424) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The origin and root of all of you is one, so why all this disagreement, superiority seeking, and discrimination? «من نفس واحدة»

2- All this variety and innovation in the creation of human beings from only one soul is a sign of Allah's greatness. «و هوالّذى انشاكم من نفس واحدة»

3- Not all provisions are stable. As long as blessings and opportunities exist, we should make the best use and benefit from them. «فمستقر و مستودع»

4- Unless a person possesses "understanding" (fahm), he will not benefit from divine knowledge. «قد فصّلنا الایات لقوم یفقهون»

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً فَأَخْرَجْنَا بِهِۦ نَبَاتَ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ فَأَخْرَجْنَا مِنْهُ خَضِرًا نُّخْرِجُ مِنْهُ حَبًّا مُّتَرَاكِبًا وَمِنَ ٱلنَّخْلِ مِن طَلْعِهَا قِنْوَانٌ دَانِيَةٌ وَجَنَّـٰتٍ مِّنْ أَعْنَابٍ وَٱلزَّيْتُونَ وَٱلرُّمَّانَ مُشْتَبِهًا وَغَيْرَ مُتَشَـٰبِهٍ ۗ ٱنظُرُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ ثَمَرِهِۦٓ إِذَآ أَثْمَرَ وَيَنْعِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكُمْ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ (۹۹)
It is He who sends down water from the sky, and brings forth with it every kind of growing thing. Then from it We bring forth vegetation from which We produce the grain, in clusters, and from the palm-tree, from the spathes of it, low-hanging clusters [of dates], and gardens of grapes, olives and pomegranates, similar and dissimilar. Look at its fruit as it fructifies and ripens. Indeed, there are signs in that for a people who have faith. (99)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, He stated: all human beings originate from a single soul (nafs wāhid). In this Ayah, He states: all plants, trees, and fruits also originate from a single source, which is rainwater. Mutarākib (متراكب), derived from the root rukūb (ركوب), refers to fruits piled upon one another or seeds pressed closely together. Ṭal‘ (طلع) is the unopened spathe of the date palm, and qinwān (قنوان) refers to the thin strands that later form the clusters of dates. Dāniyah (دانیة) refers to clusters that are close to one another, or close to the ground due to the weight of their yield. The meaning of mutashābih (متشابه) is either trees that have a superficial resemblance to one another, such as the olive and the pomegranate, or fruits that resemble each other. In this Ayah and the two preceding Ayahs, three consecutive phrases have appeared: «لقوم یعلمون», «لقوم یفقهون», and «لقوم یؤمنون». The secret behind this is explained in Message 4.

Messages

1- The source for the growth of all plants and vegetation is rainwater. «من السماء ماءً فأخرجنا به نبات كلّ شى ء»

2- Both the sending down of rain and the causing of plants and fruits to grow are the work of Allah. «أنزل... نخرج»

3- The relationship between humans and fruits should not be merely material and nutritional; it should also be intellectual and monotheistic. «اُنظروا الى ثمره... فى ذلكم لایات»

4- Benefiting from the stars requires knowledge and expertise. «لقوم یعلمون». Understanding that the human caravan originates entirely from a single soul, and that this caravan is in motion, both permanently and temporarily, requires deep comprehension. «لقوم یفقهون». Understanding that spiritual benefit must be derived from plants, rain, and fruits, and that one must not be heedless of attributing all existence to Allah, requires faith. «لقوم یؤمنون»

Ayah

وَجَعَلُوا۟ لِلَّهِ شُرَكَآءَ ٱلْجِنَّ وَخَلَقَهُمْ ۖ وَخَرَقُوا۟ لَهُۥ بَنِينَ وَبَنَـٰتٍۭ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ ۚ سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ وَتَعَـٰلَىٰ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ (۱٠٠)
They make the jinn partners of Allah, though He has created them, and carve out sons and daughters for Him, without any knowledge. Immaculate is He and exalted above what they allege [concerning Him]! (100)

Key Points

Khalaq (creation) is the calculated creation of something, while Kharaq (tearing/piercing) is the reckless and uncalculated tearing of something. Kharaqū means expressing a matter without precision or study, or fabricating something without calculation, or making an unfounded claim. [425] According to the Quran, Christians considered Jesus (عليه السلام) and Jews considered Ezra (عليه السلام) to be the son of God. [426] A group also considered the angels to be the daughters of God. Zoroastrians considered Ahriman, who was from the jinn, to be God's rival and attributed all evils to him. Some Arabs also believed in a familial relationship between Allah and the jinn. «جعلوا بینه و بین الجنّة نَسَباً» [427] This Ayah invalidates all those assumptions and states: Allah is exalted and far above all these descriptions.

425) Tafsir Nemooneh. 426) Tawbah, 30. 427) Saffat, 158.

Messages

1- Ignorance is the root of superstition. «خرقوا ... بغیر علم» (Human beliefs regarding Allah must be based on knowledge.)

2- How can the created become a partner to the Creator? «شركاء... و خلقهم»

3- Marriage and procreation fulfill a deficiency and imperfection, and Allah is exalted above such flaws. «سبحانه»

Ayahs

بَدِيعُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ أَنَّىٰ يَكُونُ لَهُۥ وَلَدٌ وَلَمْ تَكُن لَّهُۥ صَـٰحِبَةٌ ۖ وَخَلَقَ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ ۖ وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌ (۱٠۱)
The originator of the heavens and the earth—how could He have a child when He has had no spouse? He created all things and He has knowledge of all things. (101)
ذَٰلِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ ۖ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ خَـٰلِقُ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ فَٱعْبُدُوهُ ۚ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ وَكِيلٌ (۱٠۲)
That is Allah, your Lord, there is no god except Him, the creator of all things; so worship Him. He watches over all things. (102)

Key Points

The word Badī‘ (بدیع) means creating innovatively (not imitatively). He who created the heavens and the earth without imitation or a prior plan, what need does He have for a son or a spouse? He creates whatever He wills with a single command. Khāliqu kulli shay’ (خالق كلّ شى ء) is the Oneness (Tawhid) in creation, and Huwa ‘alā kulli shay’in Wakīl (هو على كلّ شى وكیل) is the Oneness (Tawhid) in command/authority. Lahu al-khalqu wa al-amr (له الخلق والامر) [428].

428) Al-A‘rāf, 54.

Messages

1- The Creator of the universe is powerful and has no need for a spouse or a son. Badī‘u as-samāwāt (بدیع السموات)

2- Compare the God introduced by the Quran with the God believed in by others. Dhālikumu Allāh (ذلكم اللَّه)

3- In Islamic belief, the Creator and the Lord (Sustainer) are one. Rabbukum... Khāliqu kulli shay’ (ربّكم... خالق كلّ شى ء) (However, polytheists consider the Creator to be Allah, but they believe in multiple lords/sustainers.)

4- The absolute Creatorship of Allah is the proof of Tawhid (Oneness). Lā ilāha illā Huwa Khāliqu kulli shay’ (لا اله الاّ هو خالق كلّ شى ء)

5- Both creation is by the hand of Allah, and the survival and stability of everything are by His will. Khāliq - Wakīl (خالق - وكیل)

6- The Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) and Creatorship (Khāliqiyyah) of Allah are the philosophy behind worship. Rabbukum... Khāliqu kulli shay’in fa-‘budūh (ربّكم... خالق كلّ شى ء فاعبدوه)

Ayah

لَّا تُدْرِكُهُ ٱلْأَبْصَـٰرُ وَهُوَ يُدْرِكُ ٱلْأَبْصَـٰرَ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱللَّطِيفُ ٱلْخَبِيرُ (۱٠۳)
The sights do not apprehend Him, yet He apprehends the sights, and He is the All-attentive, the All-aware. (103)

Key Points

The word baṣar (بَصَر) means both "eye" and "insight" (or "discernment"). [429] Therefore, the ayah can be interpreted as follows: Neither the physical eye sees Allah, nor does the heart find a path to the depth of His knowledge. Laṭīf (لطیف) has several meanings: 1. He who considers His own gift small and the obedience of people great. 2. Precise, secretly attending to affairs, and subtle discernment. 3. The Creator of delicate and unseen things. 4. Gentle and friendly. 5. The Rewarder of the faithful and the Forgiver of the harsh. Imam Reza عليه السلام said: "لایقع الاوهام و لایُدرك كیف هو" (Fantasies do not encompass Him, and how He is cannot be comprehended). Thoughts and illusions do not encompass Allah, and His Essence, as He truly is, is not comprehensible. [430] Allah is never visible. When Prophet Moses, speaking on behalf of the people, requested to see God, he received the reply: "لَن تَرانِى" [431] (You shall never see Me). Some of the Ahl al-Sunnah (Sunnis) have said that God will be seen on the Day of Judgment, citing ayāt such as "الى ربّها ناظِرَة" [432] (Looking toward their Lord). [433] They are unaware that Allah is not a body or matter, and the intent of looking at Allah is looking with the eye of the heart, because seeing with the eye occurs only when something has a body, location, limitation, color, etc., and all of these are signs of need and weakness, from which Allah is free of such imperfections. Imam Ali عليه السلام says: Not only does the eye not see Him, but imagination and thought cannot encompass Him either. [434] Sa'di says in depicting this matter:

O You who are higher than imagination, analogy, conjecture, and illusion, And higher than what they have said, we have heard, and we have read. The gathering ended and life reached its close, Yet we remain at the beginning of describing You.

The intent of the phrase "یدرك الابصار" (He comprehends the sights) is His knowledge of human vision, similar to Samī‘ (Hearing) and Baṣīr (Seeing), which mean His knowledge of what is heard and what is seen.

429) Mufradāt al-Rāghib. 430) Tafsīr Burhān. 431) Al-A‘rāf, 143. 432) Al-Qiyāmah, 23. 433) Tafsīr al-Manār. 434) Tafsīr Ṣāfī.

Messages

1- Allah is not a material body. "لاتدركه الابصار" (Vision comprehends Him not).

2- No one is aware of the Essence of Allah. "هو اللطیف" (He is the Subtle).

3- No veil or barrier prevents Allah's knowledge. "هواللطیف الخبیر" (He is the Subtle, the All-Aware).

4- Despite knowing our faults, He still shows kindness to us. "وهو اللطیف الخبیر" (And He is the Subtle, the All-Aware).

5- Allah is aware of all the subtleties and secrets of existence. "هواللطیف الخبیر" (He is the Subtle, the All-Aware).

Ayah

قَدْ جَآءَكُم بَصَآئِرُ مِن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ فَمَنْ أَبْصَرَ فَلِنَفْسِهِۦ ۖ وَمَنْ عَمِىَ فَعَلَيْهَا ۚ وَمَآ أَنَا۠ عَلَيْكُم بِحَفِيظٍ (۱٠٤)
[Say,] ‘Certainly insights have come to you from your Lord. So whoever sees, it is to the benefit of his own soul, and whoever remains blind, it is to its detriment, and I am not a keeper over you.’ (104)

Key Points

From Ayah 95 until here, the discussion focused on introducing Allah and criticizing polytheism (Shirk). This Ayah serves as the conclusion and summary of the previous verses. There are many similar verses in the Quran which attribute the result of faith and disbelief, good and evil, or human insight and blindness, back to the person himself. Such as: * «لها ما كَسَبَت وعلیها ما اكتسبت» [435] * «مَن عَمِلَ صالحاً فلنفسه ومَن اَساء فَعَلیها» [436] * «اِن أحسنتُم أحسنتُم لانفُسِكم و اِن أساتُم فلها» [437]

435) Baqarah, 286. 436) Fussilat, 46. 437) Isra, 7.

Messages

1- With the revelation of the Quran, no path for excuse remains for anyone. «قد جاءكم بصائر»

2- People are free in choosing their path. «مَن أبصر ... و مَن عمى»

3- Informing people is among the functions of Divine Lordship (Rububiyyah). «بصائر من ربّكم»

4- The benefit of people's faith and the harm of their disbelief return to themselves, not to Allah. «فلنفسه... فعلیها»

5- The punishment of a group of people is not a sign that the teachings of the prophets are false, but rather a sign of their own blindness. «و مَن عمى»

6- The duty of the Prophet is conveyance (of the message), not compulsion. «ما أنا علیكم بحفیظ»

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ نُصَرِّفُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ وَلِيَقُولُوا۟ دَرَسْتَ وَلِنُبَيِّنَهُۥ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ (۱٠٥)
Thus do We paraphrase the signs variously, lest they should say, ‘You have received instruction,’ and so that We may make it clear for a people who have knowledge. (105)

Key Points

The disbelievers, in order to open the way for denying the Prophet, would say: These words are not his own; he has learned them from someone else. «دَرَسْتَ» Just as they would say in other instances: «انّما یُعلِّمُه بَشَر» (438) A human taught him, «أعانَه عَلیهِ قَومٌ آخَرون» (439) Other groups have helped him.

(438) Nahl (The Bee), 103. (439) Furqan (The Criterion), 4.

Messages

1- Diversity in the method of expression, argumentation, and teaching opens the way for greater acceptance. «نصرّف الآیات»

2- The opponents admit the importance of the Quran and say: He learned these teachings from someone, and they do not say that the words lack content. «دَرَسْتَ»

3- In the face of Divine verses, some resist with slander and denial. «دَرَسْتَ», but some who are worthy are guided. «لقوم یعلمون»

The rain, whose nature's gentleness is undisputed, causes tulips to grow in the garden and weeds in the barren land.

Ayah

ٱتَّبِعْ مَآ أُوحِىَ إِلَيْكَ مِن رَّبِّكَ ۖ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ وَأَعْرِضْ عَنِ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (۱٠٦)
Follow that which has been revealed to you from your Lord, there is no god except Him, and turn away from the polytheists. (106)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the accusation of the polytheists against the Prophet was mentioned, where they claimed: Your speech is not revelation, but rather learned from others. Here, He consoles the Prophet, telling him to carry on with his work and pay no attention to the words of the opponents. The methods of dealing with opponents vary in different situations, including: sometimes it is greeting them (saying peace). «اذا خاطَبَهُمُ الجاهلون قالوا سلاماً» (440) Sometimes it is silence and non-engagement. «ذَرهُم» (441), «حَسبُنا اللّه» (442) Sometimes it is turning away and ignoring them. «أعرِض عنهم» (443) Sometimes it is harshness and severity. «و اغلُظ علیهم» (444) Sometimes it is retaliation in kind. «فاْعتَدوا علیه بِمِثل مَا اعتدى علیكم» (445)

440) Al-Furqan, 63. 441) Al-Hijr, 3. 442) Aal-i-Imran, 173. 443) Al-Ma'idah, 42. 444) At-Tawbah, 73. 445) Al-Baqarah, 194.

Messages

1- Divine leaders should not be weakened in their path by the accusations, insults, and unjust analyses of the enemies. «اِتّبع ما یوحى الیك»

2- Sometimes man falls so low that even the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, by divine command, turns away from him. «وأعرض عن المشركین»

Ayah

وَلَوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ مَآ أَشْرَكُوا۟ ۗ وَمَا جَعَلْنَـٰكَ عَلَيْهِمْ حَفِيظًا ۖ وَمَآ أَنتَ عَلَيْهِم بِوَكِيلٍ (۱٠۷)
Had Allah wished they would not have ascribed partners [to Him]. We have not made you a caretaker for them, nor is it your duty to watch over them. (107)

Key Points

The Will and Desire of Allah are of two types: Legislative Will (Tashrī‘ī) and Creative Will (Takwīnī). From the legislative perspective, Allah desires the guidance of all people, and thus He sent the prophets and the heavenly books. But from the creative perspective, He willed that people choose the path based on their own will and free choice, and not be compelled to accept the religion.

Messages

1- The Will and Desire of Allah cannot be violated. «لو شاءاللَّه ما اشركوا»

2- The Divine Will and Desire is based on leaving human beings free, and the existence of polytheists is a sign of this freedom. «ولو شاء اللَّه ما اشركوا»

3- The Divine Prophets are trainers and teachers of the people, not jailers who issue commands through force and compulsion. «ما جعلناك علیهم حفیظاً»

4- The Prophet is neither responsible for warding off calamity from the polytheists nor responsible for attracting benefit for them. «ما جعلناك علیهم حفیظاً و ما انت علیهم بوكیل»

5- Prophets guarantee the duty (performance of the mission), not the result. Despite all that effort, a large group remained polytheists. «و ما أنت علیهم بوكیل»

6- The Prophet felt deep anguish for the guidance of the people. «و ما أنت علیهم بوكیل»

Ayah

وَلَا تَسُبُّوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ فَيَسُبُّوا۟ ٱللَّهَ عَدْوًۢا بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ زَيَّنَّا لِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ عَمَلَهُمْ ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِم مَّرْجِعُهُمْ فَيُنَبِّئُهُم بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۱٠۸)
Do not abuse those whom they invoke besides Allah, lest they should abuse Allah out of hostility, without any knowledge. That is how to every people We have made their conduct seem decorous. Then their return will be to their Lord and He will inform them concerning what they used to do. (108)

Key Points

Allah has created man in such a way that both good deeds and bad deeds gradually affect his soul and he becomes accustomed to them. For this reason, the adornment of deeds is sometimes attributed to Allah, «زَیَّنّا» (We adorned), as He is the Creator of this effect, and sometimes it is attributed to Satan, who makes ugly things appear beautiful through temptation (waswasa) and promises. «زیّن لهم الشّیطان» (Satan made them fair-seeming) [446] Imam Ali عليه السلام advised his soldiers during the Battle of Siffin not to curse or insult the followers of Mu'awiyah, but rather to discuss their characteristics (or true nature), which is better than cursing. [447] Amir al-Mu'minin Ali عليه السلام said: Cursing the Wali (Guardian/Friend) of Allah is among the instances of cursing Allah. [448] The matter of la'n (cursing/imprecation), nafrin (condemnation), and bara'at (disassociation) is separate from the matter of dushnam (insult) and nasaza (abuse). La'n and bara'at, which have been declared by Allah in other verses of the Quran, are in reality an expression of our stance against oppressors and polytheists, whereby we declare our disassociation from their path. «لعنة اللّه على الظالمین» (The curse of Allah is upon the wrongdoers) [449], «برائة من اللَّه...» (Disassociation from Allah...) [450]

446) An'am, 43. 447) Nahj al-Balaghah, Saying 206. 448) Tafsir al-Mizan. 449) Hud, 18. 450) Tawbah, 1.

Messages

1- We must control our emotions towards opponents and refrain from all forms of foul language and abuse. «لاتسبّوا»

2- We must pay attention to the repercussions of our actions. «لاتسبّوا... فیسبّوااللَّه»

3- People cannot be deterred from the wrong path through cursing and abuse. «لا تسبّوا»

4- Abuse is a factor leading to enmity, hatred, and receiving abuse in return. «لاتسبّوا... فیسبّوا... عَدْواً»

5- Cursing is a sign of lacking either logic, etiquette, or patience. (By refraining from abuse, a Muslim must demonstrate that he possesses patience, logic, and etiquette.) «لا تسبّوا»

6- Any action that leads to the insult of sacred things is forbidden (Haram). «فیسبّوا اللَّه»

7- Using the weapon of cursing and abuse is forbidden in Nahy an al-Munkar (forbidding the wrong), propagation, and debates. «لاتسبّوا»

8- An action that causes others to be drawn into sin and forbidden acts is forbidden (Haram). [451] «لاتسبّوا... فیسبّوا»

9- Sometimes a person unintentionally becomes a partner in the sins of others, specifically when he provides the preliminaries for the sins of others. «فیسبّوااللَّه»

10- It is among the Divine traditions that the deeds of nations appear beautiful in their own eyes. «زیّنا لكلّ اُمّة عملهم» (Every nation considers its own deeds beautiful, even if they are false.)

11- Appearance and seeing something as beautiful are not signs of truthfulness (Haqqaniyyah). «زیّنا لكل اُمّة عملهم»

12- Informing people of their deeds on the Day of Judgment is among the functions of Divine Lordship (Rububiyyah). «الى ربّهم مرجعهم فینبّهم...»

451) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Ayah

وَأَقْسَمُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمَـٰنِهِمْ لَئِن جَآءَتْهُمْ ءَايَةٌ لَّيُؤْمِنُنَّ بِهَا ۚ قُلْ إِنَّمَا ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتُ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَمَا يُشْعِرُكُمْ أَنَّهَآ إِذَا جَآءَتْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۱٠۹)
They swear by Allah with solemn oaths that were a sign to come to them they would surely believe in it. Say, ‘These signs are only from Allah,’ and what will bring home to you that they will not believe even if they came? (109)

Key Points

A group of the disbelievers of Quraish came to the Prophet and said: You too, like Moses and Jesus, should bring miracles so that we may believe in you. The Prophet asked: What should I do? They said: Turn Mount Safa into gold, resurrect our dead, show us God and the angels, and then they swore that in that case, they would believe. The Ayah was revealed that the miracle is in the hand of Allah and is performed according to wisdom, not the whims of the people. [452] The miracle is necessary to the extent of completing the proof (Hujjah), not to the extent of satisfying the desires of every individual. Furthermore, some suggestions, like seeing God, are contrary to reason, and the system of existence is not a plaything for the whims of the polytheists.

452) Tafasir Majma' al-Bayan and Namuneh.

Messages

1- The polytheists of Mecca accepted "Allah" and swore by Him. «أقسموا باللَّه»

2- Do not be deceived by the false oaths of the opponents. «واقسموا باللَّه جهد ایمانهم»

3- Seeking proof is acceptable, but submitting to whims is never acceptable. «انّما الآیات عنداللَّه»

4- The miracles of the prophets are in the hand of Allah. «انّما الآیات عنداللَّه»

5- Obstinacy is an incurable ailment; its possessors will still not believe, even after seeing every kind of miracle. «لایؤمنون»

Ayah

وَنُقَلِّبُ أَفْـِٔدَتَهُمْ وَأَبْصَـٰرَهُمْ كَمَا لَمْ يُؤْمِنُوا۟ بِهِۦٓ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ وَنَذَرُهُمْ فِى طُغْيَـٰنِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ (۱۱٠)
We transform their hearts and their visions as they did not believe in it the first time, and We leave them bewildered in their rebellion. (110)

Messages

1- The inversion of the hearts of stubborn individuals is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah. «و نُقَلِّبُ أفئدتهم»

2- Faith in Allah and the prophethood requires a sound heart. «و نُقَلِّب أفئدتهم»

3- Sin and stubbornness transform human sight and insight. «و نُقَلِّبُ أفئدتهم»

4- Whoever has not believed after seeing the initial miracles should not request other miracles. «لم یؤمنوا به اوّل مَرَّة»

5- Although Allah is the "Turner of Hearts" (Muqallib al-Qulūb), it is the human being who prepares the ground for transformation by choosing faith or disbelief. «نُقَلِّبُ ... كما لم یؤمنوا»

6- Transgression is the root of disbelief. «لم یؤمنوا ... طغیانهم»

7- Whoever Allah abandons becomes bewildered. «نذرهم... یعمهون»

Ayah

۞ وَلَوْ أَنَّنَا نَزَّلْنَآ إِلَيْهِمُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةَ وَكَلَّمَهُمُ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ وَحَشَرْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ قُبُلًا مَّا كَانُوا۟ لِيُؤْمِنُوٓا۟ إِلَّآ أَن يَشَآءَ ٱللَّهُ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ يَجْهَلُونَ (۱۱۱)
Even if We had sent down angels to them, and the dead had spoken to them, and We had gathered before them all things manifestly, they would [still] not believe unless Allah wished. But most of them are ignorant. (111)

Key Points

It seems that one of the demands of the polytheists was the descent of angels and the dead speaking to them, oblivious to the fact that these polytheists are so obstinate that even if unseen matters, such as angels, were made tangible for them, they still would not believe.

Sa'di also says: When the essence of a jewel is receptive, Training will have an effect on it. No polish can beautify Iron that is of bad essence. Do not wash a dog in seven seas; When it gets wet, it becomes fouler. If you take the donkey of Jesus to Mecca, When it returns, it is still a donkey.

"قُبُل" (Qubul) is either in the meaning of "in front of" (muqābil) or the plural of "qabīl," meaning "group" or "category."

In the two preceding verses, the false claim of the polytheists was mentioned, where they said: If a miracle comes, we will believe. Here, an example of those miracles is presented.

Similar to this verse, in Surah Al-Hijr, verses 14 and 15 state: «ولو فَتَحنا علیهم باباً من السّماء فظلّوا فیه یعرجون لقالوا انّما سُكِّرَت أبصارُنا بل نحن قوم مَسحورون» (And even if We opened to them a gate from the heaven, and they continued ascending through it, they would surely say, "Our eyes have only been intoxicated [or dazzled]; rather, we are a people affected by magic.") If a door were opened from the sky and the disbelievers ascended through it, they would still say: Our eyes have been bewitched.

Messages

1- For obstinate hearts, no sign or evidence will pave the way for faith. «ما كانوا لیؤمنوا»

2- If Allah wills, He can compel everyone to believe, but this is contrary to His wisdom. «ما كانوا لیؤمنوا الاّ أن یشاء اللّه»

3- Ignorance is the factor preventing belief in divine signs. «ما كانوا لیؤمنوا... و لكنّ اكثرهم یجهلون»

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ جَعَلْنَا لِكُلِّ نَبِىٍّ عَدُوًّا شَيَـٰطِينَ ٱلْإِنسِ وَٱلْجِنِّ يُوحِى بَعْضُهُمْ إِلَىٰ بَعْضٍ زُخْرُفَ ٱلْقَوْلِ غُرُورًا ۚ وَلَوْ شَآءَ رَبُّكَ مَا فَعَلُوهُ ۖ فَذَرْهُمْ وَمَا يَفْتَرُونَ (۱۱۲)
That is how for every prophet We appointed as enemy the devils from among humans and jinn, who inspire each other with flashy words, deceptively. Had your Lord wished, they would not have done it. So leave them with what they fabricate, (112)

Key Points

In the two previous verses, it was stated that: We leave them to their own state, «نذرهم فى طغیانهم یعمهون» (We leave them to wander blindly in their transgression). Here He says: You too leave them to their own state. «فذرهم» (So leave them). The fall of man begins with accepting the whispers of Satan, «یُوَسوِسُ فى صُدُورِ النّاس» (who whispers into the hearts of mankind) [453]. Then, whoever is affected by the whisper becomes a companion of Satan, «اِخوان الشیاطین» (brothers of the devils) [454], and finally, he himself becomes a devil. «شیاطین الانس» (devils among mankind). Conflict and struggle are the groundwork for voluntary perfection. For this reason, Allah has made the world a realm of voluntary friction and opposition. «وكذلك»

453) Naas, 5. 454) Isra, 27.

Messages

1- Among the factors for human steadfastness and perseverance is awareness of history and the difficulties faced by others. «وكذلك»

2- The struggle between truth and falsehood has existed throughout history. «وكذلك»

3- The leader of the community must be prepared for opposition. «وكذلك»

4- We should not expect opponents and ill-wishers to be eradicated. «وكذلك جعلنا»

5- It is among the Divine traditions that Prophets encounter various opponents. «و كذلك جعلنا لكلّ نبىٍ ّ عَدوّا»

6- Obstinate disbelievers are devils. «شیاطین الانس...»

7- The devils among mankind and jinn communicate with each other through inspiration (or suggestion). «یوحى بعضهم الى بعض...»

8- The devils teach the methods of devilry to one another. «یوحى بعضهم الى بعض»

9- The opponents of the Prophets engage in internal group propaganda. «یوحى بعضهم الى بعض»

10- Beautiful and deceptive words can be factors in misleading man. «یوحى... زخرف القول غرورا»

11- Allah created man free and made the world a field of conflicts. «ولو شاء ربّك ما فعلوه»

12- Human free will and freedom are in the path of his growth and training. «و لو شاء ربّك ما فعلوه»

13- In the system of existence, one Will is dominant, not two powers; Yazdan (God) and Ahriman (Devil) are not opposed to each other, and deviant efforts are not outside the power and dominion of Allah. «ولو شاء ربك ما فعلوه»

Ayah

وَلِتَصْغَىٰٓ إِلَيْهِ أَفْـِٔدَةُ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ وَلِيَرْضَوْهُ وَلِيَقْتَرِفُوا۟ مَا هُم مُّقْتَرِفُونَ (۱۱۳)
so that toward it may incline the hearts of those who do not believe in the Hereafter, and so that they may be pleased with it and commit what they commit. (113)

Key Points

«تَصغى» (taṣghā) is derived from «صَغْو» (ṣaghw), meaning inclination, an inclination that results from hearing. «اِقتراف» (iqtirāf) means obtaining or acquiring something.

Messages

1- Temptations and propaganda are not the sole causes of deviation; rather, listening, giving one's heart, and being absorbed are effective. «لتصغى ... لیرضوه»

2- Lack of faith in the Hereafter is the cause of Satan's control and influence. «لایؤمنون بالاخرة»

Ayah

أَفَغَيْرَ ٱللَّهِ أَبْتَغِى حَكَمًا وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنزَلَ إِلَيْكُمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ مُفَصَّلًا ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ يَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّهُۥ مُنَزَّلٌ مِّن رَّبِّكَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۖ فَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمُمْتَرِينَ (۱۱٤)
[Say,] ‘Shall I seek a judge other than Allah, while it is He who has sent down to you the Book, well-elaborated?’ Those whom We have given the Book know that it has been sent down from your Lord with the truth; so do not be one of the skeptics. (114)

Key Points

Some have considered Hakam (حَكَم) to be the same as Hakim (حاكم), but others have said: Hakam is an arbiter whom the two parties in a dispute have chosen, whereas Hakim is any kind of judge. [455] In Majma' al-Bayan, it is stated: Hakam is one who judges justly (by the truth), but Hakim is applied to any judge (Qadi). In Ayah 111, the discussion was that even if the dead spoke to them or angels descended, they would not believe. In this Ayah, He says: The People of the Book know that the Qur'an is revelation, and their request for other miracles is merely an excuse. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله has no doubt about his path; therefore, the address "لا تكوننّ مِنَ المُمترین" (be not among the doubters) is a warning to the Muslims not to worry about the truthfulness of their path.

455) Tafsir Namuneh and Al-Manar.

Messages

1- Given the existence of a Book like the Qur'an, resorting to the judgment of others is incorrect and reprehensible. "أفغَیر اللَّه اَبتَغِى حَكَماً" (No one besides Allah has the right to legislate or judge.)

2- Allah has sent down the Qur'an in detail. "هو الّذى انزل الیكم الكتاب مفصّلاً"

3- The laws and rulings of Islam have no ambiguity. "مفصّلاً"

4- One of the proofs of the truthfulness of Islam is the glad tidings given by previous prophets in the Torah and the Gospel. "الّذین آتیناهم الكتاب یعلمون انّه منزّل من ربّك" Just as He says in another Ayah: "یَعرِفونَه كما یَعرفُون ابنائهم" [456] The People of the Book recognized the Prophet of Islam just as they recognized their own sons.

5- A leader must possess unquestionable resolve in the path of his call. "فلاتكوننّ..."

6- Do not let doubt enter your hearts because the People of the Book do not believe, for they know well the truthfulness of the Qur'an, and their denial is due to obstinacy. "یعلمون انّه منزّل من ربّك بالحقّ فلا تكوننّ من الممترین"

456) Al-Baqarah, 146; Al-An'am, 20.

Ayah

وَتَمَّتْ كَلِمَتُ رَبِّكَ صِدْقًا وَعَدْلًا ۚ لَّا مُبَدِّلَ لِكَلِمَـٰتِهِۦ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (۱۱٥)
The word of your Lord has been fulfilled in truth and justice. Nothing can change His words, and He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing. (115)

Key Points

The meaning of Kalimah (كلمة) in this Ayah, due to the preceding Ayah, refers to the verses of the Quran. However, the word Kalimah (كلمة) in the culture of the Quran and narrations sometimes means a definite promise, «و تمّت كلمة ربّك الحسنى على بنى اسرائیل بما صبروا» [457] which describes the fulfillment of the Divine promise to the Children of Israel because of their patience. Sometimes it means religion, Kalimatullah (كلمة اللّه) meaning the religion of God. And sometimes it is applied to the Prophet and the Divine Awliya (saints/guardians), as we read concerning Prophet Isa (Jesus) عليه السلام: «كلمته القاها الى مریم» [458]. In narrations, it is also reported from the Imams علیهم السلام that "We are the Divine Words." «نحن الكلمات» [459]

457) Al-A‘raf, 137. 458) An-Nisa, 171. 459) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 24, p. 174.

Messages

1- The Quran is the last Divine Book and Islam is the last Divine religion. «تمّت كلمة ربّك»

2- The Quran is a complete miracle proving the prophethood of the Noble Prophet. «تمّت كلمة ربك»

3- The Quran answers all the guidance needs of human societies. «تمّت كلمة ربّك»

4- Islam is the complement to past religions. «تمّت كلمة ربّك...»

5- The completion of religion through the mission of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله is a manifestation of Allah’s Lordship (Rububiyyah). «و تمّت كلمة ربّك»

6- The basis of Allah’s laws and traditions in the universe is truth and justice. «و تمّت كلمة ربّك صدقاً و عدلاً»

7- The reports of the Quran are truthful, and its rulings are based on justice. «و تمّت كلمة ربّك صدقاً و عدلاً» In another Ayah, we read: «و مَن اصدق من اللّه حدیثاً» [460]

8- Distortion has no way into the Quran. «لا مبدّل لكلماته»

9- Allah knows the needs very well and has revealed the Quran in consideration of them. «و هو السّمیع العلیم»

460) An-Nisa, 87.

Ayah

وَإِن تُطِعْ أَكْثَرَ مَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ يُضِلُّوكَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِن يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا ٱلظَّنَّ وَإِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا يَخْرُصُونَ (۱۱٦)
If you obey most of those on the earth, they will lead you astray from the way of Allah. They follow nothing but conjectures and they do nothing but surmise. (116)

Key Points

The word «خَرْص» (khars) means estimation and conjecture. Since some estimations turn out to be incorrect, it has also come to mean falsehood. [461]

461) Tafsir-e Nemooneh (Model Commentary).

Messages

1- The path of guidance and the path of the Quran are the criteria, not the path of the people or the majority. «و تمّت كلمة ربّك... و اِن تُطِع اكثر...»

2- The majority is not proof of righteousness. The criterion is the truth, not the number, so do not fear the small number of people when traversing the path of truth. «اِن تُطِع اكثر...»

3- Gaining the approval of the majority sometimes results in the destruction and deviation of the person himself. «اِن تطع اكثر... یضلّوك» (We must move on the path of Allah even if it is against the opinion of the majority.)

4- The majority has such an appeal that Allah warns even the Prophet against following it. «واِن تُطِع اكثر مَن فى الارض یضلّوك» (Prophets must follow revelation, not the opinions of the people.)

5- The root of deviations is reliance on conjecture and speculation. «یضلّوك - اِن یتّبعون الاّ الظنّ»

6- A majority that pursues conjecture and desire instead of the truth is not worthy of being followed. «اِن تطع اكثر... یضلّوك... یخرصون»

7- In choosing a path, proof and evidence are necessary, not conjecture and speculation. «اِن یتّبعون الاّ الظنّ... یخرصون»

Ayah

إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ مَن يَضِلُّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ ۖ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِٱلْمُهْتَدِينَ (۱۱۷)
Indeed your Lord knows best those who stray from His way; and He knows best those who are guided. (117)

Key Points

"Mahdi" (مَهدىّ) means one who has attained Divine guidance from the beginning, such as the infallible Imams علیهم السلام. However, "Muhtadi" (مُهتَدى) is one who has been guided after having gone astray (misguidance). [462]

462) Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan.

Messages

  1. Mankind also possesses knowledge, but only God is the Most Knowing (A'lam). «هو اعلم» (The pronoun "هو" [He] between the subject and the predicate indicates exclusivity/restriction.)

  2. Upbringing (or Training) requires deep awareness. «ربّك...اعلم»

  3. Let us not deceive ourselves and others through pretense and hypocrisy, for God knows everyone better. «هو أعلم مَن یَضلّ...»

  4. We should follow the Most Knowing (A'lam), not the majority. «و اِن تُطِع اكثر یضلّوك... انّ ربّك هو اعلم»

Ayah

فَكُلُوا۟ مِمَّا ذُكِرَ ٱسْمُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ إِن كُنتُم بِـَٔايَـٰتِهِۦ مُؤْمِنِينَ (۱۱۸)
Eat from that over which Allah’s Name has been mentioned, if you are believers in His signs. (118)

Key Points

Mentioning the name of Allah during the slaughter of an animal is a form of safeguarding material possessions and manifesting faith. Remembering God is good in every task, but it becomes necessary during the slaughter of an animal, where taking its life is involved. One of the signs of the comprehensiveness of the school of Islam is that, even in a minor issue like slaughter, it has addressed all its aspects: the doctrine (the name of God), the tool (iron), the method of cutting the veins, the community (the Muslim identity of the one who slaughters), the direction (Qibla), and the cases of consumption. "فكلوا مما ذكر اسم اللَّه"

Messages

1- The food and sustenance of the believer must have a divine orientation. «فكلوا مما ذكر اسم اللَّه»

2- The name of Allah is the seal of permission for consumption and the license for using the meat of permissible animals. «فكلوا ممّا ذكر اسم اللّه علیه...»

3- To strengthen the foundations of Monotheism (Tawhid), every opportunity, even the slaughter of an animal, must be utilized. Yes, Monotheism is not merely a mental issue. «فكلوا ممّا ذكر اسم اللّه علیه»

4- Halal (lawful) nutrition and adherence to religious rulings (Ahkam) are conditions of faith. «اِن كنتم بآیاته مؤمنین»

Ayah

وَمَا لَكُمْ أَلَّا تَأْكُلُوا۟ مِمَّا ذُكِرَ ٱسْمُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ وَقَدْ فَصَّلَ لَكُم مَّا حَرَّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِلَّا مَا ٱضْطُرِرْتُمْ إِلَيْهِ ۗ وَإِنَّ كَثِيرًا لَّيُضِلُّونَ بِأَهْوَآئِهِم بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ ۗ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِٱلْمُعْتَدِينَ (۱۱۹)
Why should you not eat that over which Allah’s Name has been mentioned, while He has already elaborated for you whatever He has forbidden you, excepting what you may be compelled to [eat in an emergency]? Indeed many mislead [others] by their fancies, without any knowledge. Indeed your Lord knows best the transgressors. (119)

Key Points

A group caused doubt and deviation by comparing slaughtered animals with naturally dead animals, saying: Why is the animal we kill lawful (halal), but what God kills (the naturally dead animal) is unlawful (haram)? This Ayah alerts the people and provides the answer to them.

Messages

  1. Anyone who makes divine lawful things unlawful for themselves without reason should be rebuked. «و ما لكم الاّ تأكلوا...» (It seems some Muslims were influenced by the traditions of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyya) and made lawful things unlawful for themselves.)

  2. Eating animal meat is lawful if it has a divine color (i.e., the name of Allah is mentioned), otherwise it is unlawful. «ذكر اسم اللَّه»

  3. All divine prohibitions have been explained in detail in the heavenly school of Islam. «و قد فصّل لكم ما حرّم علیكم» [463]

  4. The general principle and rule regarding edibles is lawfulness (halal), and whatever is unlawful (haram), Allah explains it. «فصّل لكم ما حرّم»

  5. Islamic rulings have no dead ends. «الاّ ما اضطررتم الیه»

  6. Necessity (Idtirar) removes the obligation (Taklif). «الاّ ما اضطررتم الیه»

  7. Divine obligations are commensurate with the conditions of time, place, and human capacity. «الاّ ما اضطررتم الیه»

  8. Consuming unlawful edibles is permissible in cases of necessity. «الاّ ما اضطررتم الیه»

  9. Determining what is lawful and unlawful is based on divine commands. «حرّم علیكم»

  10. Ignorance and following desires are factors leading to deviation. «لیضلّون بأهوائهم بغیر علم»

  11. Misleading people is an infringement upon the rights of society. «أعلم بالمعتدین»

463) This detailed explanation refers to Ayah 115 of Surah An-Nahl. Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Ayah

وَذَرُوا۟ ظَـٰهِرَ ٱلْإِثْمِ وَبَاطِنَهُۥٓ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَكْسِبُونَ ٱلْإِثْمَ سَيُجْزَوْنَ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَقْتَرِفُونَ (۱۲٠)
Renounce outward sins and the inward ones. Indeed those who commit sins shall be requited for what they used to commit. (120)

Key Points

Whether in the past or today, people have only feared and continue to fear manifest sin. Some have said that the intent behind hidden sins are sins whose effects are hidden, such as forbidden foods (ḥarām) which bring about hardness of the heart.

Messages

1- Sin has an attraction and pull that must be overcome with firm resolve. «و ذروا ظاهر الاثم و باطنه»

2- Islam pays attention to both outward purity (ṭahārat al-ẓāhir) and inward purity (bāṭin); one must avoid both practical sins and sins of the heart, such as suspicion (sū’ al-ẓann). «ظاهر الاثم و باطنه»

3- Divine punishment is for sins committed knowingly and intentionally. «انّ الّذین یكسبون الاثم»

4- Although Satan whispers (waswasa), man commits sin by choice (with resolve). «یكسبون الاثم»

5- The Day of Judgment (Qiyāmah) and the punishment of the Hereafter are not far off. «سیجزون»

6- Divine punishments are the result of our own actions. «بما كانوا یقترفون»

Ayah

وَلَا تَأْكُلُوا۟ مِمَّا لَمْ يُذْكَرِ ٱسْمُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ وَإِنَّهُۥ لَفِسْقٌ ۗ وَإِنَّ ٱلشَّيَـٰطِينَ لَيُوحُونَ إِلَىٰٓ أَوْلِيَآئِهِمْ لِيُجَـٰدِلُوكُمْ ۖ وَإِنْ أَطَعْتُمُوهُمْ إِنَّكُمْ لَمُشْرِكُونَ (۱۲۱)
Do not eat [anything] of that over which Allah’s Name has not been mentioned, and that is indeed transgression. Indeed the satans inspire their friends to dispute with you; and if you obey them, you will indeed be polytheists. (121)

Key Points

Since consuming unlawful (Haram) food brings hardness of heart (qasawat-e qalb) and is the ground for other sins, Islam has strictly forbidden it. In Ayah 5 of Surah Al-Ma'idah, we read that the food (ta'am) of the People of the Book is lawful (Halal) for you. This Ayah states: the meat of any animal upon which the Name of Allah has not been mentioned is unlawful (Haram). Since the People of the Book do not observe this condition, the meat of their animals is therefore unlawful for us. The intent of "food" (ta'am) in that Ayah is grains and similar items, not meat. [464] The temptation (waswasah) of the Satans is like this: they say, "The dead animal was killed by God, and what God kills is better than what man kills! So why is the dead animal unlawful, but the slaughtered animal lawful?!" They are heedless that executing the Divine command is important, and a dead animal usually carries diseases because the impure blood has not exited its body. Furthermore, dying itself is evidence of the animal being sick and unhealthy.

464) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- A Muslim must observe religious commitment even in matters of nutrition. «و لاتأكلوا ممّا لم یذكر اسم اللّه علیه»

2- Mentioning the Name of Allah during slaughter is not a formality; rather, it is a mandatory ruling, and abandoning it is transgression (Fisq). «اِنّه لَفِسق»

3- Wherever disbelief is greater, emphasis is also necessary. Because accepting the conditions for the lawfulness of slaughter was difficult for ignorant people, Allah presented the issue with several emphases. «انّه لفسق» (The nominal sentence, Inna (اِنّ), and the letter Lam (لام) indicate emphasis.)

4- Due to consuming unlawful food, man finds the ground ready for accepting satanic suggestions. «و لا تأكلوا... و انّ الشیاطین لیوحون»

5- Satans have the power of temptation (waswasah) and suggestion (ilqa'). «انّ الشیاطین لیوحون»

6- The temptations of the Satans are effective only in their allies, not in the allies of Allah. «لیوحون الى أولیائهم»

7- The basis of human disputes is temptations and desires. «لیوحون... لیجادلوكم»

8- Disputing Divine rulings is among the weapons and suggestions of Satan. «و انّ الشیاطین... لیجادلوكم»

9- Disputing religious rulings leads man to polytheism (Shirk). «لیجادلوكم... لمشركون»

10- Monotheists who are practically obedient to other than Allah are polytheists. «انّكم لمشركون»

Ayah

أَوَمَن كَانَ مَيْتًا فَأَحْيَيْنَـٰهُ وَجَعَلْنَا لَهُۥ نُورًا يَمْشِى بِهِۦ فِى ٱلنَّاسِ كَمَن مَّثَلُهُۥ فِى ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتِ لَيْسَ بِخَارِجٍ مِّنْهَا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ زُيِّنَ لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۱۲۲)
Is he who was lifeless, then We gave him life and provided him with a light by which he walks among the people, like one who dwells in a manifold darkness which he cannot leave? To the faithless is thus presented as decorous what they have been doing. (122)

Key Points

This Ayah was revealed concerning the conversion of Hadrat Hamza, the uncle of the Prophet, who, upon learning of the harassment and abuse inflicted by Abu Jahl upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله and His mission, went to Abu Jahl in defense of the Prophet and struck him on the head with his fist. He then declared: "From today, I believe in Muhammad." From that point until his martyrdom, he was a valiant and faithful officer for Islam.

In Quranic expressions, "death" (مَرگ/مَوت) is used to refer to: the stage before the sperm/zygote: «كنتم امواتاً فأحیاكم» [465]; misguidance: «أوَ مَن كان میتاً»; a barren and dry land: «فسقناه الى بلد میّت» [466]; temporary death: «فقال لهم اللّه موتوا ثمّ أحیاهم» [467]; and true death: «انّك میّت و انّهم میّتون» [468].

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The light that shows the people the path from the pit (right from wrong) is the Imam and the heavenly leader. [469]

465) Baqarah, 28. 466) Fatir, 9. 467) Baqarah, 243. 468) Zumar, 30. 469) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The true life and death of a human being are his faith and his disbelief. «میتاً فأحییناه»

2- Guidance is the work of Allah, even though man himself prepares the ground for guidance. «فأحییناه»

3- The use of analogy, questioning, and comparison is effective in propagation and education. «أومن كان... كمن مثله...»

4- The believer never reaches a dead end. He possesses both insight and movement within society. «نوراً یمشى به فى النّاس»

5- When there is no light, darknesses overwhelm man. «فى الظلمات لیس بخارج منها»

6- The Truth (Haqq) is one, but falsehood (Batil) is numerous. (The word «نور» [light] is singular, but the word «ظلمات» [darknesses] is plural.)

7- Nothing saves mankind except the light of faith and Divine guidance. «لیس بخارج منها»

8- Considering one's deeds beautiful prevents human growth and exit from darkness. «لیس بخارج منها كذلك زیّن للكافرین»

9- The deeds of the faithless person affect his perception and thought. «كذلك زیّن لكافرین ما كانوا یعملون»

10- The attractive works of the disbelievers (such as innovations, inventions, technology, and civilization) have been so embellished for them that they fail to perceive their own deviations and the downfall of their humanity. «زیّن للكافرین ما كانوا یعملون»

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ جَعَلْنَا فِى كُلِّ قَرْيَةٍ أَكَـٰبِرَ مُجْرِمِيهَا لِيَمْكُرُوا۟ فِيهَا ۖ وَمَا يَمْكُرُونَ إِلَّا بِأَنفُسِهِمْ وَمَا يَشْعُرُونَ (۱۲۳)
Thus have We installed in every town its major criminals that they may plot therein. Yet they do not plot except against their own souls, but they are not aware. (123)

Key Points

Regarding the occasion of revelation of the previous verse, the conflict and hostility of Abu Jahl against the Prophet of Islam was mentioned. This verse states: The existence of figures like Abu Jahl is not a new phenomenon; always and everywhere, in opposition to the calls of truth, such corrupt elements have existed. «و كذلك»

Messages

1- The Divine tradition (Sunnah) is to respond to the plots and schemes of the enemies. «و كذلك»

2- The efforts of the righteous and the wicked are all within the orbit of Divine power. «جعلنا... مجرمیها»

(The word «جعلنا» [We made/appointed] is used both in this verse, which relates to the criminals, and in the previous verse, which concerned the believers.)

3- Corrupt and mischievous leaders are the root of societal corruption. «اكابر مجرمیها لیمكروا فیها»

4- Plotting and deception are the weapons of mischievous leaders. «لیمكروا»

5- Losing purity and sincerity, and becoming afflicted by Divine wrath, is the greatest harm plotters inflict upon themselves. «ما یمكرون الاّ بأنفسهم»

6- Worse than illness is ignorance of the illness, and worse than plotting is not knowing that its negative consequences return upon the plotter. «ما یشعرون»

Ayah

وَإِذَا جَآءَتْهُمْ ءَايَةٌ قَالُوا۟ لَن نُّؤْمِنَ حَتَّىٰ نُؤْتَىٰ مِثْلَ مَآ أُوتِىَ رُسُلُ ٱللَّهِ ۘ ٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ حَيْثُ يَجْعَلُ رِسَالَتَهُۥ ۗ سَيُصِيبُ ٱلَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا۟ صَغَارٌ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ وَعَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌۢ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَمْكُرُونَ (۱۲٤)
When a sign comes to them, they say, ‘We will not believe until we are given the like of what was given to Allah’s apostles.’ Allah knows best where to place His apostleship! Soon the guilty will be visited by a degradation and severe punishment from Allah because of the plots they used to devise. (124)

Key Points

Regarding the occasion of the revelation (Shān Nuzūl) of this Ayah, it is said that Walid ibn Mughirah, the intellectual leader (or mastermind) of the disbelievers, used to say: Since my age and wealth are greater than Muhammad's صلى الله عليه وآله, revelation should also be sent to me. Of course, a similar statement has also been narrated from Abu Jahl. Concerning a group of the leaders of disbelief who were killed in the Battle of Badr, the divine promise was fulfilled, and humiliation reached them.

Messages

1- Powerful leaders, due to self-superiority, act arrogantly and do not accept the call of Islam. «قالوا لن نؤمن حتّى نؤتى مثل ما اوتى»

2- The criterion for divine selections is His knowledge of merits and worthiness. «اللَّه أعلم»

3- The result of arrogance against the truth is humiliation and disgrace. «لن نؤمن... سیصیب الّذین اجرموا صغار عند اللّه»

Ayah

فَمَن يُرِدِ ٱللَّهُ أَن يَهْدِيَهُۥ يَشْرَحْ صَدْرَهُۥ لِلْإِسْلَـٰمِ ۖ وَمَن يُرِدْ أَن يُضِلَّهُۥ يَجْعَلْ صَدْرَهُۥ ضَيِّقًا حَرَجًا كَأَنَّمَا يَصَّعَّدُ فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يَجْعَلُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلرِّجْسَ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۱۲٥)
Whomever Allah desires to guide, He opens his breast to Islam, and whomever He desires to lead astray, He makes his breast narrow and straitened as if he were climbing to a height. Thus does Allah lay [spiritual] defilement on those who do not have faith. (125)

Key Points

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: The signs of Sharh Sadr (expansion of the breast/soul) are several things: «الانابة الى دارالخلود والتجافى عن دار الغرور والاستعداد للموت قبل نزول الفوت» (Turning submissively towards the abode of eternity, keeping away from the abode of deception, and preparing for death before the opportunity is lost.) Attention and humble inclination towards the Hereafter, distance from the deceitful world, and preparation for death before the opportunity is lost. [470] Imam Sajjad عليه السلام also repeatedly recited a supplication on the night of the 27th of Ramadan that contained this very request. [471]

The meaning of Divine guidance (hidayah) and misguidance (dalalah) is providing the means of guidance for the deserving and removing the means [of guidance] for the undeserving. The intent of sadr (breast) is the soul and the heart. The intent of Sharh Sadr (expansion of the breast) is the broadening of the horizon of intellect and the elevation of the soul's thought, enabling the acceptance of truth and guidance, and this requires overcoming the heart's desires and cravings. He who does not possess Sharh Sadr always remains in his shell and does not emerge. The result of Sharh Sadr is insight and luminosity accompanied by a tender, truth-accepting heart.

This Ayah is one of the scientific miracles of the Quran, describing the effects of ascending into the sky. It states that whoever is unwilling to accept the truth, his soul becomes constricted and narrow in capacity, just like someone who tries to ascend into the sky, whose chest becomes tight and breathing difficult due to the heaviness and compression of the air and the lack of oxygen. Imam Reza عليه السلام said: The sign of Sa'at Sadr (broadness of the breast) is submission to Allah, having confidence in Him, and being reassured by Divine promises; and the sign of Dhiq Sadr (constriction of the breast) is doubt and anxiety. [472]

It is among the Divine traditions that He grants Sharh Sadr to pure-hearted people who accept the truth, and afflicts the stubborn ones who flee from faith with the withdrawal of Divine grace (tawfiq) and defilement (rijs). As soon as Prophet Moses was commissioned for propagation, in his very first supplication, he asked Allah: «ربّ اشرح لى صدرى» [473] But the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, without Him asking, Allah granted Him broadness of the breast. «ألم نشرح لك صدرك» [474].

470) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 471) Mafatih al-Jinan. 472) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa'iq. 473) Taha, 25. 474) Sharh, 1.

Messages

1- Accepting the truth requires inner capacity and readiness. «یشرح صدره للاسلام»

2- Sharh Sadr (expansion of the breast) is a Divine gift. «یشرح صدره للاسلام»

3- Deviating from the path of innate nature (fitrah) and intellect causes suffocation and constriction of the soul and spirit. «یصّعّد فى السماء»

4- Although the deviated may outwardly see themselves in ease and tranquility, they are in reality afflicted by constraints and pressure. «ضیّقاً حرجاً»

5- Impatience and lack of capacity are a type of defilement (rijs) and spiritual impurity. «یجعل صدره ضیّقاً... الرِّجس»

6- Whoever does not believe in the truth gradually becomes polluted. «كذلك یجعل اللَّه الرِّجس على الّذین لایؤمنون»

Ayah

وَهَـٰذَا صِرَٰطُ رَبِّكَ مُسْتَقِيمًا ۗ قَدْ فَصَّلْنَا ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَذَّكَّرُونَ (۱۲٦)
This is the straight path of your Lord. We have already elaborated the signs for a people who take admonition. (126)

Key Points

The word "هذا" (this) might refer to the word "Islam" which appeared in the previous Ayah.

Messages

1- Apart from the path of Allah, other paths are deviations. «وهذا صراط ربك مستقیماً»

2- For remembrance (admonition), detail, variety, and repetition are necessary. «فصّلنا... یذّكرون»

3- Allah has completed the proof (Hujjah) for everyone; if they are attentive, the verses and signs of truth have been clarified through various explanations. «لقوم یذكرون»

4- Constant attention to divine admonitions is the way to attain the Straight Path (Sirat al-Mustaqim). «هذا صراط ربّك... لقوم یذّكّرون»

Ayah

۞ لَهُمْ دَارُ ٱلسَّلَـٰمِ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ ۖ وَهُوَ وَلِيُّهُم بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۱۲۷)
For them shall be the abode of peace near their Lord and He will be their guardian because of what they used to do. (127)

Messages

1- In Paradise, violence, rivalry, regret, slander, envy, malice, lies, sorrow, and no form of death, sickness, or poverty have any place. «دار السّلام»

2- Superior to the blessing of security and health is being under the shade of the special grace of the Lord. «دار السّلام عند ربّهم»

3- Allah Himself takes charge of the affairs of those who follow the straight path. «و هو ولیّهم»

4- Attaining divine guardianship (Wilayah) is achieved through human action and effort. «و هو ولّیهم بما كانوا یعملون»

Ayah

وَيَوْمَ يَحْشُرُهُمْ جَمِيعًا يَـٰمَعْشَرَ ٱلْجِنِّ قَدِ ٱسْتَكْثَرْتُم مِّنَ ٱلْإِنسِ ۖ وَقَالَ أَوْلِيَآؤُهُم مِّنَ ٱلْإِنسِ رَبَّنَا ٱسْتَمْتَعَ بَعْضُنَا بِبَعْضٍ وَبَلَغْنَآ أَجَلَنَا ٱلَّذِىٓ أَجَّلْتَ لَنَا ۚ قَالَ ٱلنَّارُ مَثْوَىٰكُمْ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ إِلَّا مَا شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ ۗ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ حَكِيمٌ عَلِيمٌ (۱۲۸)
On the day He will gather them all together, [He will say], ‘O company of jinn! You claimed many of the humans.’ Their friends from among the humans will say, ‘Our Lord, we used each other, and we completed our term which You had appointed for us.’ He will say, ‘The Fire is your abode, to remain in it [forever], except what Allah may wish.’ Indeed your Lord is all-wise, all-knowing. (128)

Key Points

In the preceding verses, the deeds of the devils (Shayatin) and their whisperings were referenced. Here, He clarifies the fate of those who yield to the devils' temptations, which is the Fire (Dozakh). Moreover, according to the Quran, Satan is from the Jinn, and the Jinn who misleads people is Satan himself.

Messages

  1. We should reflect upon the outcome of our deeds. "ويوم" (Wherever the word "Yawm" [Day] is used at the beginning of an Ayah, the intent is to remind us of that Day.)

  2. The Jinn are responsible (mukallaf), possess free will, and are subject to address, reproach, punishment, and reward. "يا معشر الجنّ"

  3. Satan is not satisfied with misleading only a small group; he has deviated a large number of humans. "قد استكثرتم من الانس" In another Ayah, He says: "ولقد اضلّ منكم جبلاًّ كثیراً أفلم تكونوا تعقلون" (475)

  4. The court of the Day of Judgment is public. "يحشرهم, يا معشر الجنّ"

  5. On the Day of Judgment, the misleading Jinn will have no answer to the reproach, and only their human followers will speak. "يا معشر الجنّ... قال اولياؤهم من الانس"

  6. The utilization (or enjoyment) of one another by humans and Jinn is mutual. "استمتع بعضنا ببعض"

  7. On the Day of Judgment, humans and Jinn will be gathered and resurrected together in one place. "جميعاً"

  8. Following Satanic whisperings gradually leads man to accept his guardianship (wilayah). "اولياؤهم"

  9. Not all deviated individuals receive the same punishment, and remaining in the Fire depends on the Will of Allah. "إلاّ ماشاء اللَّه"

  10. The rulings of the Divine Court are based on knowledge and wisdom. "حكيم عليم"

(475) Ya Sin, 62.

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ نُوَلِّى بَعْضَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ بَعْضًۢا بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ (۱۲۹)
That is how We make the wrongdoers one another’s friends because of what they used to earn. (129)

Key Points

In Ayah 127, we read that the Guardian of those who follow the straight path is Allah alone. In this Ayah, we read that the guardians of a group of people are the oppressors, due to their own actions. According to narrations, the punishment for those who abandon Amr bil Ma'ruf wa Nahy 'anil Munkar (enjoining good and forbidding evil), those who fail to pay Khums and Zakat, and those who aid the oppressors, is the domination of the tyrants over them. [476] In a Hadith, we read: Whenever Allah is pleased with the actions of a people, He entrusts their affairs to the righteous, and if He is displeased, He places their affairs in the hands of the wicked. [477]

476) Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan. 477) Tafsir Kashf al-Asrar.

Messages

1- Just as the devils among the Jinn dominate humans, some oppressors also dominate others. «كذلك»

2- Unlawful gratification paves the way for false governments and the domination of oppressors. «استمتع بعضنا ببعض... نولّى بعض الظالمین بعضاً»

3- The rulers are not the only oppressors; the fearful, silent, and pleasure-seeking subjects are also oppressors. «بعض الظالمین بعضاً»

4- The cause of the tyrants' domination is the behavior of the people themselves. «نُولّى... بما كانوا یكسبون»

Ayah
