يَـٰمَعْشَرَ ٱلْجِنِّ وَٱلْإِنسِ أَلَمْ يَأْتِكُمْ رُسُلٌ مِّنكُمْ يَقُصُّونَ عَلَيْكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِى وَيُنذِرُونَكُمْ لِقَآءَ يَوْمِكُمْ هَـٰذَا ۚ قَالُوا۟ شَهِدْنَا عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِنَا ۖ وَغَرَّتْهُمُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَشَهِدُوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ أَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ كَـٰفِرِينَ (۱۳٠)
‘O company of jinn and humans! Did there not come to you apostles from yourselves, recounting to you My signs and warning you of the encounter of this Day?’ They will say, ‘We testify against ourselves.’ The life of this world had deceived them, and they will testify against themselves that they had been faithless. (130)

Key Points

It is understood from the verses of the Quran that Prophet Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله was also sent for the Jinn, and they too understand the Quran, and a group of them embrace faith. [478] According to some commentators (such as Alusi, Qurtubi, Tabarsi, and Sayyid Qutb), the messengers of the Jinn received revelation through the messengers of mankind and conveyed it to their own kind. This is because the word Rasul (messenger) is also applied to non-humans, such as: «اللَّه یصطفى من الملائكة رسلاً» [479]

In this verse, two confessions are made by the disbelievers: first, bearing witness to the coming of the prophets; second, admitting their own disbelief (kufr).

The situations (or stations) on the Day of Judgment are varied; sometimes, in one station, the disbelievers initially deny their deviation, such as in verse 23 of this very Surah: «واللَّه ربّنا ما كنّا مشركین», but when they realize that the Day of Judgment is not a place for denial, they confess their sin.

478) The first verses of Surah Al-Jinn and verse 28 of Surah Al-Ahqaf. 479) Al-Hajj, 75.

Messages

1- Both Jinn and mankind are obligated to accept the invitation of the prophets, and the messengers were sent for both species. «الجنّ والانس»

2- Warning (Inthar) is the strongest lever of education and a crucial duty of the prophets. «و ینذرونكم»

3- The strongest guarantee for the implementation of divine laws for the reform of the individual and society is faith in the Resurrection (Ma'ad). «ینذرونكم لقاء یومكم هذا»

4- The Day of Judgment is not a place for concealment or denial; therefore, people confess against themselves. «شهدوا على انفسهم»

5- Worldliness is the cause of indifference toward the invitations of the prophets. «غرّتهم الحیاة...» (Being deceived by the world leads to the forgetting of the Hereafter.)

6- Excessive attachment to the world leads man to disbelief (kufr). «غرّتهم الحیاة الدنیا... انهم كانوا كافرین»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ أَن لَّمْ يَكُن رَّبُّكَ مُهْلِكَ ٱلْقُرَىٰ بِظُلْمٍ وَأَهْلُهَا غَـٰفِلُونَ (۱۳۱)
This is because your Lord would never destroy the towns unjustly while their people were unaware. (131)

Key Points

It is the Sunnah (way) of Allah that He shows the path of truth to people by sending Prophets and various warnings, and He clarifies the realities, thereby completing the argument (Hujjah). In that case, if they disregard (the warnings), He punishes (them). This general law and Sunnah has been mentioned in several verses, including: «و ما أهلكنا من قریة الاّ لها منذرون» (480) We did not destroy any town except that its people had warners. «و ما كنّا معذّبین حتّى نبعث رسولاً» (481) We do not punish until We send a messenger.

(480) Ash-Shu'ara, 208. (481) Al-Isra, 15.

Messages

1- Punishing the sinner is among the affairs of Allah's Lordship (Rububiyyah). «ربّك مهلك القرى »

2- Punishment without clarification and warning is oppression (zulm) and abhorrent. «مهلك القُرى بظلمٍ و اهلها غافلون»

Ayah

وَلِكُلٍّ دَرَجَـٰتٌ مِّمَّا عَمِلُوا۟ ۚ وَمَا رَبُّكَ بِغَـٰفِلٍ عَمَّا يَعْمَلُونَ (۱۳۲)
For everyone there are ranks in accordance with what they have done; and your Lord is not oblivious of what they do. (132)

Key Points

The meaning of "درجات" (degrees) in this Ayah is not the degrees of growth (ascent), but rather includes the degrees of decline (descent) as well.

Messages

1- Allah is just, and He assigns the rank of every person according to his own performance. «درجاتٌ ممّا عملوا»

2- The happiness (felicity) and misery (wretchedness) of man depend on his deeds. «ممّا عملوا»

3- Man must be vigilant, because he is under Allah's observation. «و ما ربّك بغافل عمّا یعملون»

Ayahs

وَرَبُّكَ ٱلْغَنِىُّ ذُو ٱلرَّحْمَةِ ۚ إِن يَشَأْ يُذْهِبْكُمْ وَيَسْتَخْلِفْ مِنۢ بَعْدِكُم مَّا يَشَآءُ كَمَآ أَنشَأَكُم مِّن ذُرِّيَّةِ قَوْمٍ ءَاخَرِينَ (۱۳۳)
Your Lord is the All-sufficient dispenser of mercy. If He wishes, He will take you away, and make whomever He wishes succeed you, just as He produced you from the descendants of another people. (133)
إِنَّ مَا تُوعَدُونَ لَـَٔاتٍ ۖ وَمَآ أَنتُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ (۱۳٤)
Indeed what you are promised will surely come, and you will not be able to thwart it. (134)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- Only Allah is absolutely Self-Sufficient (Ghani). Therefore, we should never consider ourselves self-sufficient. «ربّك الغنىّ» (Your Lord is the Self-Sufficient One). In another place, He says: «یا أیّها النّاس أنتم الفقراء الى اللّه واللّه هو الغنىّ الحمید» [482] O people! All of you are in need of Allah, and He alone is the Self-Sufficient, the Praiseworthy.

2- Sinners must fear even in this world, because their annihilation by Divine power is very simple. «یذهبكم» (He can take you away).

3- The root of oppression (which was mentioned two verses prior) is either need or hard-heartedness, and neither of these exists in Allah. «الغنىّ ذوالرّحمة» (The Self-Sufficient, the Possessor of Mercy).

4- The condition for a good Educator (Rabb) is Self-Sufficiency and compassion. «ربّك الغنىّ ذو الرّحمة» (Your Lord is the Self-Sufficient, the Possessor of Mercy).

5- The survival of human societies depends on Divine Will. «اِن یشأ یذهبكم» (If He wills, He can take you away).

6- The existence or non-existence of human beings brings no benefit or harm to Allah. «الغنىّ... اِن یشأ یذهبكم» (The Self-Sufficient... If He wills, He can take you away).

7- Allah is Self-Sufficient regarding our acts of worship; the commands for worship are factors for our own growth. «الغنىّ» (The Self-Sufficient).

8- Allah's Mercy is comprehensive, but sometimes man takes matters to a point where Allah annihilates everyone. «ذوالرّحمة... یذهبكم» (The Possessor of Mercy... He can take you away).

9- Divine promises are certain, not a joke. «انّ ما توعدون لآت» (Indeed, what you are promised is surely coming).

10- The criminal will not be able to contend with Divine power on the Day of Judgment. «و ما انتم بمعجزین» (And you are not ones to frustrate Him).

482) Fatir, 15.

Ayah

قُلْ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْمَلُوا۟ عَلَىٰ مَكَانَتِكُمْ إِنِّى عَامِلٌ ۖ فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ مَن تَكُونُ لَهُۥ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلدَّارِ ۗ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُفْلِحُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ (۱۳٥)
Say, ‘O my people, act according to your ability; I too am acting. Soon you will know in whose favour the outcome of that abode will be. Indeed the wrongdoers will not be felicitous.’ (135)

Messages

1- The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, because He is certain of His path, is commanded to declare His stability and decisiveness to others in His interactions. «قل یا قوم اعملوا على مكانتكم انّى عامل...»

2- The disobedience of the people does not change the duty of the prophets. «انّى عامل»

3- The destiny of people depends on their own performance. «اعملوا ... فسوف تعلمون»

4- The criterion for success is the good outcome and the final result of the work, not fleeting appearances and efforts. «عاقبة الدّار»

5- The wrongdoer will not prosper. «انّه لا یفلح الظالمون»

6- Disobeying the path of Allah and the prophets is oppression (ظلم). «الظالمون»

Ayah

وَجَعَلُوا۟ لِلَّهِ مِمَّا ذَرَأَ مِنَ ٱلْحَرْثِ وَٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ نَصِيبًا فَقَالُوا۟ هَـٰذَا لِلَّهِ بِزَعْمِهِمْ وَهَـٰذَا لِشُرَكَآئِنَا ۖ فَمَا كَانَ لِشُرَكَآئِهِمْ فَلَا يَصِلُ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ لِلَّهِ فَهُوَ يَصِلُ إِلَىٰ شُرَكَآئِهِمْ ۗ سَآءَ مَا يَحْكُمُونَ (۱۳٦)
They dedicate a portion to Allah out of what He has created of the crops and cattle, and say, ‘This is for Allah,’ so do they maintain, ‘and this is for our partners.’ But what is for their partners does not reach Allah, and what is for Allah reaches their partners. Evil is the judgement that they make. (136)

Key Points

Those who depart from the path of the Prophets' guidance and enter the valley of imagination, their words and decisions are also imaginary and illogical. They consider themselves owners of everything and proceed to make divisions. One time they consider sons as their share and daughters as Allah's share. «الكم الذكر و له الانثى» [483] Another time they divide crops and animals between Allah and the idols. The polytheists believed that the idols' share was unchangeable, and they spent it on the idol temples and their servants. They also spent Allah's share on the idol temples when shortages occurred, using the excuse that the God of the heavens is self-sufficient (or needless). Not spending it on children, the poor, or guests.

483) Najm, 21.

Messages

1- Although humans play a role in agriculture and animal husbandry, the true cultivator and Creator is Allah. «للّه ممّا ذرأ» In another Ayah, He says: «ءانتم تزرعونه أم نحن الزّارعون» [484]

2- The law of Zakat (charity) and the division of income and profits existed in the beliefs of previous peoples as well, even if in a superstitious form. «هذا للّه... هذا لشركائنا»

3- Although the polytheists considered the idols partners with Allah, they still accorded Allah a special status of honor and self-sufficiency (Ghinā). They deducted losses from Allah's share, saying: He has no need. «ما كان للّه فهو یصل الى شركائهم»

4- The mission of the Prophets is to combat superstitions. «ساء ما یحكمون»

484) Waqi'ah, 64.

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ زَيَّنَ لِكَثِيرٍ مِّنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ قَتْلَ أَوْلَـٰدِهِمْ شُرَكَآؤُهُمْ لِيُرْدُوهُمْ وَلِيَلْبِسُوا۟ عَلَيْهِمْ دِينَهُمْ ۖ وَلَوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ مَا فَعَلُوهُ ۖ فَذَرْهُمْ وَمَا يَفْتَرُونَ (۱۳۷)
That is how to most of the polytheists the slaying of their children is presented as decorous by those whom they ascribe as partners [to Allah], that they may ruin them and confound their religion for them. Had Allah wished, they would not have done it. So leave them with what they fabricate. (137)

Key Points

Just as the division of crops and livestock between Allah and their partners appeared appealing to the polytheists, so too did the satanic temptations make the love of idols and the killing of children at the foot of the idol seem attractive to them. «یُردُوهُم» (that they may ruin them): is derived from irda' (ارداء), meaning to destroy or ruin. Polytheists either say: a daughter is a disgrace and they bury her alive, «أیمسكه على هون» (485) or they say: I have no means of support and she must be destroyed, «خشیة املاق» (486) or they consider it a type of proximity to the idols, all of which is a form of beautification (of evil).

485) An-Nahl, 59. 486) Al-Isra, 31.

Messages

1- Wrong and superstitious insight causes a person to sacrifice his own child at the foot of a stone or wooden idol and take pride in it. «زَیَّن... قتل أودلاهم» 2- Criminals even create proper justifications for infanticide to calm their minds and consciences. «زَیَّن» 3- In criticism, we must observe fairness. «لكثیر من المشركین» (for many of the polytheists), not that all are like this. 4- Justifying sin and beautifying evil deeds is a factor leading to destruction and downfall. «زَیَّن... لیُردُوهم» 5- Superstition leads to syncretism and the mixing of religion with doubts. «ولیلبسوا» 6- The Prophet is responsible for conveying the message, not for compulsion. If people do not listen, he leaves them and turns toward receptive hearts. «فذرهم» 7- The thoughts and actions of the misguided should not discourage those who seek the truth. «فذرهم و ما یفترون» 8- Considering partners for Allah is a fabrication against Him. «فذرهم و ما یفترون»

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ هَـٰذِهِۦٓ أَنْعَـٰمٌ وَحَرْثٌ حِجْرٌ لَّا يَطْعَمُهَآ إِلَّا مَن نَّشَآءُ بِزَعْمِهِمْ وَأَنْعَـٰمٌ حُرِّمَتْ ظُهُورُهَا وَأَنْعَـٰمٌ لَّا يَذْكُرُونَ ٱسْمَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْهَا ٱفْتِرَآءً عَلَيْهِ ۚ سَيَجْزِيهِم بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْتَرُونَ (۱۳۸)
And they say, ‘These cattle and tillage are a taboo: none may eat them except whom we please,’ so they maintain, and there are cattle whose backs are forbidden and cattle over which they do not mention Allah’s Name, fabricating a lie against Him. Soon He will requite them for what they used to fabricate. (138)

Key Points

  • Hujr (حِجْر) means prevention (or prohibition). Tahjir (تحجیر) means surrounding an area with stones (stone-fencing) and preventing others from entering to claim it. Hujr Isma'il (حِجر اسماعیل) is also a place separated by a stone wall. Intellect (Aql) is also called Hujr because it restrains man from indecencies (or evils). «هل فى ذلك قسم لِذى حِجر» [487]
  • In the two preceding verses, the superstitious beliefs of the polytheists regarding the share of Allah and the idols from agriculture and livestock were discussed. In this verse, the manner of consuming the idols' share is explained: no one has the right to use it, except the servants of the idols and the idol temples.
  • Considering this verse, the polytheists had four deviations:
    1. Declaring some livestock forbidden (or prohibited).
    2. Declaring some agricultural products forbidden.
    3. Prohibiting riding certain livestock.
    4. Not mentioning the Name of Allah when slaughtering animals.
  • The Quran considers the purpose of creating livestock to be riding them and consuming their meat: «جعل لكم الانعام لتركبوا منها و منها تأكلون» [488] And it considers the prohibition of utilizing livestock to be among the innovations (bid'at) of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), which is also mentioned in Ayah 103 of Surah Al-Ma'idah.
  • Islam is a comprehensive religion that does not tolerate deviation even in the utilization of animals. When the Quran condemns the uselessness of an animal and the prohibition of riding it, it condemns, a fortiori, the uselessness of human beings, resources, capital, and talents even more severely.

487) Fajr, 5. 488) Ghafir, 79.

Messages

1- The rulings of religion must be attributed to Allah, not based on conjecture, imagination, analogy (qiyas), or preference (istihsan). «قالوا... بزعمهم... افتراءً علیه»

2- Combating superstitions is one of the primary duties of the prophets. «افتراء علیه سیجزیهم»

3- Prohibiting the lawful (halal) and permitting the unlawful (haram) is a fabrication against Allah. «هذه انعام و حرث حجر... افتراء»

4- Innovators (those who introduce bid'ah) should await the punishment for their imaginary laws. «سیجزیهم»

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ مَا فِى بُطُونِ هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ خَالِصَةٌ لِّذُكُورِنَا وَمُحَرَّمٌ عَلَىٰٓ أَزْوَٰجِنَا ۖ وَإِن يَكُن مَّيْتَةً فَهُمْ فِيهِ شُرَكَآءُ ۚ سَيَجْزِيهِمْ وَصْفَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ حَكِيمٌ عَلِيمٌ (۱۳۹)
And they say, ‘That which is in the bellies of these cattle is exclusively for our males and forbidden to our wives. But if it be still-born, they will all share it.’ Soon He will requite them for their allegations. Indeed, He is all-wise, all-knowing. (139)

Key Points

Familiarity with the superstitions of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah) acquaints man with the hardships endured by the Messenger of Allah for their guidance, thereby reviving and flourishing the spirit of gratitude within him.

Messages

1- Undue discrimination between men and women is an ignorant (Jahili) and rejected practice. «خالصة لذكورنا و محرّم على ازواجنا»

2- In the Age of Ignorance, the humiliation of women reached such an extent that in some cases, they had no share in the healthy animal and were only partners in the meat of the dead animal. «شركاء»

3- Divine recompense and punishment are based on His knowledge and wisdom. «سیجزیهم ... حكیم علیم»

Ayah

قَدْ خَسِرَ ٱلَّذِينَ قَتَلُوٓا۟ أَوْلَـٰدَهُمْ سَفَهًۢا بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ وَحَرَّمُوا۟ مَا رَزَقَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ ٱفْتِرَآءً عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۚ قَدْ ضَلُّوا۟ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ مُهْتَدِينَ (۱٤٠)
They are certainly losers who slay their children foolishly without knowledge, and forbid what Allah has provided them, fabricating a lie against Allah. Certainly, they have gone astray and are not guided. (140)

Key Points

Ibn Abbas, one of the great figures of early Islam, says: Whoever wishes to know the extent of the ignorance of the nations during the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), should read these very verses of Surah Al-An'am, which describe the superstitions and baseless beliefs of the polytheists. [489] The ignorant Arabs, thinking they would draw closer to the idols, or for the sake of preserving honor, sometimes sacrificed their daughters to the idols or buried them alive.

489) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- Ignorance and foolishness are causes of loss. (Losses such as: losing children, losing compassion, forfeiting lawful blessings, earning Hellfire and divine punishment). «قد خَسر»

2- The real loss is the sacrifice of a human being in the path of falsehood. (Whether being sacrificed to an idol, or being sacrificed due to misplaced imagination and zeal/honor). «قد خَسر»

3- The undue prohibition of lawful things is a fabrication against Allah. «وحرّموا ما رزقهم اللَّه افتراء»

4- Performing any action or prohibiting anything requires either a legal (Shar'i) or a rational (Aqli) justification. «بغیر علم افتراء على اللَّه»

5- We must confront and deal with superstitions in the strongest possible manner. «قد خسر، سفهاً بغیر علم، افتراء، ضلّوا، ما كانوا مهتدین»

Ayah

۞ وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنشَأَ جَنَّـٰتٍ مَّعْرُوشَـٰتٍ وَغَيْرَ مَعْرُوشَـٰتٍ وَٱلنَّخْلَ وَٱلزَّرْعَ مُخْتَلِفًا أُكُلُهُۥ وَٱلزَّيْتُونَ وَٱلرُّمَّانَ مُتَشَـٰبِهًا وَغَيْرَ مُتَشَـٰبِهٍ ۚ كُلُوا۟ مِن ثَمَرِهِۦٓ إِذَآ أَثْمَرَ وَءَاتُوا۟ حَقَّهُۥ يَوْمَ حَصَادِهِۦ ۖ وَلَا تُسْرِفُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُسْرِفِينَ (۱٤۱)
It is He who produces gardens trellised and without trellises, and palm-trees and crops of diverse produce, olives and pomegranates, similar and dissimilar. Eat of its fruits when it fructifies, and give its due on the day of harvest, and do not be wasteful; indeed, He does not like the wasteful. (141)

Key Points

Jannāt are gardens full of trees and lands covered with cultivation. Ma'rūsh is a tree that requires a trellis. Ukul means ma'kūl (edible) or foodstuff. In Ayah 99 of this same Surah, we read: «اُنظروا الى ثمره اذا أثمر» (Look at its fruit when it yields fruit). In this Ayah, we read: «كلوا من ثمره اذا اثمر» (Eat of its fruit when it yields fruit). The conclusion is that eating must be done with contemplation and precision, not heedlessly. Imam Baqir عليه السلام, after reciting this Ayah, said: A person had cultivation and gave all his produce in charity, leaving himself and his family destitute. Allah called this act extravagance (isrāf). (490) This Ayah conveys the lesson of knowing Allah, along with permission to consume foodstuffs, attending to the deprived classes, giving charity to them, maintaining moderation, and avoiding extravagance in consumption and charity. (Ansha'a, Kulū, Ātū, Lā tusrifū). Just as we read elsewhere: «و الّذین اذا انفقوا لم یسرفوا و لم یقتروا و كان بین ذلك قواماً» (And [they are] those who, when they spend, do not spend excessively and do not restrict, but are moderate between that.) (491) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: Even if no Muslim is found, pay the right of harvest to a poor polytheist. (492) In another narration, He said: This right of harvest is separate from Zakat. (493) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Do not harvest the fruit at night and do not reap at night so that the poor may also be able to attend and take something. (494) In Surah Nūn wal-Qalam (The Pen), we also read that Allah burned the garden of those who decided to pick the fruits at night, away from the sight of the poor, and not give them anything.

490) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 491) Al-Furqan, 67. 492) Bihar, Vol. 93, p. 96. 493) Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 557. 494) Kafi, Vol. 3, p. 565.

Messages

  1. The diversity of fruits and agricultural products, all from the same water and soil, is a sign of Divine power. «انشأ... مختلفاً اُكله»
  2. Allah has established a right for Himself in the produce. «آتوا حقّه» (Yes, light and air, water and soil, strength and power, thought and talent—all belong to Allah, and He has a right over everything.)
  3. We must be moderate even in giving charity. «و آتوا حقّه یوم حصاده و لاتسرفوا»
  4. Islam is the religion of moderation; it forbids both undue prohibition (in the previous Ayah) and excessive consumption. «لاتسرفوا»
  5. The condition for consumption is paying the right of the deprived. «كلوا... آتوا حقّه»
  6. The amount of consumption is limited by the avoidance of extravagance. «كلوا... لا تسرفوا»
  7. At the time of harvest, we must remember the deprived. «یوم حصاده»
  8. We should harvest the produce at a time that allows others to benefit as well. «یوم حصاده»
  9. When the produce ripens and is harvested, a person's readiness to give charity is greater, so we should not lose the opportunity. «آتوا حقّه یوم حصاده»
  10. Do not eat unripe fruit, and consume the fruit when it is fresh. «كلوا من ثمره اذا اثمر»
  11. The extravagant person is disliked by Allah. «انّه لایحبّ المسرفین»

Ayah

وَمِنَ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ حَمُولَةً وَفَرْشًا ۚ كُلُوا۟ مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ ٱللَّهُ وَلَا تَتَّبِعُوا۟ خُطُوَٰتِ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ مُّبِينٌ (۱٤۲)
Of the cattle [some] are for burden and [some] for slaughter. Eat of what Allah has provided you and do not follow in Satan’s footsteps; he is indeed your manifest enemy. (142)

Key Points

The previous Ayah referred to Allah's blessings in agricultural products, and here it refers to animal husbandry and the various benefits that humans derive from animals. The meaning of the word "فَرش" (Farsh) is livestock, such as sheep, which are considered like carpets (farsh) because their bodies are close to the ground, or because carpets are woven from their wool, down, and hair. Most commentators have understood the word "حَمولَة" (Hamūlah) to mean burden-bearing animals, and "فَرش" (Farsh) to mean non-burden-bearing animals.

Messages

1- Existence and its creations are subjugated to humanity. "حمولة و فرشاً"

2- The general rule regarding animals is that their consumption is lawful (Halal), unless there is evidence proving their prohibition (Haram). "كلوا ممّا رزقكم اللّه"

3- Prohibiting what is lawful is among the steps of Satan. "كلوا... و لاتتّبعوا خطوات الشیطان"

4- The sole recommendation for raw foodism, which is common among many groups today, and the prohibition of meat consumption, is forbidden. "و من الانعام... كلوا ممّا رزقكم اللَّه"

5- We must be careful regarding what we eat. "كلوا... و لاتتّبعوا" Just as the first lever Satan used to mislead Prophet Adam was food.

6- At the table of Allah, we should not follow His enemy. "كلوا ممّا رزقكم اللَّه و لاتتّبعوا خطوات الشّیطان"

7- Satan's policy is gradual deviation, not sudden. "خطوات الشّیطان"

Ayah

ثَمَـٰنِيَةَ أَزْوَٰجٍ ۖ مِّنَ ٱلضَّأْنِ ٱثْنَيْنِ وَمِنَ ٱلْمَعْزِ ٱثْنَيْنِ ۗ قُلْ ءَآلذَّكَرَيْنِ حَرَّمَ أَمِ ٱلْأُنثَيَيْنِ أَمَّا ٱشْتَمَلَتْ عَلَيْهِ أَرْحَامُ ٱلْأُنثَيَيْنِ ۖ نَبِّـُٔونِى بِعِلْمٍ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۱٤۳)
Eight mates: two of sheep, and two of goats. Say, ‘Is it the two males that He has forbidden or the two females, or what is contained in the wombs of the two females? Inform me with knowledge, should you be truthful.’ (143)

Key Points

The word zawj (زوج) is used both for the pair (male and female combined) and for each individual (male or female) separately. In this ayah, it refers to each one individually, and the intent of "eight pairs" (thamāniyata azwāj) is eight individuals: four males and four females, four of which are mentioned in Ayah 143 and four in Ayah 144. In Ayah 45 of Surah An-Najm, the word zawj is also used for a single individual, not two: «خلق الزّوجین الذّكر والانثى» (He created the two pairs, the male and the female). In Ayah 6 of Surah Az-Zumar, the creation of the eight males and females of the animals is mentioned alongside the creation of man: «خلقكم من نَفسٍ واحدة، ثُمّ جعل منها زوجها وانزل لكم من الانعام ثَمانیَة اَزواج» (He created you from a single soul, then made from it its mate, and sent down for you eight pairs of livestock). It is probable that the order in which the four animals are named indicates their superiority in consumption (sheep, goat, camel, and cow). In some narrations, thamāniyata azwāj (eight pairs) is interpreted as 16 individuals, meaning that each of these four animals has two types (one male, one female) and each type exists as both domestic and wild. Imam Hadi عليه السلام said: The water buffalo is a type of wild cow. [495]

495) Tafsir Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 558.

Messages

1- To eliminate superstitions from religion, true beliefs must be presented very openly and clearly. «ثمانیة ازواج...»

2- Knowledge is necessary when issuing a fatwa or holding a belief regarding the permissibility (halal) or prohibition (haram) of something. «نبّئونى بعلم»

3- Permissibility does not require proof, because the fundamental principle concerning edibles is lawfulness (hilliyyah); rather, prohibition requires proof. «قل الذكرین حرّم ام الاُنثیین... نبئونى بعلم»

4- Slander against Allah is the greatest injustice. «فمن أظلم ممّن افترى على اللّه»

5- Lying about God leads to the misguidance of people. «لیضلّ النّاس»

6- Slander prevents a person from accepting guidance. «لایهدى»

7- Beliefs and doctrines must either be based on knowledge and intellect, as we read in the previous ayah, «نبّئونى بعلم» (Inform Me with knowledge), or based on divine law (Shar'iah). «ام كنتم شهداء اذ وصّاكم اللَّه» (Or were you witnesses when Allah enjoined this upon you?).

Ayah

وَمِنَ ٱلْإِبِلِ ٱثْنَيْنِ وَمِنَ ٱلْبَقَرِ ٱثْنَيْنِ ۗ قُلْ ءَآلذَّكَرَيْنِ حَرَّمَ أَمِ ٱلْأُنثَيَيْنِ أَمَّا ٱشْتَمَلَتْ عَلَيْهِ أَرْحَامُ ٱلْأُنثَيَيْنِ ۖ أَمْ كُنتُمْ شُهَدَآءَ إِذْ وَصَّىٰكُمُ ٱللَّهُ بِهَـٰذَا ۚ فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ ٱفْتَرَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا لِّيُضِلَّ ٱلنَّاسَ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۱٤٤)
And two of camels and two of oxen. Say, ‘Is it the two males that He has forbidden or the two females, or what is contained in the wombs of the two females? Were you witnesses when Allah enjoined this upon you?’ So who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah to mislead the people without any knowledge? Indeed Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot. (144)

Key Points

The word zawj (زوج) is used both for the pair (male and female combined) and for each individual male or female separately. In this ayah, it is used for each one separately, and the intent of "eight pairs" (thamāniyata azwāj) is eight individuals: four males and four females, which are detailed in Ayah 143 and Ayah 144. Also, in Ayah 45 of Surah An-Najm, the word zawj is used for a single individual, not for two: «خلق الزّوجین الذّكر والانثى» (He created the two pairs, the male and the female). In Ayah 6 of Surah Az-Zumar, the creation of the eight males and females of livestock is mentioned alongside the creation of man: «خلقكم من نَفسٍ واحدة، ثُمّ جعل منها زوجها وانزل لكم من الانعام ثَمانیَة اَزواج» (He created you from a single soul, then made from it its mate, and sent down for you of the cattle eight pairs.) It is probable that the order in which the four animals are named (sheep, goat, camel, and cow) refers to their superiority in consumption/utility. In some narrations, thamāniyata azwāj (ثمانية ازواج) is interpreted as 16 individuals, meaning that these four animals each have two types (one male, one female) and each type has both domestic and wild varieties. Imam Hadi عليه السلام said: The water buffalo is a type of wild cow. [495]

495) Tafsir Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 558.

Messages

1- To eliminate superstition from religion, true beliefs must be presented very openly and clearly. «ثمانیة ازواج...»

2- Knowledge is necessary when issuing a fatwa or holding a belief regarding the permissibility (halal) or prohibition (haram) of something. «نبّئونى بعلم»

3- Permissibility does not require proof, because the default principle concerning edibles is lawfulness (hilliyyah); rather, prohibition requires proof. «قل الذكرین حرّم ام الاُنثیین... نبئونى بعلم»

4- Slander against Allah is the greatest injustice. «فمن أظلم ممّن افترى على اللّه»

5- Lying about Allah leads to the misguidance of people. «لیضلّ النّاس»

6- Slander prevents a person from accepting guidance. «لایهدى»

7- Beliefs and doctrines must either be based on knowledge and reason, as we read in the previous ayah, «نبّئونى بعلم» (Inform Me with knowledge), or based on divine law (Shar'). «ام كنتم شهداء اذ وصّاكم اللَّه» (Or were you witnesses when Allah enjoined this upon you?)

Ayah

قُل لَّآ أَجِدُ فِى مَآ أُوحِىَ إِلَىَّ مُحَرَّمًا عَلَىٰ طَاعِمٍ يَطْعَمُهُۥٓ إِلَّآ أَن يَكُونَ مَيْتَةً أَوْ دَمًا مَّسْفُوحًا أَوْ لَحْمَ خِنزِيرٍ فَإِنَّهُۥ رِجْسٌ أَوْ فِسْقًا أُهِلَّ لِغَيْرِ ٱللَّهِ بِهِۦ ۚ فَمَنِ ٱضْطُرَّ غَيْرَ بَاغٍ وَلَا عَادٍ فَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱٤٥)
Say, ‘I do not find in what has been revealed to me that anyone be forbidden to eat anything except carrion or spilt blood, or the flesh of swine—for that is indeed unclean—or an impiety offered to other than Allah.’ But should someone be compelled, without being rebellious or aggressive, indeed your Lord is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (145)

Key Points

Maytah (carrion) is not only an animal that has died naturally; rather, if it is not slaughtered according to Islamic instructions, it is considered carrion and is forbidden (Haram). The prohibition of carrion and blood has been mentioned four times in the Quran: twice in Makkan Surahs [496] and twice in Madinan Surahs [497].

Uhilla (mentioned in the Ayah) is derived from Ihlal, meaning to raise the voice when sighting the crescent moon (Hilal) of the first month. Subsequently, it has been applied to any loud sound. The polytheists (Mushrikun), when slaughtering animals, would loudly invoke the names of idols; hence, the term uhilla is used.

The phrase "لا أجد... الاّ أن یكون میتة" (I do not find... except that it be carrion) is stated in response to the ignorant prohibitions imposed by the polytheists. Otherwise, the meat of certain other birds and predatory animals is also forbidden, which is not mentioned in this Ayah. This restriction (Hasr), technically speaking, is relative (Idafi) and not absolute (Haqiqi).

From the perspective of Islam, mudtarr (one who is compelled/in dire necessity) refers to someone who has not put himself in that state of necessity through oppression, transgression, or sin ("غیر باغٍ"). He is permitted (to consume the forbidden) only to the extent necessary to relieve the dire need, and no more ("و لاعاد").

496) An'am, 145 and Nahl, 115. 497) Baqarah, 173 and Ma'idah, 3.

Messages

1- The sole source of knowledge for the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله in stating rulings is revelation (Wahy). "لا اجد فى ما اوحى الىّ" (I do not find in what has been revealed to me).

2- The primary principle and initial rule is the permissibility (Halal) of animals. "لا أجد" (I do not find).

3- Only prohibitions communicated in the Ayahs and narrations are binding (Taklif-Avar). Therefore, if, after searching and investigating, we find no proof of something being forbidden, it is permissible (Halal) for us. "لا أجد" (I do not find).

4- When even the Prophet himself must receive instructions from "revelation" regarding the matter of eating and not eating, how can others arbitrarily forbid something on their own? "فى ما اوحى الىّ" (in what has been revealed to me).

5- The reason for the prohibition of pork is its impurity and filth. "فانّه رِجس" (for it is an abomination).

6- Dietary rulings are the same for men and women. "طاعم یطعمه" (one who eats it). This stands in contrast to the superstitious belief mentioned in Ayah 139, where they considered the meat of all animals permissible for men but forbade certain items for women. "خالصة لذكورنا ومحرّم على أزواجنا" (exclusively for our males, and forbidden to our wives).

7- In law, priorities (the important and the more important) must be observed. Preserving life is more important than the prohibition of consuming carrion. "فمَن اضطرّ" (But whoever is compelled).

8- There is no deadlock in Islam; when dire necessity (Idtirar) arises, the consumption of forbidden things becomes permissible. "فمَن اضطرّ" (But whoever is compelled).

9- Misuse of the law in exceptional cases is forbidden, and one must suffice with only the amount required by the necessity. "غیر باغ ولا عاد" (not seeking transgression nor exceeding the limit).

10- Blood remaining in the meat after lawful slaughter is not forbidden. The blood that is poured forth is forbidden. "دماً مسفوحاً" (blood poured forth).

11- Where necessity arises involuntarily, one may consume forbidden meat. However, if we intentionally do something that makes us compelled, consumption is not permissible. "اُضطر" (It is presented in the passive form).

12- The existence of laws granting leniency and addressing necessity is a manifestation of Divine forgiveness and Mercy. "فمن اضطرّ... فانّ ربّك غفور رحیم" (But whoever is compelled... then indeed your Lord is Forgiving, Merciful).

Ayah

وَعَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ هَادُوا۟ حَرَّمْنَا كُلَّ ذِى ظُفُرٍ ۖ وَمِنَ ٱلْبَقَرِ وَٱلْغَنَمِ حَرَّمْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ شُحُومَهُمَآ إِلَّا مَا حَمَلَتْ ظُهُورُهُمَآ أَوِ ٱلْحَوَايَآ أَوْ مَا ٱخْتَلَطَ بِعَظْمٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ جَزَيْنَـٰهُم بِبَغْيِهِمْ ۖ وَإِنَّا لَصَـٰدِقُونَ (۱٤٦)
To the Jews We forbade every animal having an undivided hoof, and of oxen and sheep We forbade them their fat, except what is borne by their backs or the entrails, or what is attached to the bones. We requited them with that for their rebelliousness, and We indeed speak the truth. (146)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the prohibitions (muharramāt) in Islam were stated, and in this Ayah, the prohibitions for the Jews are stated, so that it becomes clear that the superstitious beliefs of the polytheists are incompatible with any of the divine religions. [498] Ẓufur (ظُفُر) means nail, but it is also applied to the hoof of some animals that are not cloven (like the hoof of a horse or the tip of a camel's foot). Therefore, the camel and animals whose hooves are not cloven, whether quadrupeds or birds, are forbidden (ḥarām) to the Jews. [499] Ḥawāyā (حوایا) is the plural of ḥāwiyah (حاویه), and refers to the contents of the abdomen. [500] In Ayah 160 of Sūrat An-Nisā, we also read: «فبظلم من الّذین هادوا حَرّمنا علیهم طیّبات اُحلّت لهم» (So, because of the wrongdoing of those who were Jews, We forbade them good things which had been made lawful for them). The prohibition of some good things (ṭayyibāt) upon the Jews was due to their injustices. Furthermore, the Jews, like the polytheists, had forbidden some things upon themselves. The Quran states: «كلّ الطّعام كان حلاًّ لبنى اسرائیل الاّ ما حَرّم اسرائیل على نفسه» [501] The prohibition of some foods upon the Jews was temporary and was lifted by Prophet Jesus عليه السلام. As we read elsewhere: «لاحلّ لكم بعض الّذى حرّم عليكم» [502] (I have come to make lawful for you some of that which was forbidden to you). Divine rulings are of three types: A: Those possessing real benefit (maṣlaḥah) or harm (mafsadah), like most rulings. B: Those having a testing aspect, such as the command to sacrifice Prophet Ishmael. C: Those having a punitive aspect, such as this very Ayah. Indeed, one of Allah's punishments is restrictions in livelihood.

498) Tafsīr Namūneh. 499) Torah, Book of Leviticus, Chapter 11. 500) Tafsīr Namūneh. 501) Āl ‘Imrān, 93. 502) Āl ‘Imrān, 50.

Messages

1- Sometimes, restricting the food of transgressors and rebels is permissible. «حرّمنا... ببغیهم» (We forbade... because of their transgression).

2- Divine punishment is not limited to the Hereafter; it also occurs in this world. «جزیناهم ببغیهم» (We recompensed them because of their transgression).

Ayah

فَإِن كَذَّبُوكَ فَقُل رَّبُّكُمْ ذُو رَحْمَةٍ وَٰسِعَةٍ وَلَا يُرَدُّ بَأْسُهُۥ عَنِ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ (۱٤۷)
But if they deny you, say, ‘Your Lord is dispenser of an all-embracing mercy, but His punishment will not be averted from the guilty lot.’ (147)

Messages

1- The leader must be prepared to hear denial and slander from some people. «فان كذّبوك فقل...»

2- Deniers should be treated with a benevolent approach, and if that is ineffective, threat should be used. «ذو رحمة - لا یردّ بأسه»

3- The educator must be a sea of mercy. «ربّكم ذو رحمة»

4- Fear and hope are effective when combined. «ذورحمة، بأسه»

5- The doors of Divine Mercy are not closed even to opponents. «فان كذّبوك ربّكم ذورحمة»

6- Allah's Mercy precedes His wrath. «ذو رحمة... بأسه»

7- The vastness of Divine Mercy does not prevent Him from punishing. «ربّكم ذو رحمة... و لایردّ بأسه» Although His punishment also originates from His Mercy and Lordship, and is aimed at the training of humanity.

Ayah

سَيَقُولُ ٱلَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا۟ لَوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ مَآ أَشْرَكْنَا وَلَآ ءَابَآؤُنَا وَلَا حَرَّمْنَا مِن شَىْءٍ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ كَذَّبَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ حَتَّىٰ ذَاقُوا۟ بَأْسَنَا ۗ قُلْ هَلْ عِندَكُم مِّنْ عِلْمٍ فَتُخْرِجُوهُ لَنَآ ۖ إِن تَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا ٱلظَّنَّ وَإِنْ أَنتُمْ إِلَّا تَخْرُصُونَ (۱٤۸)
The polytheists will say, ‘Had Allah wished we would not have ascribed any partner [to Him], nor our fathers, nor would we have forbidden anything.’ Those who were before them had denied likewise until they tasted Our punishment. Say, ‘Do you have any [revealed] knowledge that you can produce before us? You follow nothing but conjectures, and you do nothing but surmise.’ (148)

Key Points

This verse presents a hidden report (a prophecy) about the future regarding what the polytheists will say, «سیقول الّذین اشركوا» (The polytheists will say). That future came to pass, and they said what had been foretold. «و قال الّذین اشركوا» (And the polytheists said) [503] This excuse, that "If Allah had willed, neither we nor our fathers would have been polytheists," has been raised by the polytheists many times, as mentioned in Ayah 35 of Surah An-Nahl and Ayah 20 of Surah Az-Zukhruf.

Question: This verse quotes the polytheists' statement: If Allah is not pleased with our polytheism, why does He not restrain us from it? Therefore, our polytheism must be acceptable to Allah!

Answer: The performance of any action by anyone is not outside the sphere of Allah's power, but Allah's Will and Decree (Mashi'ah) is that man should freely choose his own path. When the government brings water, electricity, and gas to our homes, these facilities do not indicate the government's satisfaction with our misuse of them. Allah has granted man intellect and revelation, shown him the path of truth and falsehood, and left him free in his choice. However, granting will and choice is not a sign of His satisfaction with our transgression. Allah desires the voluntary faith of the people, not forcing them to believe. It has been stated repeatedly in the Quran that: If Allah had willed, He would have guided everyone, and the Prophet does not have the right to compel or force people into faith.

503) Nahl, 35.

Messages

1- Religious leaders and thinkers must prepare themselves to respond to future doubts and excuses. «سیقول» (They will say).

2- Prophets and heavenly books were sent so that people might become monotheists through freedom and choice. Therefore, the logic of the polytheists (that if Allah had willed, He would have prevented our polytheism through might and power) is false. «لوشاءاللَّه ما اشركنا» (If Allah had willed, we would not have been polytheists).

3- Worse than sin is justifying it. The polytheists justified their polytheism and considered it the Divine Decree (Mashi'ah). «لوشاءاللَّه ما اشركنا» (If Allah had willed, we would not have been polytheists). (Determinism is an excuse to evade responsibility).

4- The polytheists justified their deviation by citing the history of their ancestors. «لو شاء اللَّه ما اشركنا و لاآباؤنا» (If Allah had willed, neither we nor our fathers would have been polytheists).

5- The belief in determinism (Jabr) is one of the baseless justifications of the deviated. «لو شاء اللَّه ما اشركنا» (If Allah had willed, we would not have been polytheists). Even Satan, the leader of the deviated, attributed his misguidance to Allah and said: «ربّ بما أغویتَنى» (My Lord, because You have put me in error) [504].

6- Opponents have always used the lever of determinism to deny the Prophets. «كذلك كذّب الّذین من قبلهم» (Thus did those before them deny).

7- The Quran demands proof and evidence even from the opponents. «قل هل عندكم من علم» (Say, "Do you have any knowledge?").

8- Those who evade responsibility under the pretext of destiny should await the taste of Allah's wrath. «ذاقوا بأسنا» (They tasted Our might).

9- Adherents of determinism lack logic and pursue mere conjectures. «ان تتّبعون الاّ الظن» (You follow nothing but assumption).

10- If we pursue assumption and conjecture instead of knowledge and certainty, we will be misguided. «ان انتم الاّ تخرصون» (You are only guessing).

504) Hijr, 39.

Ayah

قُلْ فَلِلَّهِ ٱلْحُجَّةُ ٱلْبَـٰلِغَةُ ۖ فَلَوْ شَآءَ لَهَدَىٰكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (۱٤۹)
Say, ‘To Allah belongs the conclusive argument. Had He wished, He would have surely guided you all.’ (149)

Key Points

Allah, through the monotheistic nature (Fitrah) He placed in man, and through the guidance of the Prophets and intellect, has completed the argument (Hujjah) against the people, and has also explained the path of good and evil and the good or bad end of each. He has also left the door of repentance and compensation open for the wrongdoers. The clear miracles of the Prophets, their excellent method of invitation, their firm arguments, and the compatibility of religion with nature (Fitrah) and intellect, have completed the argument against the people. Despite all this, the polytheists have no proof for their polytheism except baseless conjecture, and if they follow a path other than Allah's, they have followed errors, weaknesses, scientific and intellectual limitations, and desires. It is mentioned in narrations that on the Day of Judgment, Allah will say to man: Did you know the path of truth or did you not know it? If he says: I knew it, He will ask: Why did you not act upon it? And if he says: I did not know it, He will ask: Why were you not seeking to learn it? This is the conclusive argument (Hujjat al-Balighah) of God against the people. (505)

505) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Only Allah possesses the conclusive argument (Hujjat al-Balighah), and all of us are helpless, destitute, and negligent in responding to Him. «فللّه الحجّة»

2- In the path of Allah, there is no ambiguity or excuse that opponents can use as a pretext, neither in argumentation, nor in history, nor in the attributes of the Prophet, nor in the method of dealing with them. «فللّه الحجّة البالغة»

3- Allah's Will is based on the free and voluntary guidance of man, and his freedom and choice. «فلوشاء لهداكم»

4- The Will of Allah is inevitable. «فلو شاء لهداكم»

Ayah

قُلْ هَلُمَّ شُهَدَآءَكُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَشْهَدُونَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ حَرَّمَ هَـٰذَا ۖ فَإِن شَهِدُوا۟ فَلَا تَشْهَدْ مَعَهُمْ ۚ وَلَا تَتَّبِعْ أَهْوَآءَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ وَهُم بِرَبِّهِمْ يَعْدِلُونَ (۱٥٠)
Say, ‘Bring your witnesses who may testify that Allah has forbidden this.’ So if they testify, do not testify with them, and do not follow the desires of those who deny Our signs, and those who do not believe in the Hereafter and equate [others] with their Lord. (150)

Key Points

Islam is the religion of logic and freedom. In the two previous verses, Allah asked the polytheists: Do you have knowledge of something that We do not? «هل عندكم من عِلمٍ» (Do you have any knowledge?). Here too, He says: If you have witnesses, bring them. «هَلُمَّ شهداءكم» (Bring forth your witnesses). In this verse, He first says: If they have proof and witnesses, let them bring them. Then He says: Even if they testify, do not accept it (because it is not sincere). «یعدلون» (they equate) comes from 'idl (عِدل), meaning equal or peer. Thus, «بربّهم یَعدلون» means that they set up partners, equals, and likenesses for Allah.

Messages

1- Islam is the religion of logic and proof; it demands witnesses from its opponents and invites them to a good dispute. «قل هلمّ شهداءكم»

2- One of the duties of religious preachers is confronting innovations (bid'ah). «قل هلمّ شهداءكم... أنّ اللَّه حرّم هذا»

3- Not every testimony or witness is valid. «فانْ شهدوا فلا تشهد معهم»

4- We must be careful that social conditions and atmosphere do not lead us into error. «فاِنْ شهدوا فلا تشهد معهم» (It is forbidden to confirm or align oneself with false witnesses).

5- Human laws, if they arise from the desires of the disbelievers, are not worthy of being followed. «لاتتّبع أهواء الّذین كذّبوا بآیاتنا...»

6- Believing individuals must not imitate the traditions of the polytheists. «لاتتّبع اهواء الّذین كذّبوا بآیاتنا...»

7- The polytheists acknowledge Allah as the Creator, but they assign partners to Him in the planning and management of the affairs of existence. «و هم بربّهم یعدلون»

Ayah

۞ قُلْ تَعَالَوْا۟ أَتْلُ مَا حَرَّمَ رَبُّكُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ ۖ أَلَّا تُشْرِكُوا۟ بِهِۦ شَيْـًٔا ۖ وَبِٱلْوَٰلِدَيْنِ إِحْسَـٰنًا ۖ وَلَا تَقْتُلُوٓا۟ أَوْلَـٰدَكُم مِّنْ إِمْلَـٰقٍ ۖ نَّحْنُ نَرْزُقُكُمْ وَإِيَّاهُمْ ۖ وَلَا تَقْرَبُوا۟ ٱلْفَوَٰحِشَ مَا ظَهَرَ مِنْهَا وَمَا بَطَنَ ۖ وَلَا تَقْتُلُوا۟ ٱلنَّفْسَ ٱلَّتِى حَرَّمَ ٱللَّهُ إِلَّا بِٱلْحَقِّ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ وَصَّىٰكُم بِهِۦ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ (۱٥۱)
Say, ‘Come, I will recount what your Lord has forbidden you. That you shall not ascribe any partners to Him, and you shall be good to the parents, you shall not kill your children due to penury—We will provide for you and for them—you shall not approach indecencies, the outward among them and the inward ones, and you shall not kill a soul [whose life] Allah has made inviolable, except with due cause. This is what He has enjoined upon you so that you may exercise your reason. (151)

Key Points

In this Ayah and the two subsequent Ayahs, several important principles are mentioned which are common to all heavenly religions. Similar commands are also found in the Torah (Book of Exodus, Chapter 20). Two leaders from Medina came into the presence of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. As soon as the حضرت recited these Ayahs, they embraced Islam and requested a preacher. The Prophet then sent Mus'ab ibn Umayr along with them. This action paved the way for the people of Medina to become Muslims. The Quran has commanded kindness towards parents in four Ayahs [506], and in all four instances, this command is coupled with the issue of Monotheism (Tawhid) and the prohibition of polytheism (Shirk). Just as in creation, Allah comes first, followed by parents, in these Ayahs too, Monotheism comes first, followed by kindness to parents. Furthermore, since this Ayah enumerates prohibitions (muharramat), neglecting kindness to parents is also forbidden (haram). The five commands in this Ayah are so interconnected that they seem like a single command. The pronoun 'به' (bihi - to it) in «وصّاكم به» (He commanded you with it) is singular. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Kindness to parents means that we should not do anything that compels them to make a request or ask a question of us. [507]

506) Baqarah, 83; Nisa, 36; An'am, 151; and Isra, 23. 507) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 157; Bihar, Vol. 71, p. 39.

Messages

1- Declaring divine rulings (Ahkam) to the people is one of the duties of the prophets. «أتل ما حرّم»

2- Since the default state of all things is permissibility (halal), permissible things are not enumerated, and only the prohibitions (muharramat) are mentioned. «أتل ما حرّم»

3- The prohibitions of the religion are from Allah, and the Prophet does not forbid anything on his own accord. «حرّم ربّكم» (Your Lord has forbidden)

4- The prohibition of evils (munkarat) is for the sake of human perfection and training. «حرّم ربّكم»

5- Since polytheism (Shirk) is the root of all corruption, it is placed at the head of the prohibitions. «الاّ تشركوا» (That you associate nothing with Him).

6- After Monotheism, kindness to parents is mentioned. «بالوالدین احسانا»

7- All the commands in this Ayah are in the form of prohibition (nahy), except for kindness to parents, which is in the form of a command (amr). That is, not only should you not harm them, but you must also show kindness. «و بالوالدین احسانا»

8- Killing children and abortion out of fear of poverty is an ignorant act. If Allah guarantees sustenance, why fear poverty? «نحن نرزقكم» (We provide sustenance for you).

9- Both reforming society from corruption and reforming the soul from vices are necessary. «ما ظهر منها و ما بطن» (What is apparent of them and what is concealed).

10- Some sins are so dangerous that one should not even approach them. «لاتقربوا» (Do not approach).

11- Divine commands are either in accordance with reason or provide the ground for its flourishing. «لعلّكم تعقلون» (So that you may use reason).

Ayah

وَلَا تَقْرَبُوا۟ مَالَ ٱلْيَتِيمِ إِلَّا بِٱلَّتِى هِىَ أَحْسَنُ حَتَّىٰ يَبْلُغَ أَشُدَّهُۥ ۖ وَأَوْفُوا۟ ٱلْكَيْلَ وَٱلْمِيزَانَ بِٱلْقِسْطِ ۖ لَا نُكَلِّفُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا وُسْعَهَا ۖ وَإِذَا قُلْتُمْ فَٱعْدِلُوا۟ وَلَوْ كَانَ ذَا قُرْبَىٰ ۖ وَبِعَهْدِ ٱللَّهِ أَوْفُوا۟ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ وَصَّىٰكُم بِهِۦ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ (۱٥۲)
Do not approach the orphan’s property, except in the best [possible] manner, until he comes of age. And observe fully the measure and the balance with justice.’ We task no soul except according to its capacity. ‘And when you speak, be fair, even if it were a relative; and fulfill Allah’s covenant. This is what He enjoins upon you so that you may take admonition.’ (152)

Key Points

Allah performs His works in the best manner: «أحسن الخالقین» (508), «أحسن تقویم» (509), «نزل أحسن الحدیث» (510), and so on. He has also asked us that our actions be in the best manner, «لیبلوكم ایّكم احسن عملا» (511), whether in economic transactions and activities, «الاّ بالّتى هى احسن» (512), or in dialogue with opponents, «جادلهم بالّتى هى احسن» (513), or in accepting the words of others, «یستمعون القول فیتّبعون احسنه» (514), or in responding to people's evil deeds in the best way, «ادفع بالّتى هى احسن السیّئة» (515). In all these instances, the word "أحسن" (best) has been used. The people of Prophet Shu'ayb were afflicted by punishment due to shortchanging (in measure and weight). Shortchanging has also been prohibited three times in the Quran. The word «كیل» (kayl) means both the measure (container) and the act of measuring. The word «قسط» (qist) can relate both to «اوفوا» (fulfill), meaning the payment must be just, and to «كیل و میزان» (measure and balance), meaning the measure and the balance themselves must be sound. Of course, the result of both is the same.

508) مؤمنون، 14. 509) تین، 4. 510) زمر، 23. 511) هود، 7. 512) انعام، 152. 513) نحل، 125. 514) زمر، 18. 515) مؤمنون، 96.

Messages

1- Since the orphan has no defender and his wealth is a source of temptation, we must be careful. «لاتقربوا»

2- To protect the rights of orphans, the best method for utilizing their wealth and assets must be chosen. «بالّتى هى أحسن». Except for those who possess sufficient economic competence and piety, no one else should approach the orphan's wealth. «لاتقربوا... الا»

3- When orphans reach sufficient maturity and experience, remove your guardianship over them. «حتى یبلغ أشده»

4- The economic system of the Islamic society must be based on justice (qist). «بالقسط»

5- If the implementation of justice at the highest level is not possible, observe it at least within the limits of ability and possibility. «لا نكلّف نفساً الاّ وُسعها»

6- All divine instructions, commands, and prohibitions are not beyond human capacity. «لا نكلّف نفساً الاّ وُسعها»

7- Without ability, there is no obligation (taklif). «لا نكلّف نفساً الاّ وُسعها»

8- In Islam, "obligation" (taklif) is commensurate with "ability" (tavān). Whoever is more capable, his obligation is heavier. «لا نكلّف نفساً الاّ وُسعها»

9- Justice is a principle both in action and in speech. «اوفوا الكیل... و اذا قلتم فاعدلوا» (We must observe justice in testimonies, wills, judgments, issuing rulings, criticisms, and praises.)

10- Let us be faithful to the covenants of Allah (which include the commands of intellect, revelation, conscience, and innate nature). («عهد اللَّه» encompasses both the covenant that Allah has with man and the covenant that man makes with Allah). «بعهد اللّه اوفوا»

11- We must not prioritize kinship over the standard of truth and justice. «فاعدلوا ولو كان ذا قربى»

Ayah

وَأَنَّ هَـٰذَا صِرَٰطِى مُسْتَقِيمًا فَٱتَّبِعُوهُ ۖ وَلَا تَتَّبِعُوا۟ ٱلسُّبُلَ فَتَفَرَّقَ بِكُمْ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ وَصَّىٰكُم بِهِۦ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ (۱٥۳)
‘This indeed is my straight path, so follow it, and do not follow [other] ways, for they will separate you from His way. This is what He enjoins upon you so that you may be Godwary.’ (153)

Key Points

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله used a demonstrative method to explain this Ayah. He drew a straight line on the ground with His blessed hand and said: "This is the Straight Path, and there is only one." Then He drew lines to the right and left of that line and said: "These are the paths to which Satan invites." [516]

At the end of the last three Ayahs, three different expressions are mentioned: At the end of Ayah 151, which prohibits polytheism (shirk), murder, and indecency (fahsha), the phrase «لعلّكم تعقلون» (so that you may use reason) is mentioned. This means the ugliness of these matters becomes clear to everyone with a little reasoning and thought. In Ayah 152, which commands the preservation of the orphan's property, the observance of equity and justice, and the fulfillment of covenants, the expression «لعلّكم تذكّرون» (so that you may take heed) is mentioned. This means the goodness of justice is accepted by the innate nature (fitrah) and disposition of every person; they only need to be reminded (tadhakkur). In this Ayah (153) as well, which concerns following Allah's commands, «لعلّكم تتّقون» (so that you may become righteous/pious) is mentioned. This means piety (taqwa) is the very act of stepping onto the path of obedience and divine command.

Just as "light" (نور) is singular and "darknesses" (ظلمات) are multiple, the Straight Path is one, while the paths of deviation are multiple. (The word «صراط» is singular, but «سُبل» is plural.)

We read in narrations that the concrete manifestation (misdaq) of the Straight Path is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله and the infallible Imams علیهم السلام from His Household. [517]

516) Tafsir Maraghi. 517) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The foundation of all divine religions is following the path of Allah and avoiding the path of others. «صراطى... فاتّبعوه و لا تتّبعوا السبل»

2- Acting upon divine decrees is a cause for unity, while resorting to non-divine decrees leads to division. «صراطى مستقیماً فاتّبعوه و لا تتّبعوا السبل فتفرّق بكم عن سبیله»

3- Perhaps, by paying attention to the conclusion of the last three Ayahs, one can conclude that the stages of human growth and perfection consist of: reasoning («لعلّكم تعقلون»), taking heed («لعلّكم تذكّرون»), and piety («لعلّكم تتّقون»).

Ayah

ثُمَّ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَى ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ تَمَامًا عَلَى ٱلَّذِىٓ أَحْسَنَ وَتَفْصِيلًا لِّكُلِّ شَىْءٍ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةً لَّعَلَّهُم بِلِقَآءِ رَبِّهِمْ يُؤْمِنُونَ (۱٥٤)
Then We gave Moses the Book, completing [Our blessing] on him who is virtuous, and as an elaboration of all things, and as a guidance and mercy, so that they may believe in the encounter with their Lord. (154)

Key Points

There are many similarities between the Quran and the Torah. In the Gospel (Injīl), the emphasis is on admonitions, and in the Psalms (Zabūr), the emphasis is on supplication, but in terms of laws, the similarity between the Torah and the Quran is greater. For this reason, the Torah has been called an Imām (leader/guide). "و من قبله كتاب موسى اماماً" [518] In this Ayah too, the Torah is introduced as a mercy, a guidance, and an explainer of everything.

518) Hūd, 17.

Messages

1- Every Divine Book is complete relative to its own time. "تماماً"

2- Only the righteous and those with good intentions receive the messages of the Divine Books and the Divine laws better. "على الّذى أحسن"

3- All human needs for achieving perfection are mentioned in the Divine Books. "تفصیلاً لكلّ شى ء"

4- The Divine Book is in the path of human guidance and Divine mercy. "هدى و رحمة"

5- One of the objectives of the Divine Books and the Divine Prophets is humanity's belief in the Resurrection (Qiyāmah). "لعلّهم بلقاء ربّهم یؤمنون"

Ayahs

وَهَـٰذَا كِتَـٰبٌ أَنزَلْنَـٰهُ مُبَارَكٌ فَٱتَّبِعُوهُ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ (۱٥٥)
And this Book that We have sent down is a blessed one; so follow it, and be Godwary so that you may receive [His] mercy. (155)
أَن تَقُولُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَآ أُنزِلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبُ عَلَىٰ طَآئِفَتَيْنِ مِن قَبْلِنَا وَإِن كُنَّا عَن دِرَاسَتِهِمْ لَغَـٰفِلِينَ (۱٥٦)
Lest you should say, ‘The Book was sent down only to two communities before us, and we were indeed unaware of their studies,’ (156)

Key Points

The word mubārak (blessed), derived from the root barakah, emphasizes two matters: 1. A strong and stable root. 2. Perpetual growth. The Quran possesses fundamental, unchangeable, and firm contents, and simultaneously, as time passes, veils of its secrets are uncovered, and its splendor increases day by day.

An taqūlū (that you might say) means li-allā taqūlū (so that you do not say), meaning: so that you do not say and make excuses.

Dirāsah means recitation (tilāwah) and knowledge (‘ilm).

Messages

  1. The Quran is not merely a theoretical book; rather, it is the book of human felicity and a practical program for action. (kitāb... fa-ittabi‘ūh)

  2. Human felicity lies in two things: obedience to the Truth (Allah) and avoidance of falsehood. (fa-ittabi‘ūh wa-ittaqū)

  3. Allah has completed the proof (hujjah) against the people. (kitāb anzalnāhu... an taqūlū)

  4. The Muslim propagation apparatus must prepare sound programs at a global level for every country, nation, and language, so that the proof is completed against them, and they cannot say, "We were unaware of the Truth." (an taqūlū... kunnā ‘an dirāsatihim la-ghāfilīn)

Ayah

أَوْ تَقُولُوا۟ لَوْ أَنَّآ أُنزِلَ عَلَيْنَا ٱلْكِتَـٰبُ لَكُنَّآ أَهْدَىٰ مِنْهُمْ ۚ فَقَدْ جَآءَكُم بَيِّنَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةٌ ۚ فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّن كَذَّبَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ وَصَدَفَ عَنْهَا ۗ سَنَجْزِى ٱلَّذِينَ يَصْدِفُونَ عَنْ ءَايَـٰتِنَا سُوٓءَ ٱلْعَذَابِ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَصْدِفُونَ (۱٥۷)
or [lest] you should say, ‘If the Book had been sent down to us, surely we would have been better-guided than them.’ There has already come to you a manifest proof from your Lord and a guidance and mercy. So who is a greater wrongdoer than him who denies the signs of Allah, and turns away from them? Soon We shall requite those who turn away from Our signs with a terrible punishment because of what they used to evade. (157)

Key Points

«Yāṣdifūn» (یَصدفون) is derived from «ṣadaf» (صدف), meaning severe aversion from something without contemplation or thought.

Messages

1- The polytheists of Mecca confessed to the guidance received by the followers of the Torah and the Gospel, but they considered themselves more worthy than them to receive the Divine Book. «لكنّا اهدى منهم»

2- The revelation of the Quran is the completion of the proof (Hujjah) for everyone. «فقد جاءكم بیّنة من ربّكم»

3- The Quran is a manifestation of Allah’s Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «بیّنة من ربّكم»

4- The Quran is a book of guidance and mercy. «هدى و رحمة»

5- Before the test, there are many claimants, but the trial reveals the sincerities. «لكنّا أهدى... فمن اظلم ممن كذّب...»

6- One of the greatest injustices to humanity is turning away from the Divine Books. «فمن أظلم ممّن كذّب بآیات اللّه»

7- The punishment for turning away from the true religion and rejecting the Divine verses is severe torment. «الّذین یصدفون عن آیاتنا سوء العذاب بما كانوا یصدفون»

8- The main factor behind human misery is his own actions. «بما كانوا یصدفون»

Ayah

هَلْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّآ أَن تَأْتِيَهُمُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ أَوْ يَأْتِىَ رَبُّكَ أَوْ يَأْتِىَ بَعْضُ ءَايَـٰتِ رَبِّكَ ۗ يَوْمَ يَأْتِى بَعْضُ ءَايَـٰتِ رَبِّكَ لَا يَنفَعُ نَفْسًا إِيمَـٰنُهَا لَمْ تَكُنْ ءَامَنَتْ مِن قَبْلُ أَوْ كَسَبَتْ فِىٓ إِيمَـٰنِهَا خَيْرًا ۗ قُلِ ٱنتَظِرُوٓا۟ إِنَّا مُنتَظِرُونَ (۱٥۸)
Do they not consider that the angels may come to them, or your Lord may come, or some of your Lord’s signs may come? The day when some of your Lord’s signs do come, faith shall not benefit any soul that had not believed beforehand and had not earned some goodness in its faith. Say, ‘Wait! We too are waiting!’ (158)

Key Points

In Ayah 92 of Surah Al-Isra, the undue expectations of the disbelievers were mentioned, where they said: We will not believe in you unless you cause the sky to fall upon us, or you bring Allah and the angels to us. These expectations are rejected in this Ayah.

Messages

  1. Stubborn disbelievers will not submit even after seeing divine miracles. «هل ینظرون ...»
  2. The result of evading faith is failure. «لاینفع نفساً ایمانها»
  3. Faith and deeds are effective under free and natural conditions, not in a state of necessity or fear for one's life. «یوم یأتى بعض آیات ربّك لاینفع»
  4. Righteous deeds without faith are not beneficial. «او كسبت فى ایمانها خیراً»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ فَرَّقُوا۟ دِينَهُمْ وَكَانُوا۟ شِيَعًا لَّسْتَ مِنْهُمْ فِى شَىْءٍ ۚ إِنَّمَآ أَمْرُهُمْ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ ثُمَّ يُنَبِّئُهُم بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْعَلُونَ (۱٥۹)
Indeed those who split up their religion and became sects, you will not have anything to do with them. Their matter rests only with Allah; then He will inform them concerning what they used to do. (159)

Key Points

| Causing division (schism) in religion means introducing innovations (bid'ah) and interpreting religion according to one's own opinion (ra'y), concerning which the severest expressions have been mentioned in the Quran and Hadith. Pay attention to some of these examples: * The Quran says: Woe to those who write the scripture with their own hands, and then say: "This is from Allah." [519] * Ali عليه السلام states: Whenever an innovation (bid'ah) appears, a tradition (sunnah) disappears. [520] And one of the duties of the Prophets and scholars is to eradicate innovations and prevent distortions. [521] * It is mentioned in narrations (Riwāyāt): The repentance of the one who introduces innovation is not accepted. [522] * Allah accepts no deed from the innovator. [523] * Whoever respects an innovator or smiles at him has taken a step toward the collapse of religion. * The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: Whenever disagreement occurs among my Ummah, whoever acts according to my Sunnah and my saying, and does not follow the path and method of others, will have the reward of one hundred martyrs. [524] * In the Quran, the Jews are criticized repeatedly because of the distortions their scholars introduced into the religion. Regarding Prophet Moses عليه السلام, we read: When he returned from Mount Tur and saw the deviation and calf-worship of his people, out of extreme distress, he cast down the Tablets of the Torah and seized the beard of his brother, who was his successor, and said: "Why did my Ummah deviate?" Aaron replied: "I feared that if I acted severely to prevent their deviation, the people would become scattered, and you would say, 'Why did the people become divided into sects?' (A division that would not be rectifiable even upon your return)." These verses and narrations illustrate the heavy responsibility of religious scholars in preserving the authenticity of the school of thought and confronting intellectual deviations, while simultaneously maintaining the social unity of the Ummah.

519) Baqarah, 79. 520) Bihar, vol. 2, p. 264. 521) Al-Hayat, vol. 2, p. 344. 522) Bihar, vol. 72, p. 216. 523) Kanz al-'Ummal, Hadith 1115. 524) Bihar, vol. 2, p. 262.

Messages

1- There is no difference among the religious teachings; one must believe in and act upon all of them, not just some. «انّ الّذین فرّقوا دینهم»

2- Among the factors causing division and separation are change and distortion within the school of thought. «انّ الّذین فرّقوا دینهم و كانوا شیعاً»

3- One should not cooperate with those who do not accept the entirety of the school of Islam; they will not be counted among the Ummah of Muhammad. «لستَ منهم فى شى ء» They are under the threat of Allah. «انّما امرهم الى اللَّه»

4- Causing division in religion is a type of polytheism (Shirk). «فرّقوا دینهم... لستَ منهم فى شى ء» It is mentioned in verses 32 and 33 of Surah Ar-Rum: «ولا تكونوا من المشركین . من الّذین فرّقوا دینهم» Do not be among the polytheists—those who caused division in their religion.

Ayah

مَن جَآءَ بِٱلْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُۥ عَشْرُ أَمْثَالِهَا ۖ وَمَن جَآءَ بِٱلسَّيِّئَةِ فَلَا يُجْزَىٰٓ إِلَّا مِثْلَهَا وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ (۱٦٠)
Whoever brings virtue shall receive [a reward] ten times its like; but whoever brings vice shall not be requited except with its like, and they will not be wronged. (160)

Key Points

The use of the word jā’a (جاء - came) indicates that the reward and punishment discussed in this Ayah relate to the court of the Day of Judgment. Otherwise, many sins might be erased through repentance or converted into good deeds. «یبدّل اللَّه سیّئاتهم حَسنات» [525], or they might be forgiven. «یَعفُو عن كثیر» [526]. And many good deeds might be erased and nullified by hypocrisy (riyā’), vanity (‘ujb), and other sins. Therefore, the criterion is the deed that is brought to the scene of the Day of Judgment. «جاء بالحسنة... جاءبالسیّئة»

Although the Ayah relates to good and bad deeds, according to tradition, whoever intends a good deed receives a reward, but an evil intention is not punished unless it reaches the stage of action, and this is Divine favor.

It is mentioned in narrations: Whoever fasts three days in a month is considered to have fasted the entire month, because each day is counted as ten days. «عشر أمثالها»

The tenfold reward is the minimum. Some deeds, under certain conditions and for certain individuals, have a reward up to seven hundred times, or even an immeasurable reward.

Out of ten Divine rewards, only one part is the "wage" (ujūr), and the other nine parts are "favor" (faḍl). «فیوَفِّیهم اُجورهم و یزیدهم من فضله» [527]

Question: If the punishment for the sinner is equal to his sin, then why does breaking one day of fast require sixty days of compensatory fasting (kaffārah)?

Answer: The meaning of mithl (مثل - like/equal) in the Ayah is not in terms of number, but rather in terms of quality and nature. The importance of one day of fasting in the month of Ramadan is equivalent to 60 days outside of Ramadan. Like the Night of Qadr (Laylat al-Qadr), which in importance is greater than a thousand months. This expresses importance, not numerical superiority.

525) Al-Furqan, 70. 526) Al-Ma'idah, 15. 527) An-Nisa, 173.

Messages

1- In the educational method of Islam, encouragement is tenfold the punishment. «عشر امثالها»

2- Multiplied encouragement is not oppression, but excessive punishment is oppression. «فلایجزى الا مثلها و هم لایظلمون»

3- Allah deals with rewards through His favor, but with punishment through justice. «عشر أمثالها... الاّ مثلها»

4- A person's deed is always and everywhere accompanying that person. «من جاء بالحسنة... و من جاء بالسیّئة»

Ayah

قُلْ إِنَّنِى هَدَىٰنِى رَبِّىٓ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ دِينًا قِيَمًا مِّلَّةَ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ حَنِيفًا ۚ وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (۱٦۱)
Say, ‘Indeed my Lord has guided me to a straight path, the upright religion, the creed of Abraham, a Hanif, and he was not one of the polytheists.’ (161)

Key Points

The words qiyam and qayyim mean uprightness, firmness, and permanence. Yes, a religion remains firm and eternal only if it pays attention to the material and spiritual issues of the people in this world and the Hereafter. Imam Husayn عليه السلام said: No one except us and our Shi'a (followers) adheres to the religion of Abraham عليه السلام. [528]

528) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The words and teachings of the Prophet are not His personal opinion, but the text of Divine revelation, which He must announce to the people. «قل» (Say)

2- The straight path is the path of the Prophet. «هدانى ربّى الى صراط مستقیم» (My Lord has guided me to a straight path)

3- Guidance is solely the work of Allah; even the Prophets find the straight path through Divine guidance. «هدانى ربّى» (My Lord has guided me)

4- Guidance is among the attributes of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «هدانى ربّى» (My Lord has guided me)

5- The path of Abraham عليه السلام is monotheism (Tawhid), not polytheistic ideas. «حنیفاً و ما كان من المشركین» (a Hanif, and he was not of the polytheists). (The polytheists attributed their deviant beliefs to Abraham عليه السلام.)

6- Idol-breaking and turning away from polytheism is not solely the work of the Prophet of Islam; rather, He did so in adherence to Prophet Abraham. «ملّة ابراهیم حنیفاً» (the religion of Abraham, a Hanif)

7- The foundation of monotheistic religions throughout history has been one. Islam is the same religion as Prophet Abraham, and aversion to polytheism is the path of all Prophets. «قل انّنى... ملّة ابیكم ابراهیم و ما كان من المشركین» (Say: I... the religion of your father Abraham, and he was not of the polytheists)

Ayahs

قُلْ إِنَّ صَلَاتِى وَنُسُكِى وَمَحْيَاىَ وَمَمَاتِى لِلَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱٦۲)
Say, ‘Indeed my prayer and my worship, my life and my death are for the sake of Allah, the Lord of all the worlds. (162)
لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُۥ ۖ وَبِذَٰلِكَ أُمِرْتُ وَأَنَا۠ أَوَّلُ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ (۱٦۳)
He has no partner, and I have been commanded [to follow] this [creed], and I am the first of those who submit [to Allah].’ (163)

Key Points

It has been narrated that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله used to recite these verses on the verge of prayer. [529] Death encompasses life, and life encompasses nusuk (acts of devotion/sacrifice), and nusuk encompasses prayer. Therefore, prayer is the central core within acts of worship. Islam means submission to the command of Allah and has been attributed to all the Prophets. Prophet Noah علیه السلام considered himself a Muslim: «اُمرت اَن اكون من المسلمین» [530] Prophet Abraham علیه السلام asked Allah to make him and his progeny submit to Him: «و اجعلنا مسلمین لك و من ذریّتنا اُمّة مسلمة لك» [531] Prophet Joseph علیه السلام also requested from Allah to die as a Muslim: «توفّنى مسلماً» [532] And the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله is also the first Muslim: «و انا اوّل المسلمین», meaning that either in his own time, or in the rank and station of submission, he is foremost among all.

529) Tafsir Qurtubi. 530) Yunus, 72. 531) Baqarah, 128. 532) Yusuf, 101.

Messages

1- We should declare our path, method, and goal explicitly and proudly in the face of deviant paths. «قل»

2- Although prayer is part of acts of worship, it is mentioned separately to show its importance. «صلاتى و نُسُكى»

3- Sincere individuals consider their existential path (death and life) and their legislative path (prayer and nusuk) to be solely for Allah, the Lord of the worlds. «انّ صلاتى و نُسُكى و محیاى و مماتى للّه»

4- Just as we intend proximity (to Allah) in prayer, we can also intend proximity in every breath, in being alive, and in dying. «محیاى و مماتى للّه...»

5- Death and life are not what is important; what is important is that they be for Allah and in the way of Allah. «محیاى و مماتى للّه»

6- Whatever is for Allah grows. «للّه ربّ العالمین»

7- Death and life are not in our hands, but giving direction to them is in our hands. «للّه»

8- Sincerity (Ikhlas) in deeds is a Divine command. «بذلك امرت»

9- The leader of the community must be a pioneer in traversing the path and implementing the Divine command. «اوّل المسلین»

Ayah

قُلْ أَغَيْرَ ٱللَّهِ أَبْغِى رَبًّا وَهُوَ رَبُّ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ ۚ وَلَا تَكْسِبُ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ إِلَّا عَلَيْهَا ۚ وَلَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَىٰ ۚ ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُم مَّرْجِعُكُمْ فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ فِيهِ تَخْتَلِفُونَ (۱٦٤)
Say, ‘Shall I seek a Lord other than Allah, while He is the Lord of all things?’ No soul does evil except against itself, and no bearer shall bear another’s burden; then to your Lord will be your return, whereat He will inform you concerning that about which you used to differ. (164)

Key Points

The subject of Divine Justice in punishment, and the fact that no one bears the sin of another, is mentioned not only in Islam but, according to the explicit statement of the Quran, also in the Scrolls (Suhuf) of Abraham and Moses (peace be upon them). «ام لم یُنبّأ بما فى صُحُف موسى . وابراهیم الّذى وَفّى . ألاّ تَزِرُ وازِرة وِزرَ اُخرى» [533]

Question: If no one bears the sin of others, then what is the meaning of what is stated in the Quran, that misguided and deviant leaders also take responsibility for the sins of their followers? «لیحملوا أوزارهم كاملة یوم القیامة و من أوزار الّذین یضلّونهم بغیر علم» [534]

Answer: This matter is not without reason, because the leaders of misguidance have caused the deviation of others, and in reality, they bear the sin of "misguiding" (or idhlal).

A pregnant woman who had committed adultery was brought before Umar for punishment, and he ordered her to be stoned. Imam Ali عليه السلام said: What is the sin of the child in the mother's womb? He then recited this Ayah: «لاتزر وازرة وزر اُخرى» [535]

533) Najm, 37-38. 534) Nahl, 25. 535) Bihar, Vol. 76, p. 49.

Messages

1- When dealing with deniers and polytheists, we must decisively declare our rightful position. «قل أغیر اللَّه»

2- A vigilant conscience is the best responder to internal questions. «أغیر اللَّه أبغى»

3- Since Allah is the sole Lord of all existence, He is also my Lord. «و هو ربّ كلّ شى ء»

4- The disbelief, polytheism, goodness, or corruption of people does not harm God; rather, it affects them alone. «لا تكسب كلّ نفس الاّ علیها»

5- Before Allah, the responsibility for everyone's action rests upon himself. «لا تزر وازرة وزر اُخرى»

6- The establishment of the Resurrection and the accountability of man is a manifestation of Allah's Lordship (Rububiyyah). «ثمّ الى ربّكم مرجعكم»

7- The world also has an end, «ثمّ الى ربّكم مرجعكم», and eventually we will become aware of the truth of that which we stubbornly opposed. «فینبّئكم بما كنتم فیه تختلفون»

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَكُمْ خَلَـٰٓئِفَ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَرَفَعَ بَعْضَكُمْ فَوْقَ بَعْضٍ دَرَجَـٰتٍ لِّيَبْلُوَكُمْ فِى مَآ ءَاتَىٰكُمْ ۗ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ سَرِيعُ ٱلْعِقَابِ وَإِنَّهُۥ لَغَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌۢ (۱٦٥)
It is He who has made you successors on the earth, and raised some of you in rank above others so that He may test you in respect to what He has given you. Indeed your Lord is swift in retribution, and indeed, He is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (165)

Key Points

Surah Al-An'am began with the praise of Allah, «الحمد للّه الّذى خلق...» (All praise belongs to Allah Who created...), and concludes with His mercy. «انّه لغفور رحیم» (Indeed, He is Forgiving, Merciful). The meaning of «خلائف الارض» (successors on the earth) is either the vicegerents of God on earth or the succession of present-day humanity from previous nations.

Messages

1- Man is the vicegerent of Allah and the ruler of the earth, not a captive of existence. «و هو الّذى جعلكم خلائف الارض»

2- The differences among people and the divine tests are wise, aimed at the growth and training of humanity, and originate from the Lordship (Rububiyyah) of Allah. «و هو ربّ كلّ شى ء... رفع بعضكم فوق بعض...»

3- The difference and superiority of some in the endowments granted by Allah is not a criterion for inherent superiority; it is a means of testing. «لیبلوكم فى ما آتاكم»

4- We must know that what we possess is from Allah. «فى ما آتاكم»

5- The criterion and extent of everyone's test is commensurate with the endowments and resources given to him. «لیبلوكم فى ما آتاكم»

6- After the test, Allah is «سریع العقاب» (swift in retribution) towards those who fail, and «غفور رحیم» (Forgiving, Merciful) towards those who pass.

7- Fear and hope must exist side by side. «سریع العقاب... لغفور رحیم»

«الحمد للّه ربّ العالمین»
